  interest_score varchar [note: 'Interest score component of the LAI (Linkage-Ability-Interest) assessment. Measures alignment of prospect\'s philanthropic interests with organizational mission and programs. Typically scored 1-5.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent meaningful contact or engagement with the prospect. Includes meetings, calls, events, correspondence.']
  last_contact_type varchar [note: 'Type of the most recent contact or engagement activity with the prospect. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: meeting|phone_call|email|event|site_visit|proposal_submission|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  linkage_score varchar [note: 'Linkage score component of the LAI (Linkage-Ability-Interest) assessment. Measures strength of existing connections to the organization (board members, staff, volunteers, beneficiaries). Typically scored 1-5.']
  mailing_address varchar [note: 'Full mailing address for prospect correspondence. Includes street address, city, state/province, postal code, and country.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this prospect record was last modified. Audit trail field for data governance and change tracking.']
  next_action_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next cultivation activity or touchpoint with this prospect. Used for pipeline management and follow-up tracking.']
  next_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of the planned next cultivation action or engagement activity (e.g., \'Schedule site visit\', \'Send program impact report\', \'Invite to annual gala\', \'Submit grant proposal\').']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for prospect contact. Includes country code for international prospects.']
  previous_giving_history varchar [note: 'Summary of the prospect\'s known philanthropic giving history to other organizations or prior gifts to this organization. Includes gift amounts, recipient organizations, and giving patterns.']
  probability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated probability (0-100%) that this prospect will convert to an active donor and make a gift. Used for pipeline forecasting and weighted revenue projections.']
  program_interest_area varchar [note: 'Primary program area or thematic focus that aligns with the prospect\'s philanthropic interests (e.g., \'WASH\', \'Education\', \'Health\', \'Emergency Response\', \'Gender Equality\'). May reference SDG (Sustainable Development Goal) alignment.']
  prospect_name varchar [note: 'Full legal or preferred name of the prospective donor or funder. For institutional donors, this is the organization name.']
  prospect_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the prospect record. Active (currently being cultivated), Inactive (no current engagement), Disqualified (determined not viable), Converted (became active donor), On Hold (temporarily paused cultivation).']
  prospect_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the prospect by donor category. Individual (major gift prospects), Foundation (private/family/community foundations), Corporate (corporate sponsors), Institutional (USAID, DFID, DAC members), Government (bilateral government donors), Multilateral (UN agencies, World Bank, regional development banks).']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the prospect was qualified as a viable cultivation target after initial research and assessment. Null if not yet qualified.']
  rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating assigned by prospect research or major gift officer indicating overall prospect quality and likelihood of conversion. A+ (highest priority, strongest capacity and affinity), D (lowest priority).']
  research_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of prospect research activities. Not Started (no research conducted), Preliminary (initial screening and public records review), In Depth (comprehensive research including wealth screening and relationship mapping), Completed (research finalized and briefing prepared), Ongoing Monitoring (periodic updates and news monitoring).']
  solicitation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Specific dollar amount being requested or proposed in the current solicitation. Null if not yet at solicitation stage.']
  solicitation_date date [note: 'Date when the formal ask or grant proposal was submitted to the prospect. Null if not yet solicited.']
  source_of_wealth varchar [note: 'Primary source of the prospect\'s wealth or funding capacity (e.g., \'Technology entrepreneur\', \'Inherited wealth\', \'Real estate\', \'Government appropriation\', \'Endowment income\'). For institutional donors, describes funding source.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the donor cultivation pipeline lifecycle. Identification (newly identified prospect), Qualification (research and capacity assessment underway), Cultivation (relationship building and engagement), Solicitation (active proposal or ask), Negotiation (terms and agreement discussion), Stewardship (post-gift relationship management).']
  wealth_screening_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative wealth screening score from third-party wealth screening service (e.g., WealthEngine, iWave). Typically scaled 0-100 indicating relative wealth capacity.']

  Note: 'Prospect research and donor cultivation pipeline record managed in Raisers Edge NXT and Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud. Tracks prospective donors and funders through the cultivation lifecycle from identification through qualification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship. Captures prospect rating, estimated giving capacity, wealth screening score, research stage, assigned major gift officer, next action date, cultivation strategy notes, and linkage-ability-interest (LAI) assessment. Supports the Donor Cultivation and Fundraising core business process.'
}

Table donor.gift {
  gift_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the gift transaction. Primary key for the gift data product.']
  appeal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific appeal or solicitation within a campaign that generated this gift.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fundraising campaign that solicited or attributed this gift.']
  compliance_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.incident. Business justification: Compliance incidents may involve specific gifts (fraudulent donations, money laundering concerns, restriction violations). Incident reports reference specific gift transactions for investigation, pote']
  evaluation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.evaluation. Business justification: Major gifts may trigger or fund specific evaluations as a donor requirement. Tracking which gifts funded which evaluations is essential for donor reporting, acknowledgment, and demonstrating how donor']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Gifts are deposited into specific finance funds for GL posting, restriction tracking, and fund balance management. Core nonprofit financial operation linking fundraising revenue to accounting records.']
  fundraising_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.fundraising_event. Business justification: Gifts can be made at or through fundraising events (ticket purchase, auction bid, event donation). Event attribution enables event ROI calculation, revenue tracking, and event effectiveness analysis. ']
  gift_constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor who made this gift. Links to the donor master record in the donor domain.']
  gift_matching_employer_constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate donor or employer providing the matching gift. Populated only when matching_gift_flag is true.']
  indicator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.indicator. Business justification: Restricted gifts often fund specific programmatic outcomes tracked by indicators. Linking gift to indicator enables outcomes-based donor reporting, showing donors the specific results their contributi']
  indicator_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.indicator_result. Business justification: Gifts restricted to specific outcomes need linkage to actual results achieved. This is the core of outcomes-based donor reporting, enabling nonprofits to show donors the specific results their contrib']
  major_gift_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.major_gift_opportunity. Business justification: Gifts can close major gift opportunities. Linking closed opportunities to realized gifts enables pipeline conversion tracking, forecasting accuracy analysis, and gift officer performance measurement. ']
  original_gift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original employee gift that this matching gift is matching. Populated only for matching gifts.']
  pledge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.pledge. Business justification: Gifts can be payments against pledges. This FK is CRITICAL for pledge fulfillment tracking, balance calculation, and installment schedule management. Nullable (not all gifts are pledge payments; some ']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Major gifts trigger specific regulatory filings (IRS Form 990 Schedule B for large donors, state charitable solicitation reports). Compliance teams track which gifts necessitate disclosure in which fi']
  safeguarding_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safeguarding.safeguarding_incident. Business justification: Gifts may be restricted, returned, or refused due to safeguarding incidents involving the donor. Tracks financial impact of safeguarding cases, supports gift acceptance policy enforcement, and documen']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Individual gifts (especially large or international) trigger sanctions screening to ensure compliance with anti-terrorism financing regulations. Gift processing includes sanctions screening step befor']
  single_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.single_audit. Business justification: Federal grants received as gifts (pass-through awards) are included in single audit federal expenditure calculations. Grants accounting identifies which gifts are federal pass-throughs for audit scope']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date the donor acknowledgement or thank-you letter was sent for this gift.']
  acknowledgement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the donor acknowledgement or thank-you communication sent for this gift. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|personalized|major-gift|planned-giving|corporate|foundation|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross monetary value of the gift in the transaction currency before any adjustments or fees.']
  anonymous_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the donor requested anonymity for this gift in public recognition and donor lists.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Gift processing batch identifier for grouping gifts deposited or processed together for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the gift amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction processing fees or costs deducted from the gross gift amount (e.g., credit card fees, wire transfer fees).']
  gift_date date [note: 'The date the gift was received or pledged. This is the principal business event timestamp for revenue recognition per FASB ASC 958.']
  gift_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this gift transaction, used for donor communication and receipt references.']
  gift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the gift transaction in the processing workflow.']
  gift_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating the nature and form of the gift. Determines processing rules, revenue recognition treatment, and IRS substantiation requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash|check|wire|in-kind|stock|cryptocurrency|matching|tribute|pledge|recurring|refund|adjustment — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code where this gift revenue is posted in the financial system per the Chart of Accounts (CoA).']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date this gift transaction was posted to the general ledger for financial reporting.']
  goods_services_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fair market value of any goods or services provided to the donor in exchange for the gift, required for IRS substantiation and deductibility calculation.']
  honoree_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person being honored or memorialized by this tribute gift. Populated only when tribute_flag is true.']
  irs_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gift acknowledgement includes IRS-required substantiation language for charitable contribution deductions per IRS Publication 1771.']
  match_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the matching gift request with the employer. Populated only for matching gifts.']
  match_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'The employer matching ratio (e.g., 1.00 for 1:1, 2.00 for 2:1, 0.50 for 1:2). Populated only for matching gifts.']
  match_request_date date [note: 'Date the matching gift request was submitted to the employer. Populated only for matching gifts.']
  matching_gift_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gift is a corporate matching gift from an employer matching an employees donation.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount received after deducting processing fees, transaction costs, or goods-and-services fair market value per IRS requirements.']
  notification_recipient_address varchar [note: 'Mailing address for sending tribute gift notification to the honorees family or designated recipient.']
  notification_recipient_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who should be notified about this tribute gift (typically a family member of the honoree).']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tribute gift notification has been sent to the designated recipient.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'The interface or touchpoint through which the donor made the gift, used for campaign attribution and channel effectiveness analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: online|mobile-app|direct-mail|phone|in-person|event|major-gift-officer|peer-to-peer|text-to-give|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The financial instrument or mechanism used to make the gift payment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit-card|debit-card|ach|wire-transfer|check|cash|paypal|stock-transfer|cryptocurrency|mobile-payment|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Unique receipt number issued for this gift for IRS substantiation and donor tax deduction purposes.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gift record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gift record was last modified in the system.']
  refund_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents a gift refund or reversal transaction.']
  refund_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or business reason for refunding or reversing this gift. Populated only when refund_flag is true.']
  restriction_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of any donor-imposed restrictions, purpose designations, or conditions on the use of the gift funds.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of donor-imposed restrictions on the use of the gift per FASB ASC 958 net asset classification requirements.']
  tribute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gift is made in honor or memory of another person (tribute gift).']
  tribute_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tribute gift as either in honor of a living person or in memory of a deceased person. Populated only when tribute_flag is true.']

  Note: 'Authoritative transactional ledger and single source of truth for every financial transaction in the donor domain — gifts, donations, matching gifts, tribute gifts, refunds, adjustments, and acknowledgements — processed through Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud and Raisers Edge NXT. This is the ONE table that answers \'what is this donor\'s total giving?\' Captures gift date, amount, currency, gift type discriminator (cash, check, wire, in-kind, stock, crypto, matching, tribute, refund/adjustment, recurring), fund designation, campaign and appeal attribution, gift source (online, direct mail, event, major gift officer), payment method, batch reference, anonymous flag, restricted vs. unrestricted designation, and GL posting reference. For matching gifts: match ratio, matching employer/corporation, match request date, match approval status, match payment received date, maximum match amount. For tribute gifts: tribute type (in-memoriam, in-honor-of), honoree name, honoree relationship, notification recipient, notification sent flag. For refunds/adjustments: original gift reference, refund date, refund amount, refund reason, refund method, GL reversal reference. For acknowledgements: acknowledgement type, acknowledgement date, receipt number, delivery method, acknowledgement status, IRS-compliant language flag, goods-or-services disclosure. Core financial transaction for donor revenue recognition per FASB ASC 958 and IRS 501(c)(3) substantiation requirements.'
}

Table donor.pledge {
  pledge_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pledge commitment record. Primary key.']
  appeal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific appeal or solicitation that generated this pledge.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fundraising campaign associated with this pledge.']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor who made this pledge commitment.']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Pledges commit future revenue to specific finance funds. Finance uses this for cash flow forecasting, fund balance projections, and accounts receivable management. Essential for nonprofit liquidity pl']
  indicator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.indicator. Business justification: Multi-year pledges frequently fund specific indicator targets over time. Tracking pledge fulfillment against indicator achievement enables longitudinal impact reporting to donors and supports major gi']
  major_gift_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.major_gift_opportunity. Business justification: Pledges can result from major gift opportunities (e.g., major gift solicitation results in multi-year pledge commitment). Linking pipeline to commitment enables conversion tracking and forecasting. Nu']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or volunteer who solicited this pledge commitment.']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'The date on which the pledge acknowledgment was sent to the donor.']
  acknowledgment_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pledge acknowledgment letter or receipt has been sent to the donor.']
  amount_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative amount paid to date across all installments. Updated as installment payments are received.']
  balance_outstanding decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining unpaid balance on the pledge. Calculated as total_pledge_amount minus amount_paid.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'The date on which the donor or organization cancelled the pledge commitment. Populated only when pledge_status is cancelled.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the pledge was cancelled (e.g., donor request, duplicate entry, data entry error).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this pledge record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the pledge amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  first_installment_date date [note: 'The scheduled due date for the first installment payment.']
  fulfillment_date date [note: 'The date on which the pledge was fully paid and marked as fulfilled. Populated only when pledge_status is fulfilled.']
  installment_frequency varchar [note: 'The recurring schedule for installment payments. One-time for single-payment pledges; monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual for standard recurring schedules; custom for irregular or donor-specified schedules.']
  installments_paid varchar [note: 'The count of installments that have been fully paid to date. Updated as installment payments are received.']
  installments_remaining varchar [note: 'The count of installments that are still unpaid. Calculated as number_of_installments minus installments_paid.']
  is_anonymous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the donor has requested that this pledge be kept anonymous in public recognition and reporting.']
  is_matching_gift_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pledge is eligible for corporate matching gift programs.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pledge is a recurring sustainer commitment with ongoing installments beyond the initial schedule.']
  last_installment_date date [note: 'The scheduled due date for the final installment payment.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of the most recent installment payment received.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent installment payment was received.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this pledge record was last modified in the system.']
  next_installment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The scheduled amount for the next unpaid installment.']
  next_installment_due_date date [note: 'The due date for the next unpaid installment. Used for reminder generation and overdue tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the pledge, including special donor instructions, stewardship considerations, or internal processing notes.']
  number_of_installments varchar [note: 'The total number of scheduled installment payments for this pledge. For one-time pledges, this is 1.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The primary payment instrument or method the donor intends to use for installment payments.']
  pledge_date date [note: 'The date on which the donor made the pledge commitment. This is the principal business event timestamp for the pledge.']
  pledge_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the pledge commitment, often displayed on donor communications and receipts.']
  pledge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pledge commitment. Active indicates ongoing installment schedule; fulfilled indicates all installments paid; lapsed indicates overdue with no recent payment; written-off indicates uncollectible; cancelled indicates donor withdrew commitment; pending indicates awaiting approval or activation.']
  pledge_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pledge based on its purpose and structure. Standard for general multi-installment pledges; sustainer for recurring monthly giving; major gift for high-value commitments; planned giving for estate or deferred gifts; capital campaign for infrastructure projects; endowment for permanent fund contributions.']
  reminder_schedule varchar [note: 'The timing for automated payment reminders sent to the donor before each installment due date.']
  total_pledge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value committed by the donor across all installments. This is the gross pledge amount before any payments.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding balance amount that was written off. Populated only when pledge_status is written_off.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'The date on which the pledge was written off as uncollectible. Populated only when pledge_status is written_off.']
  write_off_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the pledge was written off (e.g., donor deceased, donor financial hardship, unable to contact, donor dispute).']

  Note: 'Tracks multi-installment giving commitments, pledge agreements, and their individual installment schedules from donors, managed in Raisers Edge NXT and Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud. Captures pledge date, total pledge amount, installment schedule (monthly, quarterly, annual), number of installments, pledge balance outstanding, pledge status (active, fulfilled, lapsed, written-off), fund designation, campaign, appeal, reminder schedule, and write-off reason. Includes installment-level detail: individual due dates, scheduled amounts, actual payment dates, amounts paid, installment status (scheduled, paid, overdue, skipped, waived), payment method, and linked gift reference upon payment. Enables granular pledge fulfillment tracking, overdue installment identification, automated reminder generation, and BvA (Budget vs. Actual) reconciliation for pledged revenue. Distinct from gift — a pledge is a commitment to give, not a completed transaction.'
}

Table donor.major_gift_opportunity {
  major_gift_opportunity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the major gift opportunity record. Primary key for this entity.']
  appeal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.appeal. Business justification: Major gift opportunities can be generated from specific appeals (e.g., direct mail appeal identifies a major gift prospect). Tracking appeal attribution enables ROI analysis and source channel reporti']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fundraising campaign this opportunity is associated with, if applicable.']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor or prospect associated with this major gift opportunity. Links to the donor master record in the CRM system.']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Major gift opportunities target specific finance funds for pipeline forecasting and fund development planning. Finance uses weighted pipeline values for multi-year budget planning and endowment growth']
  indicator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.indicator. Business justification: Major gift solicitations are built around funding specific programmatic results. Linking opportunity to target indicator enables proposal development with concrete impact metrics and supports post-gif']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member (major gift officer or development officer) assigned to manage and cultivate this opportunity.']
  actual_close_date date [note: 'Actual date when the opportunity was closed (won or lost). Populated when solicitation_stage reaches closed_won or closed_lost.']
  ask_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The specific dollar amount being requested from the donor in this solicitation. Represents the formal ask figure.']
  ask_strategy varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the solicitation approach, cultivation plan, and key messaging tailored to this donor\'s interests and capacity.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this major gift opportunity record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the gift amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  donor_recognition_level varchar [note: 'Anticipated recognition tier or giving society membership the donor will qualify for if the gift is secured (e.g., Leadership Circle, Legacy Society).']
  expected_close_date date [note: 'Anticipated date when the donor is expected to make a commitment decision or when the gift will be secured.']
  expected_gift_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The anticipated or forecasted gift amount based on donor capacity, engagement level, and cultivation progress. May differ from ask amount.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The organization\'s fiscal year in which this opportunity is expected to close, used for annual fundraising goal tracking and reporting.']
  gift_purpose varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the intended use or program impact of the gift, aligned with donor interests and organizational priorities.']
  gift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the expected gift instrument or vehicle (e.g., outright cash, multi-year pledge, bequest, stock transfer).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this opportunity is currently active in the pipeline. False for closed opportunities or those marked inactive.']
  is_anonymous boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the donor has requested anonymity for this gift. True if donor wishes to remain anonymous in public recognition.']
  is_matching_gift_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this gift is eligible for corporate matching gift programs through the donor\'s employer.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent substantive interaction or touchpoint with the donor regarding this opportunity.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this opportunity record was last updated or modified.']
  loss_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or categorization of why the opportunity was closed-lost, if applicable. Used for pipeline analysis and strategy refinement.']
  next_step_action varchar [note: 'Description of the next planned cultivation or solicitation activity (e.g., site visit, proposal submission, follow-up meeting).']
  next_step_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next cultivation or solicitation activity with this prospect.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Confidential internal notes and observations about the opportunity, donor relationship dynamics, cultivation history, and strategic considerations.']
  opportunity_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or tracking code for this opportunity, used for external reporting and donor communications.']
  opportunity_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title for this major gift opportunity, typically including donor name and purpose (e.g., \'Smith Foundation - WASH Program Endowment\').']
  probability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated likelihood (0-100%) that this opportunity will close successfully, based on donor engagement, capacity, and stage progression.']
  proposal_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the formal written proposal or case for support submitted to the donor, if applicable.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of donor-imposed restrictions on the gift per FASB ASC 958 standards for not-for-profit accounting.']
  solicitation_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the major gift solicitation pipeline. Tracks progression from initial identification through cultivation, ask, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: identification|qualification|cultivation|solicitation|negotiation|stewardship|closed_won|closed_lost — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which this major gift opportunity was originally identified or sourced. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_mail|event|peer_referral|board_introduction|prospect_research|planned_giving|online — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stage_changed_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the solicitation_stage was last changed, used to track velocity through the pipeline.']
  weighted_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated forecast value (expected_gift_amount × probability_percentage) used for revenue forecasting and pipeline analysis.']

  Note: 'Pipeline management record for major gift solicitations tracked in Raisers Edge NXT and Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud. Represents a specific ask or solicitation opportunity with a named prospect or donor. Captures opportunity name, ask amount, expected gift amount, probability percentage, solicitation stage (identification, qualification, cultivation, solicitation, stewardship, closed-won, closed-lost), expected close date, assigned major gift officer, fund designation, campaign, proposal reference, and ask strategy. Enables major gift pipeline reporting and revenue forecasting.'
}

Table donor.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fundraising campaign. Primary key.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to an institutional grant or award if this campaign is tied to a specific funder\'s matching or challenge grant.']
  campaign_modified_by_user_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the campaign record.']
  campaign_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created the campaign record.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Donor-funded campaigns create compliance obligations (grant reporting, IATI publication, donor-specific audits). Campaign managers and compliance teams track obligations triggered by campaign funding ']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country_office. Business justification: Campaigns are often country-specific (e.g., Yemen Crisis Appeal, Bangladesh Rohingya Response) for donor targeting, regulatory compliance, and financial reporting. Links campaign fundraising to operat']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Donor-funded campaigns have specific compliance requirements from funding sources (reporting schedules, branding guidelines, impact metrics). Campaign managers track donor-imposed requirements to ensu']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Campaigns often have dedicated finance funds for revenue tracking, expense allocation, and donor reporting. Standard nonprofit practice for capital campaigns, annual funds, and restricted initiatives.']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or project that this campaign is raising funds for, enabling fund accounting and impact tracking.']
  parent_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent campaign if this campaign is part of a larger multi-phase or hierarchical campaign structure.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Fundraising campaigns (capital campaigns, emergency appeals) trigger state charitable solicitation registrations and filings. Compliance officers link campaigns to required regulatory submissions for ']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or user responsible for managing and executing this campaign.']
  acknowledgment_template varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the donor acknowledgment letter or email template used for gifts to this campaign.']
  appeal_channel varchar [note: 'Primary communication channel used to solicit donations for this campaign. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_mail|email|social_media|website|event|phone|peer_to_peer — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  appeal_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the campaign\'s mission, beneficiaries, and impact story used in donor communications.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used for internal tracking and gift attribution. Often used in appeal codes and donation forms.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the fundraising campaign as displayed to donors and staff.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign indicating its operational state.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fundraising campaign by its strategic purpose and donor engagement model.']
  cost_of_fundraising decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total expenses incurred to execute the campaign, including marketing, events, staff time, and materials.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the campaign goal and raised amounts.']
  donor_count varchar [note: 'Total number of unique donors who have contributed to this campaign.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the campaign officially closes. Null for ongoing campaigns without a defined end.']
  gift_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual gift transactions attributed to this campaign.']
  goal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The target fundraising amount the campaign aims to raise, expressed in the organization\'s base currency.']
  impact_report_url varchar [note: 'Web address of the campaign impact report or results summary shared with donors.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the campaign is currently active and accepting donations.']
  is_public boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the campaign is publicly visible on the organization\'s website and donation pages.']
  matching_gift_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether donations to this campaign are eligible for corporate matching gift programs.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign record was last updated.']
  roi_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated return on investment for the campaign, expressed as a percentage of net revenue over cost.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of UN Sustainable Development Goal numbers (1-17) that this campaign contributes to, supporting impact reporting.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the campaign officially begins accepting donations and engaging donors.']
  stewardship_plan varchar [note: 'Description of the donor stewardship and engagement activities planned for campaign contributors.']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'Description of the donor segment or constituency this campaign is designed to engage (e.g., major donors, monthly sustainers, corporate partners, lapsed donors).']
  tax_deductible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether donations to this campaign are tax-deductible under IRS 501(c)(3) regulations or equivalent.']
  total_raised_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount of gifts and pledges received to date for this campaign.']

  Note: 'Fundraising campaign master record managing all donor-facing fundraising initiatives in Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud. Captures campaign name, campaign type (annual fund, capital campaign, emergency appeal, planned giving, corporate partnership, digital/online), campaign goal amount, start and end dates, campaign status, target audience segment, appeal codes associated, total gifts raised to date, number of donors, cost of fundraising, and ROI. Supports campaign performance tracking and donor attribution across all gift transactions.'
}

Table donor.appeal {
  appeal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fundraising appeal. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fundraising campaign under which this appeal is organized.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Appeals are marketing campaigns with budgets and expenses (printing, postage, digital ads) tracked in cost centers. Standard nonprofit fundraising accounting—enables ROI calculation by comparing appea']
  donor_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.fund. Business justification: Appeals direct donors to specific funds. Currently fund_designation is a STRING (likely fund name/code); replacing with FK to fund.fund_id provides referential integrity and enables JOIN to retrieve f']
  acknowledgment_template varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the gift acknowledgment letter template used for donors responding to this appeal.']
  appeal_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the appeal used in gift attribution and tracking (e.g., DM2024Q1, EMAIL-SPRING, PHONE-EOY).']
  appeal_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the appeal (e.g., Spring Direct Mail Appeal, Year-End Email Solicitation).']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the appeal.']
  appeal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the appeal based on donor lifecycle stage and solicitation strategy.']
  ask_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Suggested or recommended gift amount presented to constituents in the appeal solicitation.']
  ask_string varchar [note: 'Formatted ask string presented to constituents showing multiple giving levels (e.g., $50, $100, $250, $500, Other).']
  average_gift_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average gift size for responses to this appeal calculated as total_revenue_amount / response_count.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary communication channel through which the appeal is delivered to constituents. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_mail|email|phone|digital|event|face_to_face|social_media — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  control_group_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this appeal represents the control group in an A/B test (true) or a test variant (false).']
  cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to execute the appeal including production, postage, vendor fees, and staff time.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the appeal cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the appeal record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date the appeal closes and stops accepting new responses. Nullable for ongoing appeals.']
  mailing_date date [note: 'Date the appeal solicitation was sent or deployed to the target segment.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staff member responsible for managing and executing this appeal.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified the appeal record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the appeal record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the appeal including lessons learned, special circumstances, and post-campaign observations.']
  package_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the solicitation package contents including letter, brochure, reply device, premium, and other components.']
  pieces_sent varchar [note: 'Total count of solicitation pieces (letters, emails, calls) sent to constituents for this appeal.']
  premium_offered varchar [note: 'Description of any premium or thank-you gift offered to donors responding to this appeal (e.g., tote bag, calendar, recognition pin).']
  response_count varchar [note: 'Total number of constituents who responded to the appeal with a gift or pledge.']
  response_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of constituents who responded to the appeal calculated as (response_count / pieces_sent) * 100.']
  revenue_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total revenue amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  roi_ratio decimal(10,4) [note: 'Return on investment ratio for the appeal calculated as total_revenue_amount / cost_amount. Values greater than 1.0 indicate positive ROI.']
  solicitor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary solicitor or fundraiser credited with this appeal (may be staff, volunteer, or board member).']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the appeal becomes active and begins accepting responses.']
  test_segment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this appeal is part of an A/B test or experimental segment for testing messaging, package, or channel effectiveness.']
  total_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total gross revenue generated by gifts and pledges attributed to this appeal.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created the appeal record.']

  Note: 'Specific fundraising appeal or solicitation package within a campaign, managed in Raisers Edge NXT. An appeal is a targeted ask sent to a defined segment of constituents via a specific channel (direct mail, email, phone, event). Captures appeal code, appeal name, parent campaign, appeal type (acquisition, renewal, upgrade, lapsed reactivation), mailing date, channel (mail, email, phone, digital), cost, number of pieces sent, response rate, and total revenue generated. Enables granular attribution of gifts to specific solicitation efforts.'
}

Table donor.donor_fund {
  donor_fund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the donor-designated fund record. Primary key.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Restricted funds create ongoing compliance obligations (donor reporting, audit requirements, investment policy compliance). Fund accounting tracks obligations associated with each fund to ensure restr']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country_office. Business justification: Restricted funds often have country-specific designations per donor agreements. Essential for compliance with donor restrictions, country-level financial reporting, registration requirements, and ensu']
  donor_constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or partner contact responsible for fund stewardship, donor reporting, and compliance oversight for this fund.']
  donor_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Restricted funds have donor-imposed compliance requirements (spending timelines, allowable expenses, reporting schedules). Fund managers track requirements for each restricted fund to ensure donor res']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Donor.fund (fundraising view) and finance.finance_fund (accounting view) represent the same entity from different departmental perspectives. This link enables reconciliation between development and fi']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or project to which this fund is designated. Links donor intent to operational program delivery.']
  primary_donor_constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary donor or institutional funder who established or designated this fund. Links fund to donor cultivation and stewardship records in Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud or Raisers Edge NXT.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Restricted funds, endowments, and donor-advised funds require specific regulatory disclosures (990 Schedule D, state attorney general reports). Finance teams track which funds must be reported in whic']
  audit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fund is subject to separate audit requirements beyond organizational annual audit, such as single audit or donor-specific financial audit per OMB Uniform Guidance or grant terms.']
  balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current available balance in the fund representing cumulative gifts received minus disbursements and allocations. Updated through integration with SAP S/4HANA or Unit4 ERP fund accounting modules.']
  beneficiary_population varchar [note: 'Description of the target beneficiary population or vulnerable group that this fund is designated to serve (e.g., IDPs, refugees, children under 5, women survivors of GBV).']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the fund was formally closed and ceased accepting new gifts. Nullable for active funds. Used for endowment spend-down tracking and restricted fund completion.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing special compliance requirements, donor-specific terms and conditions, procurement restrictions, or regulatory obligations unique to this fund.']
  cost_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required percentage of organizational cost-sharing or matching funds relative to total project budget. Nullable if cost share is not required.']
  cost_share_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fund requires organizational cost-sharing or matching contributions per donor agreement terms. True if cost share is mandatory.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fund record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fund\'s financial transactions and reporting. Supports multi-currency fund accounting for international NGO operations.']
  dac_sector_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit OECD DAC sector classification code indicating the primary sector focus of the fund (e.g., health, education, WASH, humanitarian aid). Required for DAC reporting by institutional donors.']
  endowment_spending_policy varchar [note: 'Description of the board-approved spending policy for endowment funds, including annual distribution rate, calculation methodology, and underwater endowment provisions per ASC 958.']
  fund_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the fund\'s purpose for portfolio management and strategic planning. Distinguishes operating funds from capital campaigns, emergency response funds, and endowments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operating|capital|program|emergency_response|endowment|scholarship|research — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the fund in donor communications, grant agreements, and financial reports. Used as the business identifier for fund accounting and stewardship.']
  fund_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose, scope, and intended use of the fund. Captures donor intent and programmatic objectives for stewardship and compliance reporting.']
  fund_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the fund as presented to donors and in financial statements. Human-readable identity label for the fund.']
  fund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fund indicating whether it is actively accepting gifts and disbursing funds, or has been closed or temporarily suspended.']
  fund_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fund based on donor restrictions and organizational policy. Determines accounting treatment per ASC 958 net asset classification requirements.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach of the fund\'s programmatic activities. Indicates whether the fund supports global operations, regional initiatives, or country-specific programs.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'Chart of Accounts (CoA) code in SAP S/4HANA or Unit4 ERP to which this fund maps for financial accounting and fund accounting purposes. Enables reconciliation between donor-facing fund designations and GL postings.']
  iati_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique IATI activity identifier for this fund if reported under IATI transparency standards. Enables public disclosure and donor coordination.']
  inception_date date [note: 'Date when the fund was established and opened for gift acceptance. Marks the beginning of the fund\'s lifecycle.']
  indirect_cost_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate for Facilities and Administration (F&A) indirect costs allowed under this fund per donor agreement or NICRA. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%).']
  investment_policy varchar [note: 'Summary of investment strategy and asset allocation policy for endowment or quasi-endowment funds. References board-approved investment policy statement.']
  minimum_gift_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum contribution threshold required to designate a gift to this fund. Used for major gift funds, endowments, and named funds with donor-established minimums.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this fund record. Supports change management and audit compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fund record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of stewardship and financial reporting to the donor or funding agency for this fund per grant agreement or donor stewardship plan.']
  restriction_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of donor-imposed restrictions, including specific programmatic purposes, geographic limitations, beneficiary populations, or time-bound conditions that govern fund usage.']
  restriction_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when time-based donor restrictions expire and temporarily restricted funds are released to unrestricted net assets. Nullable for perpetual or purpose-only restrictions.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Nature of donor-imposed restrictions on the fund. Purpose-restricted funds must be used for specific programs or activities; time-restricted funds have temporal constraints; perpetual restrictions apply to endowments.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of United Nations Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) numbers (1-17) to which this fund\'s programmatic purpose aligns. Used for impact reporting and IATI transparency.']
  target_countries varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where the fund\'s resources are designated to be used. Supports geographic restriction tracking and field operations planning.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this fund record. Audit trail for accountability and data stewardship.']

  Note: 'Donor-designated fund master record representing the specific programmatic or operational purpose to which a gift is directed. Captures fund code, fund name, fund type (restricted, temporarily restricted, unrestricted, endowment, quasi-endowment), fund description, associated program or project, GL account mapping, fund status (open, closed, suspended), minimum gift threshold, and fund stewardship contact. Serves as the bridge between donor intent (gift designation) and financial stewardship (fund accounting in SAP/Unit4). Distinct from GL account — fund is the donor-facing designation.'
}

Table donor.stewardship_activity {
  stewardship_activity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stewardship activity record. Primary key.']
  appeal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.appeal. Business justification: Stewardship activities can be triggered by specific appeals (e.g., thank-you call after appeal response, acknowledgment letter for appeal gift). Appeal attribution enables integrated donor journey tra']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fundraising campaign context for this stewardship activity. Nullable if not campaign-specific.']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor or constituent involved in this stewardship activity. Links to the constituent master record in Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud or Raisers Edge NXT.']
  distribution_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_plan. Business justification: Donor stewardship includes site visits to distributions, impact reports showing how gifts funded specific distribution plans, and donor recognition at distribution events. Critical for major donor eng']
  donor_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.fund. Business justification: Stewardship activities can be fund-specific (e.g., endowment impact report, scholarship recipient introduction, program site visit). Fund attribution enables fund-specific stewardship planning and imp']
  evaluation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.evaluation. Business justification: Stewardship meetings often discuss evaluation findings with donors to demonstrate accountability and learning. Linking stewardship activity to evaluation enables tracking which evaluations were shared']
  gift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific gift or donation that triggered or is associated with this stewardship activity. Nullable for activities not tied to a specific gift.']
  indicator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mel.indicator. Business justification: Stewardship communications share impact stories tied to specific indicators the donor funded. This link enables personalized donor reporting, tracking which indicators were discussed in stewardship me']
  major_gift_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.major_gift_opportunity. Business justification: Stewardship activities can be tied to specific major gift opportunities (e.g., proposal presentation, site visit during solicitation). Linking stewardship to active opportunities enables pipeline mana']
  pledge_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pledge commitment associated with this stewardship activity. Nullable for activities not related to a pledge.']
  primary_stewardship_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the system user who created this stewardship activity record. Audit trail for data governance.']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.project_site. Business justification: Stewardship activities include donor field visits to project sites for relationship cultivation and impact demonstration. Critical for visit logistics, security coordination, beneficiary consent manag']
  related_program_intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a specific program or project that was discussed or showcased during the stewardship activity. Nullable if not program-specific.']
  safeguarding_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safeguarding.safeguarding_incident. Business justification: Stewardship activities (site visits, donor meetings, events) may be paused or modified when safeguarding incidents involve donors. Operational link between donor engagement planning and safeguarding c']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the major gift officer, stewardship coordinator, or staff member responsible for conducting this activity.']
  acknowledgement_sent_date date [note: 'The date on which the acknowledgement communication was sent to the donor. Nullable if no acknowledgement was sent.']
  acknowledgement_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a formal acknowledgement or thank-you communication was sent following this stewardship activity.']
  activity_date date [note: 'The date on which the stewardship activity was conducted or scheduled. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  activity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the stewardship activity, including context, discussion points, and any relevant details captured by the staff member.']
  activity_outcome varchar [note: 'Summary of the outcome or result of the stewardship activity, including donor response, feedback received, and any commitments made.']
  activity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stewardship activity indicating whether it is planned, completed, cancelled, rescheduled, or pending.']
  activity_subject varchar [note: 'Brief subject line or title summarizing the purpose of the stewardship activity.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stewardship activity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acknowledgement_letter|impact_report|site_visit|phone_call|event_invitation|personal_meeting|grant_report_delivery|thank_you_call|donor_briefing|recognition_event — promote to reference product]']
  attendee_count varchar [note: 'Number of individuals who participated in the stewardship activity, including staff and constituents. Applicable for meetings, site visits, and events.']
  communication_channel varchar [note: 'The medium or channel through which the stewardship activity was conducted (in-person meeting, phone call, email, video conference, postal mail, or event).']
  completed_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the stewardship activity was marked as completed. Nullable for activities that are planned or cancelled.']
  cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The direct cost incurred for conducting this stewardship activity, including event costs, travel expenses, materials, or gifts. Nullable if no cost was tracked.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this stewardship activity record was first created in the system. Record audit trail.']
  donor_response varchar [note: 'The donor\'s response to any solicitation or engagement request made during the stewardship activity.']
  donor_sentiment varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the donor\'s sentiment or engagement level observed during the stewardship activity, used for relationship health tracking.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The length of the stewardship activity in minutes, applicable for meetings, calls, and site visits. Nullable for activities without a defined duration.']
  engagement_score varchar [note: 'Quantitative engagement score assigned to the activity based on donor responsiveness, interest level, and interaction quality. Scale typically 1-10.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'The target date by which follow-up action should be completed. Nullable if no follow-up is required.']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether follow-up action is required based on the outcome of this stewardship activity.']
  impact_story_shared_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a beneficiary impact story or program outcome was shared with the donor during this activity.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the stewardship activity took place (office address, donor site, event venue, or virtual meeting platform).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this stewardship activity record was last modified. Record audit trail.']
  next_steps varchar [note: 'Documented follow-up actions or next steps identified during or after the stewardship activity to continue donor cultivation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal staff notes and observations about the stewardship activity, donor preferences, or relationship insights. Confidential business information.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the stewardship activity indicating its importance in the overall donor cultivation strategy.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The originally scheduled date for the stewardship activity. May differ from activity_date if the activity was rescheduled or completed on a different date.']
  solicitation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The specific funding amount requested from the donor during the solicitation. Nullable if no solicitation was made.']
  solicitation_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the solicitation amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  solicitation_made_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a funding solicitation or ask was made to the donor during this stewardship activity.']
  stewardship_plan_stage varchar [note: 'The stage in the donor cultivation and stewardship lifecycle that this activity supports, aligned with the organization\'s donor relationship management framework.']

  Note: 'Records all donor stewardship touchpoints and relationship management activities conducted by major gift officers and stewardship staff. Captures activity type (acknowledgement letter, impact report, site visit, phone call, event invitation, personal meeting, grant report delivery), activity date, staff member responsible, constituent involved, related gift or pledge, activity outcome, next steps, and stewardship plan stage. Supports the donor cultivation and stewardship lifecycle and enables relationship health tracking in Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud.'
}

Table donor.planned_giving {
  planned_giving_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the planned giving arrangement. Primary key for the planned giving record.']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Planned gifts (bequests, trusts, annuities) are designated to specific finance funds, often endowments. Finance needs this for long-term fund planning, endowment management, and estate settlement proc']
  major_gift_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.major_gift_opportunity. Business justification: Planned gifts can originate from major gift opportunities (e.g., bequest commitment secured through major gift cultivation). Pipeline tracking for planned giving enables conversion analysis and gift o']
  primary_planned_donor_constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor who has made the planned giving commitment. Links to the donor master record in Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud or Raisers Edge NXT.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Planned gifts (bequests, charitable remainder trusts, life insurance) trigger IRS filings (Form 8282 for non-cash gifts, 990 disclosures). Gift administration coordinates with compliance on required r']
  attorney_contact varchar [note: 'Contact information (email or phone) for the estate planning attorney. Used for verification and coordination purposes.']
  attorney_firm varchar [note: 'Law firm or legal practice of the estate planning attorney.']
  attorney_name varchar [note: 'Name of the estate planning attorney or legal advisor who prepared the planned giving documentation.']
  commitment_date date [note: 'Date when the donor formally committed to the planned gift arrangement, typically when legal documentation was executed.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the donor has requested that the planned gift remain confidential and not be publicly acknowledged or included in donor recognition materials.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Discount rate applied for present value calculations of future planned gift proceeds. Typically based on IRS Section 7520 rate or organizational cost of capital.']
  documentation_received_date date [note: 'Date when the organization received formal legal documentation of the planned gift (e.g., copy of will provision, trust agreement, beneficiary designation form).']
  estate_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor\'s estate plan, will, or trust document that contains the planned gift provision. May include estate case number or trust name.']
  estimated_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated present or future value of the planned gift in the organization\'s functional currency. For bequests, this represents the estimated bequest amount; for trusts and annuities, the expected remainder value.']
  estimated_value_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated value (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  financial_advisor_firm varchar [note: 'Financial advisory firm or wealth management company of the financial advisor.']
  financial_advisor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial advisor or wealth manager involved in the planned giving arrangement.']
  gift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the planned gift. Tracks progression from pending notification through active commitment to maturity, realization, or potential revocation.']
  gift_vehicle_subtype varchar [note: 'Detailed subtype of the planned gift vehicle, such as CRUT (Charitable Remainder Unitrust), CRAT (Charitable Remainder Annuity Trust), or specific bequest type (residual, specific, contingent).']
  legacy_society_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the donor has been inducted into the organization\'s legacy society or planned giving recognition program as a result of this commitment.']
  legal_documentation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the legal documentation supporting the planned gift. Tracks progression from verbal intent through executed legal documents to verification and potential revocation.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'Expected date when the planned gift will be realized by the organization. For life-contingent gifts, this may be estimated based on actuarial life expectancy; for term trusts, this is the trust termination date.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the planned gift arrangement, including special conditions, donor intentions, stewardship activities, or coordination details.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date when the organization was formally notified of the planned giving intention by the donor, estate attorney, or financial advisor.']
  planned_gift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the planned giving vehicle. Includes bequest intentions, charitable remainder trusts (CRT), charitable lead trusts (CLT), charitable gift annuities (CGA), life insurance policy assignments, and retained life estate arrangements.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Life insurance policy number if the planned gift is a life insurance assignment or beneficiary designation.']
  present_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Present value of the planned gift calculated by discounting the estimated future value to current dollars using the discount rate and expected maturity date.']
  probability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated probability (0-100%) that the planned gift will be realized, based on documentation status, donor age, relationship strength, and other factors. Used for revenue forecasting.']
  realization_date date [note: 'Actual date when the planned gift was received and converted to a realized gift. Populated when the gift transitions from planned to actual.']
  realized_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual value received when the planned gift was realized. May differ from estimated value due to market changes, estate settlement, or other factors.']
  recognition_preference varchar [note: 'Donor\'s preference for recognition of the planned gift. Options include public recognition, anonymous giving, inclusion in legacy society, or no recognition.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this planned giving record was first created in the system of record.']
  record_updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier of the person or process that last updated this planned giving record.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this planned giving record was last modified in the system of record.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Net asset classification of the planned gift proceeds per ASC 958. Indicates whether the gift will be unrestricted, temporarily restricted (purpose or time), or permanently restricted (endowment).']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date when the planned gift was revoked or withdrawn by the donor. Populated if the donor amends their estate plan to remove the organization as beneficiary.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for revocation of the planned gift, if known. May include change in financial circumstances, change in philanthropic priorities, or family considerations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record from which this planned giving record originated. Typically Raisers Edge NXT for major gift tracking or Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud for constituent management.']
  stewardship_tier varchar [note: 'Stewardship tier or level assigned to this planned giving donor for cultivation and engagement purposes. May correspond to estimated gift value ranges or donor relationship status.']
  trust_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the charitable trust (CRT or CLT) if applicable. May include trust name, trust tax ID, or trustee reference number.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'Date when the estimated value was determined or last updated.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to determine the estimated value of the planned gift. Options include donor self-estimate, professional appraisal, actuarial calculation for annuities, estate planning document reference, or financial statement review.']

  Note: 'Master record for planned and legacy giving arrangements tracked in Raisers Edge NXT. Captures bequest intentions, charitable remainder trusts, charitable lead trusts, gift annuities, life insurance assignments, and other deferred gift vehicles. Tracks planned gift type, estimated value, estate/trust reference, legal documentation status, notification date, maturity/realization date, fund designation, confidentiality flag, and relationship to estate attorney or financial advisor. Supports long-term revenue forecasting and legacy donor stewardship.'
}

Table donor.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the donor segment record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fundraising campaign this segment is associated with. Null if segment is not campaign-specific.']
  owner_staff_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.staff_member']
  segment_created_by_staff_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who created this segment record.']
  segment_last_modified_by_staff_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who last modified this segment record.']
  segment_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member (fundraiser, development officer, or marketing manager) responsible for managing and stewarding this segment.']
  acquisition_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel through which donors in this segment were acquired (e.g., \'Direct Mail\', \'Online Campaign\', \'Event\', \'Peer-to-Peer\', \'Major Gift Officer\'). Null if not channel-specific.']
  appeal_code varchar [note: 'Appeal or solicitation code associated with this segment for tracking response rates and attribution. Null if not appeal-specific.']
  communication_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred communication channel for outreach to this segment: email, direct_mail, phone, sms, in_person, mixed (multi-channel).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the system.']
  criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules or filter logic defining segment membership (e.g., \'Total giving >= $10,000 in last 12 months\', \'RFM score >= 400\', \'Geographic region = East Africa\').']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System or process that created this segment: salesforce (Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud), raisers_edge (Raisers Edge NXT), manual (user-created), rfm_engine (automated RFM scoring), wealth_screening (external screening import), import (bulk data load).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the segment definition was retired or archived. Null for currently active segments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the segment definition became active and began accepting member assignments.']
  exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this segment represents an exclusion list (true) or an inclusion list (false). Exclusion segments are used to suppress communications.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic region or territory this segment targets (e.g., \'North America\', \'East Africa\', \'Global\', \'USA-Northeast\'). Null if not geographically defined.']
  giving_level_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative giving amount threshold for inclusion in this segment (e.g., $49,999 for mid-level segment). Null if no upper bound.']
  giving_level_min decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum cumulative giving amount threshold for inclusion in this segment (e.g., $10,000 for major donor segment). Null if not applicable.']
  is_dynamic boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether segment membership is dynamically recalculated based on criteria (true) or manually managed (false).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was last updated.']
  last_refresh_date date [note: 'Date when the segment membership was last recalculated or updated.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Donor lifecycle stage this segment represents: prospect (not yet donated), first_time (new donor), repeat (multi-year donor), lapsed (inactive), lybunt (Last Year But Unfortunately Not This Year), sybunt (Some Years But Unfortunately Not This Year), major (major gift donor), planned_giving (legacy/bequest donor). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospect|first_time|repeat|lapsed|lybunt|sybunt|major|planned_giving — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Current number of active constituent members assigned to this segment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the segment, including special handling instructions, historical context, or strategic rationale.']
  programmatic_focus varchar [note: 'Thematic program area or SDG (Sustainable Development Goal) focus that defines this segment (e.g., \'WASH (Water Sanitation and Hygiene)\', \'Education\', \'Health\', \'Emergency Response\'). Null if not program-specific.']
  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which dynamic segment membership is recalculated: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, on_demand (triggered by user), manual (no automatic refresh).']
  rfm_score_max varchar [note: 'Maximum RFM (Recency-Frequency-Monetary) composite score for inclusion in this segment. Null if no upper bound.']
  rfm_score_min varchar [note: 'Minimum RFM (Recency-Frequency-Monetary) composite score for inclusion in this segment. Null if RFM scoring not used.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code or abbreviation for the segment used in reporting and campaign tagging (e.g., MAJ-2024, LYBUNT-Q1, MID-ANN).']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the segment purpose, target audience, and business rationale (e.g., \'Donors who gave last year but not this year, targeted for re-engagement\').']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the donor segment (e.g., Major Donors 2024, LYBUNT Q1, Mid-Level Annual Givers).']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment: active (in use for campaigns), inactive (temporarily disabled), archived (historical, no longer used), draft (under development).']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment by its primary segmentation dimension: giving_level (major, mid-level, annual), rfm_tier (recency-frequency-monetary scoring), donor_type (individual, institutional, foundation, corporate), geographic (region-based), programmatic_interest (thematic focus area), acquisition_channel (source of donor acquisition).']
  stewardship_tier varchar [note: 'Level of stewardship and engagement this segment receives: standard (basic acknowledgment), enhanced (personalized updates), premium (quarterly reports and calls), vip (executive engagement and site visits).']
  suppression_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for exclusion if this is a suppression segment (e.g., \'Do Not Contact\', \'Deceased\', \'Unsubscribed\', \'Litigation Hold\'). Null if not an exclusion segment.']
  target_member_count varchar [note: 'Planned or target number of members for this segment, used for capacity planning and campaign sizing.']
  wealth_screening_tier varchar [note: 'Wealth capacity rating tier from prospect research or wealth screening services (e.g., \'High Net Worth\', \'Ultra High Net Worth\', \'Mid-Level Capacity\'). Null if wealth screening not applied.']
  creation_date date [note: 'Date when the donor segment was first created in the CRM (Constituent Relationship Management) system.']

  Note: 'Donor segmentation master records and constituent-to-segment membership assignments used for targeted fundraising, stewardship, and communication strategies. Captures segment definition (name, type, criteria, owner, creation date, status) and constituent membership records including assignment date, assigned-by staff member, assignment source (manual, automated RFM scoring, wealth screening import), expiry date, is_active flag, and segment transition history.
