Segment types include giving level (major, mid-level, annual), recency-frequency-monetary (RFM) tier, donor type, geographic, programmatic interest, acquisition channel, and lifecycle stage. Enables LYBUNT (Last Year But Unfortunately Not This Year), SYBUNT, and RFM-based segmentation for direct mail, email, and major gift cultivation strategies. Supports point-in-time segment membership queries and longitudinal donor behavior analysis.'
}

Table donor.wealth_screening {
  wealth_screening_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the wealth screening record. Primary key for the wealth screening data product.']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor or prospect constituent record in Raisers Edge NXT for whom this wealth screening was performed.']
  prospect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.prospect. Business justification: Wealth screening is a prospect research activity performed on prospects in the cultivation pipeline. Linking to prospect (in addition to constituent) provides pipeline context, solicitation stage, and']
  business_affiliations varchar [note: 'Summary of the constituent\'s current and past business affiliations, including corporate board memberships, executive positions, and professional associations.']
  business_ownership_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated value of private business ownership interests held by the constituent, including closely-held corporations, partnerships, and sole proprietorships.']
  capacity_rating_tier varchar [note: 'Assigned capacity rating tier or gift level classification based on the screening results, used by major gift officers for portfolio segmentation and solicitation strategy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this wealth screening record was first created in the Raisers Edge NXT system.']
  data_privacy_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the constituent has provided consent for wealth screening and prospect research activities, in compliance with data privacy regulations.']
  data_sources_used varchar [note: 'Summary of the data sources and databases consulted by the screening provider, such as public records, SEC filings, property records, and philanthropic databases.']
  estimated_net_worth decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total net worth of the constituent in USD, calculated by the screening provider based on real estate holdings, stock portfolios, business ownership, and other wealth indicators.']
  foundation_affiliations varchar [note: 'List of private foundations or donor-advised funds with which the constituent is affiliated as a trustee, officer, or donor, indicating institutional giving capacity.']
  import_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch import process that loaded this screening record from the third-party provider into Raisers Edge NXT, used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this wealth screening record was last updated or modified in the Raisers Edge NXT system.']
  net_worth_range varchar [note: 'Categorical range classification of the constituent\'s estimated net worth, used for segmentation and prioritization in major gift programs.']
  next_screening_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next wealth screening refresh or update, typically set annually or based on portfolio management cycles.']
  nonprofit_board_affiliations varchar [note: 'List or summary of nonprofit organizations where the constituent serves or has served on the board of directors, trustees, or advisory council, indicating philanthropic engagement and sector interests.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or observations recorded by the prospect researcher or major gift officer regarding the screening results, data quality, or follow-up actions.']
  philanthropic_capacity_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated annual or lifetime philanthropic giving capacity in USD, calculated by the screening provider based on wealth indicators, income, and giving history patterns.']
  political_giving_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of political contributions made by the constituent to candidates, parties, and political action committees (PACs), sourced from Federal Election Commission (FEC) records.']
  real_estate_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total value of real estate holdings owned by the constituent, including primary residence, investment properties, and land holdings.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the screening results were reviewed and approved by the major gift officer or prospect research team.']
  reviewed_by_officer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the major gift officer or prospect researcher who reviewed and validated the screening results.']
  screening_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence score or quality rating assigned by the screening provider, typically expressed as a percentage (0-100), indicating the reliability and completeness of the wealth data.']
  screening_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost in USD charged by the third-party screening provider for this individual screening or as part of a batch screening contract.']
  screening_date date [note: 'Date when the wealth screening was conducted by the third-party provider.']
  screening_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party wealth screening and prospect research vendor that performed the screening analysis.']
  screening_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the wealth screening record in the prospect research workflow, indicating whether the screening is complete, under review, or requires follow-up.']
  screening_type varchar [note: 'Type or category of wealth screening performed, indicating whether it was an individual deep-dive, batch upload, or portfolio-wide refresh.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this screening record in the source screening provider\'s system, used for reconciliation and data synchronization.']
  stock_holdings_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total value of publicly traded stock holdings and securities owned by the constituent, derived from SEC filings and public disclosures.']

  Note: 'Prospect wealth screening and capacity rating records imported from third-party research tools (DonorSearch, iWave, WealthEngine) into Raisers Edge NXT. Captures screening date, screening provider, estimated net worth, real estate holdings value, stock holdings value, philanthropic giving capacity estimate, political giving history, nonprofit board affiliations, business affiliations, screening confidence score, and assigned capacity rating tier. Supports major gift officer prioritization and portfolio management.'
}

Table donor.portfolio_assignment {
  portfolio_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for portfolio_assignment']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor or prospect constituent assigned to this portfolio. Links to the constituent master record in the CRM system.']
  primary_portfolio_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the major gift officer or fundraising staff member responsible for cultivating this constituent. Links to the staff/workforce record.']
  stewardship_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customized stewardship plan for this constituent. Links to the detailed stewardship activity schedule and touchpoint requirements.']
  affinity_focus_area varchar [note: 'The program area, cause, or mission focus that aligns with the constituent\'s philanthropic interests. Used for matching donors with appropriate gift officers and program staff.']
  assignment_created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this portfolio assignment record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  assignment_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this portfolio assignment record was first created in the CRM system. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The date when the constituent was assigned to the gift officer\'s portfolio. Marks the start of the cultivation relationship.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'The date when the portfolio assignment was terminated or transferred. Null for active assignments. Used for calculating relationship duration and officer performance metrics.']
  assignment_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this portfolio assignment record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  assignment_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this portfolio assignment record was last updated. Used for tracking data freshness and change history.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the context, history, or special considerations for this portfolio assignment. May include relationship history, donor preferences, or strategic guidance.']
  assignment_reason varchar [note: 'The business rationale for assigning this constituent to the gift officer. Provides context for portfolio management decisions and relationship continuity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_prospect|capacity_upgrade|geographic_alignment|relationship_transfer|officer_departure|portfolio_rebalance|strategic_priority|donor_request — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assignment_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the portfolio assignment became effective. Used for tracking officer workload and relationship tenure.']
  capacity_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated capacity amount. Supports multi-currency fundraising operations for international donors.']
  contact_frequency_target_days varchar [note: 'The target number of days between meaningful contacts for this portfolio tier. Used to monitor adherence to cultivation standards and identify overdue outreach.']
  engagement_readiness_indicator varchar [note: 'Assessment of the constituent\'s current receptiveness to cultivation and solicitation activities. Informs timing and approach for next moves.']
  estimated_capacity_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The prospect research team\'s estimate of the constituent\'s philanthropic capacity. Used for portfolio segmentation and solicitation strategy. Confidential business intelligence.']
  expected_ask_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The planned solicitation amount for the next major gift request. Based on capacity research, giving history, and strategic priorities. Confidential cultivation planning data.']
  expected_ask_date date [note: 'The target date for making the next major gift solicitation. Used for pipeline forecasting and officer activity planning.']
  geographic_territory varchar [note: 'The geographic region or territory this constituent belongs to, used for portfolio segmentation when officers are assigned by geography. May be city, state, region, or country.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'The most recent date the gift officer had meaningful contact with the constituent. Used for tracking engagement frequency and identifying constituents requiring outreach.']
  last_gift_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the most recent gift from this constituent. Used for upgrade potential assessment and solicitation sizing.']
  last_gift_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent gift from this constituent. Used for recency analysis and lapsed donor identification.']
  next_contact_scheduled_date date [note: 'The planned date for the next cultivation contact or stewardship touchpoint. Used for officer task management and ensuring consistent donor engagement.']
  portfolio_load_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted value representing the workload contribution of this assignment to the officer\'s total portfolio. Higher-tier prospects carry greater weight. Used for portfolio load balancing.']
  portfolio_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking of this constituent within the officer\'s portfolio based on giving capacity, engagement readiness, and strategic importance. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  portfolio_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the portfolio assignment. Active indicates ongoing cultivation; on-hold indicates temporary pause; transferred indicates reassignment to another officer; inactive indicates no current engagement; graduated indicates moved to sustained giving program; declined indicates prospect declined engagement.']
  portfolio_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the portfolio segment based on giving capacity and cultivation strategy. Determines the level of personalized engagement and stewardship required.']
  proposal_submitted_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent gift proposal was submitted to the constituent. Used for tracking proposal aging and follow-up timing.']
  proposal_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal gift proposal has been submitted to this constituent. True if proposal is pending or under consideration; false otherwise.']
  relationship_strength_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (typically 1-10 or 1-100) representing the depth and quality of the relationship between the gift officer and constituent. Used for prioritization and readiness assessment.']
  solicitation_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the major gift fundraising cycle. Tracks progression from initial prospect identification through post-gift stewardship.']
  total_lifetime_giving_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative total of all gifts from this constituent to the organization across their entire giving history. Used for portfolio segmentation and recognition planning.']
  transfer_date date [note: 'The date when the constituent was transferred from the previous gift officer to the current officer. Null if this is the initial assignment.']

  Note: 'Manages the assignment of prospects and donors to major gift officers for cultivation and stewardship. Captures assigned gift officer (staff reference), constituent assigned, portfolio tier (major gift, mid-level, planned giving), assignment date, assignment reason, portfolio status (active, on-hold, transferred), last contact date, and next scheduled contact. Enables portfolio load balancing, officer performance tracking, and relationship continuity management.'
}

Table donor.fundraising_event {
  fundraising_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fundraising event record. Primary key.']
  appeal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.appeal. Business justification: Fundraising events are often promoted through specific appeals (direct mail invitation, email invitation). Linking event to appeal enables integrated campaign tracking and multi-channel attribution an']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this fundraising event to a broader fundraising campaign or appeal in the donor management system.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fundraising events are cost centers for expense tracking, budget management, and P&L analysis. Standard nonprofit accounting practice—each event has a cost center code for capturing venue, catering, m']
  donor_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.fund. Business justification: Event proceeds typically go to ONE designated fund (e.g., gala for scholarship fund, benefit for disaster relief fund). This FK is essential for accounting, fund balance tracking, and donor stewardshi']
  email_broadcast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to communication.email_broadcast. Business justification: Fundraising events require direct email broadcast tracking for event invitations, reminders, and thank-you emails. Event marketing workflows need to link specific broadcasts (save-the-date, RSVP remin']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this fundraising event to a specific program or initiative that will benefit from the funds raised.']
  primary_digital_content_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to communication.digital_content. Business justification: Events generate dedicated digital content (event landing pages, social media posts with event hashtags, photo galleries, promotional videos). Event marketing teams need direct event-to-content linkage']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.project_site. Business justification: Fundraising events (donor site visits, impact tours) are frequently held at project sites to demonstrate impact and cultivate major donors. Essential for event logistics planning, security clearance, ']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Special events may trigger unrelated business income tax filings (Form 990-T), gaming/raffle licenses, or state charitable solicitation reports. Event managers work with compliance to ensure proper re']
  acknowledgment_sent_date date [note: 'The date when thank-you acknowledgments or tax receipts were sent to event participants and donors.']
  capacity_total varchar [note: 'The maximum number of attendees the event can accommodate based on venue or platform constraints.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the system user who created this fundraising event record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fundraising event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts associated with this event.']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code or identifier used for internal tracking and reporting of the event.']
  event_date date [note: 'The scheduled date on which the fundraising event takes place.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the fundraising event, including purpose, activities, and beneficiary impact.']
  event_end_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the fundraising event is scheduled to conclude.']
  event_manager_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the event manager for coordination and communication purposes.']
  event_manager_name varchar [note: 'The name of the staff member or volunteer responsible for managing and coordinating the fundraising event.']
  event_manager_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number of the event manager for coordination and communication purposes.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'The official name or title of the fundraising event (e.g., Annual Gala, Spring Golf Tournament, Virtual Benefit Concert).']
  event_start_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the fundraising event is scheduled to begin.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fundraising event (planned, active, completed, cancelled, postponed).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fundraising event by format and purpose (e.g., gala, benefit dinner, golf tournament, auction, virtual fundraiser, cultivation event).']
  fundraising_goal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The target revenue amount the organization aims to raise from this event.']
  is_tax_deductible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contributions made to this event qualify for tax deduction under IRS 501(c)(3) regulations or equivalent.']
  is_virtual_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fundraising event is conducted virtually (online) rather than at a physical venue.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the system user who last modified this fundraising event record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fundraising event record was last updated or modified.']
  net_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net proceeds from the event, calculated as total revenue raised minus total event cost.']
  registration_close_date date [note: 'The date when registration for the event closes and no further registrations are accepted.']
  registration_open_date date [note: 'The date when registration for the event opens to constituents.']
  sponsorship_levels_available varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of sponsorship tiers offered for the event (e.g., Platinum, Gold, Silver, Bronze).']
  tax_deductible_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of ticket price or contribution that qualifies as a tax-deductible charitable donation (e.g., 70% if 30% represents fair market value of goods/services received).']
  ticket_price_tiers varchar [note: 'Description or list of ticket pricing options available for attendees (e.g., General Admission, VIP, Student, Early Bird).']
  total_attendance varchar [note: 'The actual number of individuals who attended the fundraising event, confirmed via check-in or participation tracking.']
  total_event_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total expenses incurred to plan, organize, and execute the fundraising event (venue, catering, marketing, staff, etc.).']
  total_registrations varchar [note: 'The total number of individuals who registered for the fundraising event.']
  total_revenue_raised decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual total revenue generated from the fundraising event, including ticket sales, sponsorships, donations, and auction proceeds.']
  venue_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address for the event venue.']
  venue_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address for the event venue (suite, floor, building).']
  venue_city varchar [note: 'City where the event venue is located.']
  venue_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the event venue location.']
  venue_name varchar [note: 'The name of the physical or virtual venue where the fundraising event is hosted.']
  venue_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the event venue location.']
  venue_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the event venue is located.']
  virtual_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the online platform or service used to host the virtual fundraising event (e.g., Zoom, Microsoft Teams, custom web portal).']

  Note: 'Master record for donor-facing fundraising events and attendee registrations including galas, benefit dinners, golf tournaments, virtual fundraisers, and cultivation events managed in Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud. Captures event name, event type, event date and venue, fundraising goal, ticket price tiers, sponsorship levels, total revenue raised, total attendance, event cost, net revenue, and event manager. Includes individual constituent registration records with registration date, registration type (attendee, table sponsor, individual sponsor, volunteer), ticket type and price paid, table assignment, meal preference, attendance confirmation status, check-in timestamp, and post-event follow-up status. Enables event capacity management, seating logistics, and post-event stewardship workflows. Distinct from field operations events — fundraising events are donor cultivation and revenue generation activities.'
}

Table donor.soft_credit {
  soft_credit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the soft credit record. Primary key.']
  appeal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific appeal or solicitation associated with this soft credit. Tracks which appeal or outreach effort influenced the gift for attribution analysis.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fundraising campaign associated with this soft credit. Enables campaign-level attribution reporting and gift officer performance tracking by campaign.']
  credited_constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the constituent receiving soft credit attribution for this gift. This is the individual or organization being credited for influence or shared recognition, distinct from the primary donor of record.']
  donor_fund_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fund or designation to which the soft credit is attributed. Enables fund-specific attribution reporting and stewardship tracking.']
  gift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary gift transaction for which this soft credit is being attributed. Links to the original gift record in the donor management system.']
  major_gift_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.major_gift_opportunity. Business justification: Soft credits can be tied to major gift opportunities (e.g., gift officer who cultivated the opportunity receives soft credit when it closes). Attribution for major gift solicitation work enables perfo']
  pledge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.pledge. Business justification: Soft credits can be given for pledge solicitation (e.g., gift officer who secured the pledge commitment receives soft credit). Linking soft credit to pledge enables attribution for multi-year commitme']
  primary_soft_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that created the soft credit record. Used for audit trail and accountability tracking.']
  split_from_soft_credit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on soft_credit (split_from_soft_credit_id)']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the gift officer or fundraising staff member managing the relationship with the credited constituent. Used for gift officer performance metrics and portfolio management.']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date when the soft credit acknowledgment or notification was sent to the credited constituent. Used for stewardship tracking and compliance reporting.']
  acknowledgment_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a soft credit acknowledgment or notification has been sent to the credited constituent. True if acknowledgment sent, False if not sent. Used to track stewardship communication completion.']
  acknowledgment_template varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the acknowledgment template used for soft credit notification. Enables tracking of communication templates and stewardship messaging consistency.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total gift amount allocated to this soft credit. Used when multiple soft credits split attribution for a single gift. Value ranges from 0.00 to 100.00. Sum of all allocation percentages for a gift may equal or exceed 100% depending on organizational attribution policy.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the soft credit attribution. This amount is used for calculating donor lifetime value, household giving totals, and gift officer performance metrics. May equal the full gift amount or a partial allocation depending on attribution rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the soft credit record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the soft credit amount. Ensures accurate multi-currency tracking and reporting for international donor relationships.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the soft credit is recorded for financial and fundraising reporting purposes. Format typically YYYY or FY-YYYY depending on organizational convention.']
  is_anonymous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the soft credit should be treated as anonymous in public recognition and reporting. True if anonymous, False if public recognition is permitted. Respects donor privacy preferences.']
  lifetime_value_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this soft credit amount should be included in the credited constituent\'s lifetime giving value calculation. True if included in lifetime value, False if excluded. Used to ensure accurate donor lifetime value metrics.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the soft credit record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or explanatory information about the soft credit attribution. Used by fundraising staff to document attribution rationale or special circumstances.']
  recognition_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this soft credit counts toward donor recognition levels, giving societies, or cumulative giving milestones. True if eligible for recognition programs, False if excluded.']
  soft_credit_date date [note: 'Date when the soft credit was recorded or attributed. Typically matches the gift date but may differ if soft credit is applied retroactively or during gift processing workflows.']
  soft_credit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the soft credit record. Active indicates the credit is valid and counted in reports. Inactive indicates the credit has been deactivated. Reversed indicates the credit was removed due to gift refund or correction. Pending indicates the credit is awaiting approval or verification.']
  soft_credit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the soft credit attribution. Defines the relationship or role of the credited constituent to the gift. Common types include household member (spouse/partner sharing credit), solicitor credit (fundraiser who secured the gift), matching gift credit (employer matching program), tribute notification recipient (honoree or memorial notification), gift officer (staff member managing the relationship), and volunteer solicitor (board member or volunteer who influenced the gift).']
  solicitor_relationship varchar [note: 'Describes the relationship of the credited constituent to the organization or primary donor. Used to segment soft credit attribution by relationship type for performance analysis and stewardship planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: staff|board_member|volunteer|peer|family|advisor|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the soft credit record in the source system. Enables traceability back to the originating system for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this soft credit record originated. Used for data lineage tracking and system integration auditing.']

  Note: 'Records gift attribution splits and soft credits for donors who influence or share credit for a gift but are not the primary donor of record. Captures soft credit type (household member, solicitor credit, matching gift credit, tribute notification recipient), soft credit amount, percentage allocation, related gift reference, and credited constituent. Essential for accurate donor lifetime value calculation, household giving reports, and gift officer performance metrics. Supports joint gift attribution and influenced-giving tracking in Raisers Edge NXT and Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud.'
}

Table donor.appeal_targeting {
  appeal_targeting_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this appeal-segment targeting record. Primary key.']
  appeal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fundraising appeal being deployed to this segment']
  donor_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the donor_segment product']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the donor segment being targeted by this appeal']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who created this targeting assignment.']
  average_gift_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average gift size for responses from this segment to this appeal, calculated as total_revenue_amount / response_count. Used to compare segment giving capacity.']
  cost_allocated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of the total appeal cost allocated to this segment based on pieces sent or other cost allocation methodology. Enables segment-level ROI calculation.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the allocated cost amount.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this appeal-segment targeting record was created in the system.']
  deployment_date date [note: 'Date when the appeal was deployed to this specific segment. May differ from overall appeal mailing_date if segments are deployed in waves.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when performance metrics for this appeal-segment combination were last updated.']
  pieces_sent varchar [note: 'Number of solicitation pieces (letters, emails, calls) sent specifically to constituents in this segment for this appeal. This is segment-specific volume tracking.']
  response_count varchar [note: 'Number of constituents from this segment who responded to this appeal with a gift or pledge. Enables segment-level response analysis.']
  response_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Response rate percentage for this specific appeal-segment combination, calculated as (response_count / pieces_sent) * 100. Critical metric for segment performance evaluation.']
  revenue_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the segment revenue amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  roi_ratio decimal(10,4) [note: 'Return on investment ratio for this appeal-segment combination, calculated as total_revenue_amount / cost_allocated. Critical metric for optimizing future segment targeting decisions.']
  segment_ask_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Suggested gift amount presented to constituents in this segment, which may differ from the general appeal ask_amount based on segment giving capacity (e.g., major donors receive higher asks).']
  target_segment varchar [note: 'Description of the constituent segment targeted by this appeal (e.g., lapsed donors 12-24 months, major gift prospects $10K+, monthly sustainers). [Moved from appeal: The target_segment STRING attribute in appeal is a text description that cannot capture the reality of multi-segment targeting. When an appeal targets multiple segments (e.g., \'Major Donors\', \'LYBUNT Q1\', \'Mid-Level Annual\'), this single STRING field would require concatenation or comma-separated values, making it impossible to query segment-specific performance. This attribute should be removed from appeal as the relationship is now properly modeled through the appeal_targeting association.]']
  test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier for A/B test variant or creative package version used for this segment (e.g., \'Control\', \'Variant_A\', \'High_Ask\'). Enables multivariate testing across segments.']
  total_revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total gross revenue generated by gifts and pledges from this segment in response to this appeal. Enables segment-level revenue attribution and ROI calculation.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the targeting relationship between fundraising appeals and donor segments. It captures the operational execution of multi-segment direct marketing campaigns where a single appeal is deployed to multiple constituent segments with segment-specific performance tracking. Each record links one appeal to one donor segment with attributes that measure the effectiveness of that specific appeal-segment combination including pieces sent, response metrics, revenue attribution, and ROI analysis.. Existence Justification: In nonprofit fundraising operations, appeals routinely target multiple donor segments simultaneously with segment-specific execution parameters (different ask amounts for major vs. annual donors, different creative variants for A/B testing, wave-based deployment schedules). Each appeal-segment combination generates its own performance metrics (pieces sent, response rate, revenue, ROI) that fundraisers actively analyze to optimize future targeting decisions. This is not a theoretical correlation but an operational business process where development teams create targeting plans, execute multi-segment campaigns, and measure segment-level results.'
}

Table donor.event_registration {
  event_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this event registration record. Primary key.']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the constituent who registered for or attended the fundraising event']
  fundraising_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fundraising event for which the constituent registered']
  attendance_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the constituent\'s attendance (registered, confirmed, checked_in, no_show, cancelled). Tracks the registration lifecycle from initial signup through event day check-in. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  auction_paddle_number varchar [note: 'The paddle or bidder number assigned to the constituent for auction participation at the event. Links to auction transaction tracking. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the constituent checked in at the event. Used to confirm actual attendance and trigger post-event stewardship workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this registration record was created in the system.']
  dietary_restrictions varchar [note: 'Any dietary restrictions or meal preferences specified by the constituent during registration (vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, allergies). Used for catering planning. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  guest_count varchar [note: 'The number of guests included in this registration (e.g., table sponsorships may include multiple seats). Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this registration record was last updated.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method of payment used for the registration (credit card, check, cash, wire transfer, complimentary). Supports reconciliation and revenue tracking.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'The current payment status for this registration (paid, pending, refunded, waived). Tracks financial completion of the registration.']
  post_event_follow_up_status varchar [note: 'The status of post-event stewardship and cultivation follow-up activities for this constituent (pending, thank_you_sent, survey_sent, completed). Tracks donor engagement workflow completion.']
  registration_date date [note: 'The date on which the constituent registered for the fundraising event. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  registration_source varchar [note: 'The channel through which the constituent registered for the event (online, phone, mail, in-person, staff entry). Used for marketing effectiveness analysis.']
  registration_type varchar [note: 'The type of registration or participation role for this constituent at the event (attendee, table sponsor, individual sponsor, volunteer). Determines engagement level and stewardship approach.']
  special_requests varchar [note: 'Any special requests or accommodations requested by the constituent (accessibility needs, seating preferences, VIP treatment). Used for event logistics and donor stewardship.']
  table_number varchar [note: 'The table number or seating assignment for the constituent at the event. Used for seating logistics and VIP placement. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data (table_assignment/table_number).']
  ticket_price_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual amount paid by the constituent for their ticket or registration. May differ from standard ticket price due to discounts, early bird pricing, or sponsorship levels. Corresponds to amount_paid in detection phase data.']
  ticket_type varchar [note: 'The specific ticket tier or type purchased by the constituent (e.g., general admission, VIP, early bird). Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the registration and attendance relationship between constituents and fundraising events. It captures the complete lifecycle of constituent participation in donor cultivation and fundraising activities, from initial registration through post-event follow-up. Each record links one constituent to one fundraising event with transactional attributes including registration details, ticketing information, seating assignments, payment tracking, attendance confirmation, and stewardship workflow status. This is a core operational entity managed actively by development and events staff for capacity planning, revenue tracking, donor engagement measurement, and post-event cultivation workflows.. Existence Justification: In nonprofit fundraising operations, constituents register for and attend multiple fundraising events over their donor lifecycle (galas, golf tournaments, cultivation dinners), and each event hosts multiple constituent attendees. Event registration is an actively managed operational process with its own lifecycle: registration → payment → seating assignment → check-in → post-event follow-up. This is distinct from gift processing—not all attendees make gifts, and registration data (ticket type, table assignment, dietary restrictions, attendance confirmation) is managed separately from donation transactions.'
}

Table donor.participation {
  participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this participation record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the campaign in which the constituent participated']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the constituent who participated in the campaign']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member who solicited or engaged this constituent for this campaign. Enables tracking of fundraiser effectiveness.']
  acknowledgment_sent_date date [note: 'The date when acknowledgment communication was sent to the constituent for this participation. Null if not yet sent.']
  acknowledgment_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a thank-you or acknowledgment communication was sent to the constituent for this participation. Used for stewardship tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this participation record was first created in the system.']
  gift_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount contributed by the constituent to this campaign in this participation instance. Null if participation type is not gift. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  participation_date date [note: 'The date when this participation event occurred. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  participation_type varchar [note: 'The type of participation or engagement activity. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data. Values include solicitation (outreach attempt), gift (monetary contribution), pledge (commitment to give), volunteer (time contribution), advocacy (campaign promotion), event_attendance (campaign event participation), non_response (solicited but did not respond).']
  pledge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount pledged by the constituent to this campaign in this participation instance. Null if participation type is not pledge. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  response_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the constituent responded to the campaign solicitation. True = responded (gift, pledge, volunteer, etc.); False = solicited but no response. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  solicitation_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel through which the constituent was solicited or engaged for this campaign. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this participation record was last modified.']
  volunteer_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'The number of hours the constituent volunteered for this campaign in this participation instance. Null if participation type is not volunteer. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the participation event between constituent and campaign. It captures every instance of constituent engagement with a fundraising campaign, including solicitation attempts, responses, gifts, pledges, volunteer activities, and advocacy actions. Each record links one constituent to one campaign with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific engagement instance.. Existence Justification: In nonprofit fundraising operations, constituents participate in multiple campaigns over time (annual fund, emergency appeals, capital campaigns, etc.), and each campaign engages multiple constituents through various solicitation channels. Campaign participation is a recognized business concept that fundraising teams actively manage, track, and analyze. The relationship carries rich operational data including participation type, solicitation channel, response outcomes, gift/pledge amounts, and volunteer hours that belong to neither the constituent nor the campaign alone but to each specific engagement instance.'
}

Table donor.segment_membership {
  segment_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this segment membership record. Primary key.']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the constituent who is a member of this donor segment']
  donor_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to donor_segment. Part of the many-to-many relationship.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the donor segment to which the constituent is assigned']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member (fundraiser, development officer, or data analyst) who manually assigned this constituent to the segment. Null for automated assignments. Referenced in Product B description as assignment attribution.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the constituent was assigned to this segment (manual, automated RFM scoring, wealth screening import, campaign response, lifecycle trigger, data import). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  assignment_source varchar [note: 'Source system or process that created this membership record (e.g., \'Salesforce NPSP\', \'Raiser\'s Edge NXT\', \'RFM Batch Job\', \'Manual Entry\'). Referenced in Product B description.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment membership record was created in the system.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment membership is currently active. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment membership record was last updated.']
  membership_end_date date [note: 'Date when the constituent membership in this segment ended or expired. Null for active memberships. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  membership_start_date date [note: 'Date when the constituent was assigned to this donor segment. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  score_at_assignment varchar [note: 'RFM score, wealth rating, or other quantitative score that qualified the constituent for this segment at the time of assignment. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  segment_assignment_reason varchar [note: 'Business rationale or rule that triggered this segment assignment (e.g., \'Total giving >$10K in FY2024\', \'LYBUNT - gave $5K in 2023, $0 in 2024\', \'Wealth screening capacity $100K+\'). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the membership relationship between constituents and donor segments. It captures the assignment of constituents to fundraising and stewardship segments for targeted communication, cultivation, and reporting. Each record links one constituent to one donor segment with temporal, attribution, and scoring attributes that exist only in the context of this specific membership assignment.. Existence Justification: Constituent segmentation is a core operational fundraising process where nonprofits actively manage multi-dimensional segment assignments. Constituents simultaneously belong to multiple segments (e.g., a major donor can be in \'Major Donors 2024\', \'LYBUNT Q1\', \'East Coast Region\', and \'Health Program Interest\' at the same time), and each segment contains many constituents. The business manages segment membership as a distinct lifecycle with assignment rules, temporal tracking, scoring attribution, and transition history for targeted cultivation, stewardship, and campaign execution.'
}

Table donor.indicator_funding {
  indicator_funding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this donor-indicator funding relationship record. Primary key.']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the constituent (donor/funder) providing funding for this indicator']
  indicator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the MEL indicator being funded by this constituent']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this donor-indicator funding relationship was created in the system.']
  donor_target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The donor-specific target value for this indicator, which may differ from the indicator\'s overall target. Represents what this specific donor expects to achieve with their funding contribution.']
  funding_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary amount allocated by this donor specifically for this indicator. Currency should align with constituent\'s preferred currency or grant currency.']
  funding_end_date date [note: 'The date when donor funding for this indicator expires or the funding period ends.']
  funding_start_date date [note: 'The date when donor funding for this indicator becomes active and eligible for expenditure.']
  grant_reference_number varchar [note: 'The donor\'s grant or award reference number under which this indicator funding is provided. Links to the broader grant agreement.']
  indicator_funding_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this donor-indicator funding relationship. Active = currently funded; Pending = approved but not yet active; Completed = funding period ended; Suspended = temporarily paused; Cancelled = funding withdrawn.']
  modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this donor-indicator funding relationship was last modified.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this donor requires progress reporting specifically for this indicator. May differ from the indicator\'s standard reporting frequency due to donor-specific requirements.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'The type of restriction the donor has placed on this indicator funding. Unrestricted = no restrictions; Restricted = tied to specific activities/geographies; Temporarily Restricted = time-bound restrictions; Permanently Restricted = endowment-style restrictions; Earmarked = donor-specified use.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the funding relationship between a constituent (donor/funder) and a specific MEL indicator. It captures donor-specific funding allocations, restrictions, targets, and reporting requirements for individual indicators across programs and grants. Each record links one constituent to one indicator with funding terms, amounts, timeframes, and performance targets that exist only in the context of this donor-indicator funding relationship. Essential for donor-specific impact reporting, compliance with funder restrictions, and tracking indicator-level funding sources.. Existence Justification: In nonprofit operations, donors (bilateral agencies like USAID/DFID, multilateral organizations, foundations) fund specific MEL indicators across multiple programs and grants, and each indicator receives funding from multiple donor sources. The business actively manages these donor-indicator funding relationships with specific funding amounts, timeframes, restrictions (unrestricted vs. earmarked), donor-specific targets, and reporting frequencies that differ by donor. This is a recognized operational concept called \'Donor Indicator Funding\' or \'Indicator-Level Funding Allocation\' that program managers and MEL officers actively create, track, and report on for donor compliance and impact reporting.'
}

Table donor.event_volunteer_assignment {
  event_volunteer_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the event volunteer assignment record. Primary key.']
  fundraising_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fundraising event where the volunteer is assigned to serve']
  volunteer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the volunteer assigned to serve at the fundraising event']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The date when the volunteer was assigned to serve at this event. Tracks when the assignment was created in the system.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes specific to this volunteer assignment, such as special instructions, accommodation needs, or post-event feedback.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the volunteer assignment (confirmed, pending, cancelled, completed). Tracks the state of this specific assignment.']
  check_in_status varchar [note: 'Current attendance status of the volunteer for this event assignment (not arrived, checked in, checked out, no show). Tracks actual attendance vs. scheduled assignment.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the volunteer checked in at the event venue or virtual platform. Null if not yet checked in.']
  check_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the volunteer checked out after completing their shift. Null if not yet checked out.']
  hours_worked decimal(5,2) [note: 'The actual number of hours the volunteer worked at this event, calculated from check-in/check-out or manually recorded. Used for volunteer engagement tracking and reporting.']
  role_assignment varchar [note: 'The specific role or function assigned to the volunteer for this event (e.g., registration desk, setup crew, catering support, cleanup, greeter, auction support). This attribute exists only in the context of the assignment.']
  shift_end_time timestamp [note: 'The scheduled end date and time for the volunteer\'s shift at this event. Specific to this volunteer-event assignment.']
  shift_start_time timestamp [note: 'The scheduled start date and time for the volunteer\'s shift at this event. Specific to this volunteer-event assignment.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment of volunteers to fundraising events. It captures the specific role, shift timing, and attendance tracking for each volunteer serving at each event. Each record links one fundraising event to one volunteer with attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment relationship, enabling event staffing management, volunteer coordination, and post-event reporting.. Existence Justification: Fundraising events require multiple volunteers serving in different operational roles (registration, setup, catering, cleanup), and volunteers serve at multiple events throughout the year. The nonprofit actively manages event volunteer assignments as a distinct operational process, coordinating staffing needs, assigning specific roles and shifts, tracking attendance via check-in/check-out, and recording hours worked for each volunteer at each event. This is not a derived correlation but an operational entity that event managers create, update, and monitor.'
}

Table field.project_site {
  project_site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the project site. Primary key for the project site entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Project sites are typically cost centers in nonprofit accounting. All site expenses roll up to the site\'s cost center for financial reporting, budget vs. actual tracking, and management reporting.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country. Business justification: project_site has country_code (STRING) but no FK to the country reference table. The country table contains authoritative country metadata including codes, names, region, operational status, and human']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the country office that oversees operations at this project site.']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Field sites are funded by specific restricted or unrestricted funds. Program and finance managers need to track which fund supports each site for donor compliance reporting and fund utilization tracki']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the humanitarian program under which this project site operates.']
  network_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.network_site. Business justification: Field sites require connectivity infrastructure for operations. This link supports bandwidth planning, outage response, operational readiness assessments, and disaster recovery planning—essential for ']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner organization responsible for or collaborating on operations at this project site.']
  statutory_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.statutory_registration. Business justification: Project sites operate under legal registrations in their jurisdictions. Site establishment requires verification that the organization has legal operating authority (MOU, registration) in that locatio']
  accessibility_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of physical accessibility to the project site considering road conditions, security, terrain, and seasonal factors.']
  admin_level_1 varchar [note: 'First-level administrative division (e.g., state, province, region) where the project site is located.']
  admin_level_2 varchar [note: 'Second-level administrative division (e.g., district, county, department) where the project site is located.']
  admin_level_3 varchar [note: 'Third-level administrative division (e.g., municipality, sub-district, commune) where the project site is located.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the project site was officially closed or decommissioned. Null for active sites.']
  cluster_affiliation varchar [note: 'OCHA humanitarian cluster(s) that this project site is affiliated with for coordination purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: health|nutrition|wash|shelter|protection|education|food_security|logistics|emergency_telecom|camp_coordination|early_recovery — promote to reference product]']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for official communication with the project site.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for reaching the project site or site manager.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this project site record was first created in the system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this project site record originated (e.g., KoboToolbox, Dynamics 365, OpenStreetMap).']
  electricity_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether electrical power is available at the project site.']
  establishment_date date [note: 'Date when the project site was officially established and became operational.']
  facility_ownership varchar [note: 'Legal ownership or usage arrangement for the physical facility at the project site.']
  gis_data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or method used to capture GIS coordinates for this project site (e.g., GPS device, OpenStreetMap, satellite imagery).']
  internet_connectivity varchar [note: 'Type of internet connectivity available at the project site for data collection and reporting.']
  kobo_collection_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mobile data collection via KoboToolbox is enabled and operational at this site.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent field assessment (FGD, KII, or site visit) conducted at this project site.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the project site in decimal degrees format. Used for GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the project site in decimal degrees format. Used for GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this project site record was last modified in the system.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the project site in its lifecycle.']
  pcode varchar [note: 'OCHA-assigned unique place code (P-code) for standardized geographic referencing in humanitarian operations and reporting.']
  population_catchment varchar [note: 'Estimated number of beneficiaries or persons of concern (PoC) within the service catchment area of this project site.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the project site location, if applicable.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Current security risk assessment level for the project site based on threat analysis and security incidents.']
  site_address varchar [note: 'Physical street address or location description of the project site.']
  site_area_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total physical area of the project site measured in square meters.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the project site for field operations and reporting. Used in SitReps and OCHA coordination.']
  site_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the project site including physical characteristics, surrounding environment, and operational context.']
  site_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staff member responsible for managing operations at this project site.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the project site or operational location.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the project site based on its primary function and service delivery model.']
  water_source_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a reliable water source is available at or near the project site for WASH operations.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical field project site or operational location where humanitarian programs are delivered. Captures site identity, GIS coordinates, administrative boundaries (country, region, district), site type (health post, distribution point, WASH facility, school, camp), operational status, accessibility rating, population catchment area, cluster affiliation, and OCHA P-code. Serves as the geographic anchor for all field operations, service delivery events, and SitRep generation.'
}

Table field.country_office {
  country_office_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the country office record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Country offices are organizational cost centers. All country-level expenses are tracked against the office\'s cost center for financial management, budget tracking, and HQ consolidation reporting.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country. Business justification: country_office has iso_country_code and country_name as STRING attributes but no FK to the country reference table. The country table contains authoritative country data including country_code_alpha2,']
  network_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.network_site. Business justification: Country offices are primary network nodes. This link is essential for IT infrastructure management, disaster recovery planning, connectivity SLA tracking, and bandwidth allocation—critical for operati']
  parent_office_country_office_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent country office or regional office to which this office reports, enabling hierarchical office structure.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address or building location of the country office.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite, floor, or building name.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the country office is located.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the country office ceased operations or was officially closed.']
  country_director_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the country director for official communications.']
  country_director_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the senior management official responsible for leading the country office operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this country office record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the primary local currency used in financial operations at this country office.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for official correspondence with the country office.']
  establishment_date date [note: 'Date when the country office was first established and began operations.']
  is_emergency_response boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this office was established specifically for emergency or humanitarian crisis response operations.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the office location in decimal degrees for Geographic Information System (GIS) mapping and field coordination.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Official legal name under which the country office is registered with host government authorities, which may differ from the operational office name.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the office location in decimal degrees for Geographic Information System (GIS) mapping and field coordination.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this country office record was last modified in the system.']
  mou_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the host government became effective.']
  mou_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the host government expires.']
  mou_with_government boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) is in place with the host government defining the terms of operation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about the country office, including operational context, special considerations, or historical information.']
  ocha_cluster_participation varchar [note: 'List of Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA) humanitarian clusters in which this country office actively participates (e.g., WASH, Health, Protection, Education).']
  office_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the country office following organizational naming convention (e.g., KEN-NAI01 for Nairobi office in Kenya).']
  office_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the country office or sub-office (e.g., Kenya Country Office, Nairobi Sub-Office).']
  office_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the office based on its operational mandate and scope within the organizational hierarchy.']
  operational_mandate varchar [note: 'Description of the office\'s operational mandate, including sectors of focus (e.g., Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH), health, education, protection) and target populations served.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the country office indicating whether it is actively managing field operations.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the country office.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the office location.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date when the country office was officially registered with the host government.']
  registration_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current host government registration expires and requires renewal.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number or certificate number issued by the host government for the country office\'s legal operations.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the office\'s legal registration with the host government authorities, required for operational legitimacy.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Current security risk assessment level for the country office location, informing operational protocols and staff safety measures.']
  staff_count varchar [note: 'Total number of staff members (national and international) currently assigned to this country office.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region within the host country.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the country office location (e.g., Africa/Nairobi, Asia/Damascus) for scheduling and coordination purposes.']

  Note: 'Master record for each NGO country office or sub-office managing field operations within a specific country or region. Captures office name, ISO country code, office type (country office, sub-office, field hub, liaison office), physical address, operational mandate, registration status with host government, legal entity details, and responsible senior management. Links to project sites, grants, and workforce assignments within that country.'
}

Table field.distribution_event {
  distribution_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution event. Primary key.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grant funding this distribution event.']
  cluster_coordination_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OCHA cluster responsible for coordinating this distribution (e.g., WASH, Shelter, Food Security).']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Distributions are charged to cost centers for expense tracking. Finance needs this for cost allocation, budget vs. actual reporting, and management reporting by cost center.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to field.country. Business justification: distribution_event has country_code (STRING) but no FK to the country reference table. Distribution events are country-specific operations requiring linkage to country-level operational context includ']
  distribution_vehicle_it_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.it_asset. Business justification: Distribution events use vehicles/equipment for logistics. Tracking asset utilization supports cost allocation, maintenance planning, mileage tracking, and insurance claims—essential for distribution o']
  field_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field team responsible for implementing this distribution event.']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Each distribution is charged to a specific fund (often donor-restricted). Financial managers must track fund utilization by distribution for donor reporting, fund balance management, and compliance.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to beneficiary.household. Business justification: Distributions target households as the unit of food/NFI assistance. Core to food security programming, household entitlement tracking, and distribution planning. Standard practice in WFP, UNHCR, and g']
  impact_story_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to communication.impact_story. Business justification: Distribution events are key moments for capturing beneficiary stories and program outcomes for donor stewardship. Links field operations to communication content sourcing for fundraising and advocacy.']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent program under which this distribution event is conducted.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Distribution events are frequently implemented by partner organizations in humanitarian operations. Tracking the implementing partner is essential for accountability reporting, partner performance man']
  partnership_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Distributions are often governed by specific partnership agreements defining scope, modalities, beneficiary targeting, and compliance requirements. Linking enables agreement-level expenditure tracking']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to communication.communication_plan. Business justification: Distribution events require communication plans for beneficiary mobilization, distribution scheduling announcements, and post-distribution messaging. Essential for community engagement and accountabil']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project site where the distribution event took place.']
  registrant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to beneficiary.registrant. Business justification: Individual distributions (cash transfers, vouchers, individual rations) target specific beneficiaries. Essential for individual entitlement tracking, cash programming, and beneficiary-level distributi']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member who approved the distribution event plan.']
  actual_beneficiary_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of beneficiaries who received assistance in this distribution event, verified through registration or biometric verification.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the distribution event concluded.']
  actual_expenditure_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure incurred for this distribution event, including commodity costs, logistics, and operational expenses.']
  actual_household_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of households that received assistance in this distribution event.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the distribution event commenced on the ground.']
  admin_level_1 varchar [note: 'First-level administrative division (e.g., governorate, province, state) where the distribution event took place.']
  admin_level_2 varchar [note: 'Second-level administrative division (e.g., district, county) where the distribution event took place.']
  admin_level_3 varchar [note: 'Third-level administrative division (e.g., sub-district, municipality) where the distribution event took place.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the distribution event plan was approved by the program manager or field coordinator.']
  budget_allocated_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budget amount allocated for this distribution event, used for Budget versus Actual (BvA) reconciliation.']
  commodity_category varchar [note: 'Primary category of items distributed in this event. For NFI distributions may include shelter materials, hygiene kits, blankets; for food distributions may include staple foods, fortified foods; for WASH may include water containers, purification tablets. Pipe-enum overflow candidate with 15+ standard categories.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this distribution event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this distribution event.']
  data_collection_method varchar [note: 'Tool or method used to collect field data during the distribution event: KoboToolbox, CommCare, ODK (Open Data Kit), paper form, mobile app, or tablet-based system.']
  distribution_event_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the distribution event, typically formatted as country-cluster-sequence (e.g., SYR-WASH-000123).']
  distribution_event_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the distribution event for reporting and communication purposes.']
  distribution_location_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the distribution event location, captured via GPS or GIS system.']
  distribution_location_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the distribution event location, captured via GPS or GIS system.']
  distribution_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the distribution location (e.g., community center, camp sector, village name).']
  distribution_modality varchar [note: 'Method of assistance delivery: in-kind (physical goods), cash (physical currency), voucher (paper/card voucher), mobile money (digital wallet), e-transfer (bank transfer), or hybrid (combination of modalities).']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the distribution event in the operational workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|approved|in_progress|completed|cancelled|suspended|under_review — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  distribution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution approach: general (universal coverage), targeted (specific beneficiary criteria), blanket (entire population segment), voucher redemption (voucher exchange), emergency response (rapid onset), or seasonal (recurring planned distribution).']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any incident, disruption, or complaint that occurred during the distribution event.']
  incident_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any security incident, operational disruption, or beneficiary complaint was reported during this distribution event.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this distribution event record was last updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, observations, or context about the distribution event.']
  pdm_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the Post-Distribution Monitoring activity for this distribution event was completed.']
  pdm_scheduled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Post-Distribution Monitoring activity has been scheduled for this distribution event to assess beneficiary satisfaction and assistance effectiveness.']
  planned_beneficiary_count varchar [note: 'Target number of beneficiaries planned to receive assistance in this distribution event.']
  planned_household_count varchar [note: 'Target number of households planned to receive assistance in this distribution event.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date for the distribution event to take place.']
  sitrep_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution event has been included in a Situation Report (SitRep) submitted to OCHA or other coordination bodies.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify beneficiary identity and eligibility at the distribution point: biometric (fingerprint/iris), token (physical token), ID card (government ID), beneficiary list (pre-registered list), household head (representative), or mobile verification (SMS/app-based).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a single distribution event (NFI, food, cash, or voucher) conducted at a project site. Captures event date, site, distribution type (general, targeted, blanket, voucher redemption), planned vs. actual beneficiary count, commodity categories, distribution modality (in-kind, cash, voucher, mobile money), responsible cluster, implementing field team, verification method, and PDM scheduling status. Core operational transaction for field service delivery and the primary unit of work for BvA reconciliation against supply pipeline.'
}

Table field.field_distribution_line {
  field_distribution_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution line item. Primary key.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grant or funding source that financed this distribution line item. Enables grant-level expenditure tracking and compliance reporting.']
  distribution_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent distribution event header. Links this line item to the overall distribution activity.']
