  source_system_contact_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier assigned to this contact record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Salesforce Contact ID, Dynamics 365 Contact GUID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing in the lakehouse Silver layer.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner contact was first formally engaged or registered in the partnership management system. Marks the beginning of the contact\'s active role in the partnership lifecycle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the partner contact record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner contact\'s identity and credentials were last verified as part of the partner due diligence process. Used to schedule re-verification cycles and maintain compliance audit trails.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the identity and credential verification process for the partner contact. Supports due diligence requirements for sub-award management and partner capacity assessments per USAID, DFID, and OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 requirements.']

  Note: 'Master record for individual contacts within partner organizations including focal points, authorized representatives, technical leads, and financial officers. Captures name, title, role type, communication channels, language preference, and active status. Supports relationship management and coordination workflows across the partnership lifecycle.'
}

Table partnership.agreement_amendment {
  agreement_amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each formal modification record to a partnership agreement. Serves as the primary key for the agreement_amendment entity.']
  partnership_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent partnership agreement (MoU, LoA, SoW, or sub-award) that this amendment modifies. Links the amendment to its originating agreement record.']
  primary_agreement_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member record of the individual who formally approved this amendment. Links to the workforce domain for accountability and audit trail purposes.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known sequential identifier for this amendment within the context of the parent agreement (e.g., AMD-2024-001). Used in official correspondence, donor reporting, and compliance filings. Supports full amendment history tracking per agreement.']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the amendment record. Draft indicates preparation in progress; pending_approval indicates submission to approval authority; approved indicates fully executed; rejected indicates declined by approver; superseded indicates replaced by a subsequent amendment; withdrawn indicates retracted before approval.']
  amendment_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title summarizing the purpose of the amendment (e.g., \'No-Cost Extension to December 2025\', \'Budget Realignment Q3 2024\'). Used in correspondence, dashboards, and partner communications.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the formal modification being made to the agreement. Scope change alters deliverables or geographic coverage; budget realignment redistributes funds across cost categories; no-cost extension extends the period without additional funding; period of performance adjusts start/end dates; signatory change updates authorized representatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scope_change|budget_realignment|no_cost_extension|period_of_performance|signatory_change|other — promote to reference product]']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or title of the individual, committee, or organizational body with delegated authority to approve this amendment (e.g., Country Director, Grants Manager, Donor Agreement Officer). Supports RACI accountability tracking.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the amendment was formally approved by the designated approval authority. Null if not yet approved or if rejected/withdrawn.']
  beneficiary_target_change boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment modifies the target beneficiary numbers, population groups, or vulnerability criteria for the program. True = beneficiary targets modified; False = no change. Relevant for MEL/MEAL reporting and SDG indicator tracking.']
  budget_change_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net monetary change to the agreement budget introduced by this amendment (revised_budget_amount minus original_budget_amount). Positive values indicate budget increases; negative values indicate reductions. Null for non-financial amendments.']
  clauses_modified varchar [note: 'Enumeration or description of the specific agreement clauses, articles, or sections that are modified by this amendment (e.g., \'Article 3 — Scope of Work, Article 7 — Budget\'). Supports legal review and compliance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this amendment record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with record-keeping requirements under 2 CFR 200 and FASB ASC 958.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts in this amendment record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency partnership portfolios.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or URL pointing to the executed amendment document (signed PDF or equivalent). Supports audit trail and compliance evidence requirements.']
  donor_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number, letter identifier, or correspondence tracking code for the donor\'s written prior approval of this amendment. Required for audit evidence when donor_prior_approval_required is True.']
  donor_prior_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment requires prior written approval from the donor or prime recipient before implementation, as mandated under 2 CFR 200.308 or specific award terms. True = prior approval required; False = notification only or no approval needed.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the amendment becomes legally binding and operationally effective. This is the real-world event date, distinct from the signature date or record creation timestamp. Critical for period-of-performance tracking and financial reporting.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which all required signatories formally signed and executed the amendment document. May differ from the effective date when retroactive amendments are issued.']
  extension_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which the period of performance is extended by this amendment. Null for non-extension amendments. Used for program planning, resource allocation, and donor reporting on timeline changes.']
  geographic_scope_change boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment modifies the geographic coverage or operational areas of the agreement (e.g., adding or removing districts, countries, or OCHA cluster areas). True = geographic scope modified; False = no geographic change.']
  gms_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for this amendment as recorded in the Grant Management System (GMS) or donor\'s award management platform (e.g., USAID ASIST, DFID ARIES). Enables cross-system reconciliation and donor audit support.']
  iati_activity_identifier varchar [note: 'IATI-standard activity identifier associated with the parent agreement, used to publish amendment data to the IATI Registry for transparency and open data compliance. Format follows IATI hierarchical identifier standard.']
  is_no_cost_extension boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment constitutes a no-cost extension (NCE), extending the period of performance without additional funding. True = NCE; False = other amendment type. Critical for grant closeout planning and donor reporting.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the business rationale for the amendment, including the circumstances that necessitated the change (e.g., context changes, operational constraints, donor-directed modifications). Required for donor reporting and audit compliance under 2 CFR 200.']
  ngo_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized representative of Ngo who signs this amendment on behalf of the organization. Required for legal enforceability and signatory change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, internal commentary, or operational notes related to this amendment that do not fit structured fields. Used by grants management and partnership teams for internal coordination.']
  original_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total authorized budget of the agreement prior to this amendment, expressed in the agreement currency. Retained for Budget versus Actual (BvA) analysis and audit trail of financial modifications.']
  original_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period of performance as defined in the agreement prior to this amendment. Used to calculate extension duration and track no-cost extension history.']
  original_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period of performance as defined in the agreement prior to this amendment. Retained for audit trail and comparison purposes when a period-of-performance amendment is processed.']
  partner_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized representative of the partner organization who signs this amendment on behalf of the CSO, CBO, INGO, government body, or UN agency. Required for signatory change amendments and legal enforceability.']
  revised_beneficiary_target varchar [note: 'Updated total number of direct beneficiaries targeted under the agreement following this amendment. Null if beneficiary_target_change is False. Used for MEL/MEAL indicator tracking and donor results reporting.']
  revised_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total authorized budget of the agreement as updated by this amendment. Null if this amendment does not modify the budget. Used for BvA reporting and donor financial compliance.']
  revised_end_date date [note: 'The updated end date of the period of performance as established by this amendment. Null if this amendment does not modify the end date. Key field for no-cost extension tracking.']
  revised_start_date date [note: 'The updated start date of the period of performance as established by this amendment. Null if this amendment does not modify the start date.']
  scope_change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing changes to the Scope of Work (SoW), program activities, geographic coverage, target beneficiary populations, or deliverables introduced by this amendment. Null if amendment type is not scope_change.']
  submitted_date date [note: 'The date on which the amendment request was formally submitted to the approval authority or donor/prime for review. Used to track processing timelines and compliance with advance notification requirements under 2 CFR 200.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this amendment record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing formal modifications to existing partnership agreements including scope changes, budget realignments, no-cost extensions, period of performance adjustments, and signatory changes. Tracks amendment number, amendment type, effective date, justification, approval authority, and the specific clauses modified. Maintains the full amendment history of each agreement.'
}

Table partnership.partner_performance_review {
  partner_performance_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each partner performance review record. Primary key for the partner_performance_review data product.']
  implementation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or project that the implementing partner is delivering under review.']
  internal_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.internal_review. Business justification: Partner performance reviews may trigger or reference internal compliance reviews when issues are identified. Links performance monitoring to compliance oversight. Essential for integrated risk managem']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the implementing partner organisation being reviewed. Links to the partner master record in the partnership domain.']
  partnership_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing agreement (MoU, LoA, SoW, or sub-award) under which this performance review is conducted.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to communication.communication_plan. Business justification: High-performing partnerships warrant visibility in donor communications, annual reports, and advocacy materials. Links performance reviews to communication plans for identifying success stories, coord']
  primary_partner_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or team lead who conducted and is accountable for this performance review.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the performance review was formally approved by the authorised reviewer or partnership manager.']
  areas_for_improvement varchar [note: 'Narrative description of performance gaps, weaknesses, or risks identified during the review that require corrective action or capacity strengthening.']
  beneficiary_feedback_incorporated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether beneficiary feedback (e.g., from Post-Distribution Monitoring (PDM), Focus Group Discussions (FGD), or KoboToolbox surveys) was incorporated into the review findings.']
  budget_utilisation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the sub-award budget expended by the partner during the review period (0.00–100.00). Key Budget versus Actual (BvA) indicator for financial stewardship assessment.']
  capacity_building_areas varchar [note: 'Narrative description of specific capacity building areas recommended for the partner (e.g., financial management, MEL systems, safeguarding, ICT4D). Populated when capacity_building_recommended is True.']
  capacity_building_recommended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reviewer recommends a formal capacity building or organisational development intervention for the partner based on identified gaps.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of required corrective actions, including specific steps the partner must take to address identified deficiencies. Populated when corrective_action_required is True.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the partner must demonstrate completion of required corrective actions. Null when corrective_action_required is False.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Corrective Action Plan (CAP) is required as an outcome of this review. True triggers a CAP workflow in the partnership management system.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action plan associated with this review. Tracks partner remediation progress for risk management and compliance reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance review record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  field_visit_conducted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a physical field visit to the partner\'s implementation site was conducted as part of this review. Required for high-risk partners per OMB Uniform Guidance subrecipient monitoring standards.']
  financial_accountability_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the partner\'s financial management practices including Budget versus Actual (BvA) variance, expenditure documentation, and compliance with Indirect Cost Rate (ICR) / NICRA requirements.']
  key_findings_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the principal findings from the performance review covering programmatic, financial, and compliance dimensions. Informs partnership renewal and risk management decisions.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance review record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  milestone_achievement_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of agreed SoW milestones achieved within the review period (0.00–100.00). Key Results-Based Management (RBM) indicator for partner performance tracking.']
  milestones_achieved_count varchar [note: 'Number of SoW milestones fully achieved within the review period. Numerator for milestone achievement rate calculation.']
  milestones_planned_count varchar [note: 'Total number of SoW milestones scheduled for delivery within the review period. Denominator for milestone achievement rate calculation.']
  next_review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next performance review of this partner under the current agreement. Determined by risk level and agreement terms.']
  overall_performance_rating varchar [note: 'Composite performance rating assigned to the partner for the review period. Drives partnership renewal, risk classification, and sub-award decisions. Aligned with DAC evaluation criteria.']
  overall_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric composite score (e.g., 0.00–100.00) aggregating all scored dimensions of the review. Enables quantitative ranking and trend analysis across partners.']
  partner_acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date on which the implementing partner formally acknowledged the review findings. Null until acknowledgement is received.']
  partner_acknowledgement_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the implementing partner has formally acknowledged receipt of the review findings and agreed corrective actions. Required for CHS accountability compliance.']
  partnership_renewal_recommendation varchar [note: 'Reviewer\'s recommendation on whether to renew, conditionally renew, or terminate the partnership agreement based on this review\'s findings. Feeds into partnership pipeline decisions.']
  programmatic_quality_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the partner\'s delivery quality against agreed SoW milestones, Theory of Change (ToC) outputs, and LogFrame indicators. Distinct from financial accountability.']
  reporting_compliance_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the partner\'s adherence to agreed reporting schedules, SitRep submissions, and donor reporting requirements including IATI data publication obligations.']
  reports_due_count varchar [note: 'Total number of reports required from the partner during the review period per the agreement schedule.']
  reports_submitted_count varchar [note: 'Number of required narrative and financial reports submitted by the partner during the review period.']
  review_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the performance review meeting or assessment was conducted with the partner.']
  review_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the performance review. Field visits provide direct observation; desk reviews rely on submitted documentation; joint reviews involve donor or cluster lead participation.']
  review_period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the performance period being assessed in this review. Together with review_period_start_date defines the assessment window.']
  review_period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the performance period being assessed in this review. Aligns with the reporting cycle defined in the partner SoW.']
  review_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this review for tracking in correspondence, donor reports, and audit trails. Format: PPR-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the performance review record. Drives workflow routing for approval and archiving.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review occasion. Scheduled reviews occur at fixed intervals per the SoW; triggered reviews are initiated by risk events or compliance concerns; final reviews occur at agreement close-out.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the partner as an outcome of this review. Informs sub-award monitoring intensity, financial controls, and escalation protocols.']
  safeguarding_compliance_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the partner\'s compliance with safeguarding policies including GBV prevention, child protection, and HAP/CHS accountability commitments.']
  strengths_noted varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the partner\'s notable strengths and positive performance areas identified during the review. Used for learning and best-practice documentation.']

  Note: 'Periodic performance review records for implementing partners assessing delivery against agreed SoW milestones, reporting compliance, financial accountability, and programmatic quality. Captures review period, performance rating, key findings, corrective action requirements, and reviewer details. Feeds into partnership renewal and risk management decisions.'
}

Table partnership.consortium {
  consortium_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the consortium record. Primary key for the consortium master data product within the partnership domain.']
  advocacy_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to communication.advocacy_campaign. Business justification: Consortia frequently run joint advocacy campaigns on policy issues, humanitarian access, or localization. Tracks which advocacy efforts are consortium-led versus single-organization for coordinating m']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.intervention. Business justification: Consortia are formed to deliver specific interventions through multi-partner collaboration. Linking consortium to intervention enables consolidated program reporting, multi-partner results tracking, a']
  lead_partner_org_id varchar [note: 'FK to partnership.partner_org']
  ngo_focal_point_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Consortium management requires designated NGO staff focal point for coordination, steering committee participation, and member communication. Standard practice in multi-agency humanitarian response co']
  partnership_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partnership_agreement. Business justification: Consortium currently stores agreement_reference as STRING but has no FK to the actual partnership_agreement record. Consortia are formalized through partnership agreements (MoUs). Adding partnership_a']
  primary_donor_constituent_id varchar [note: 'FK to donor.constituent']
  single_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.single_audit. Business justification: Consortia receiving US federal funds above threshold require single audits per OMB Uniform Guidance. Links consortium structure to regulatory audit requirement. Essential for federal grant compliance ']
  chs_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Status of the consortium\'s adherence to the Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) on Quality and Accountability, assessed through partner due diligence and capacity assessments.']
  consortium_name varchar [note: 'Official full name of the multi-partner consortium or joint programming arrangement as registered with the lead donor or coordinating body (e.g., OCHA cluster).']
  consortium_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consortium arrangement indicating Ngo\'s role and the structural form. Lead_agency means Ngo is the prime recipient; member means Ngo is a sub-partner; pooled_fund indicates a CERF or similar mechanism. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lead_agency|member|co_implementer|pooled_fund|consortium_of_consortia — promote to reference product]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the primary country of consortium operations. For multi-country consortia, this represents the lead country of registration or primary operational context.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consortium record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  decision_making_protocol varchar [note: 'Formal protocol governing how decisions are made within the consortium. Consensus requires unanimous agreement; majority_vote uses simple majority; lead_agency_authority delegates decisions to the prime recipient; weighted_vote allocates votes by funding share.']
  due_diligence_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent due diligence assessment for the consortium was completed. Used to track expiry and schedule renewal per donor compliance requirements.']
  due_diligence_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the due diligence process for the consortium arrangement, covering financial management capacity, safeguarding policies, and anti-terrorism compliance checks.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which the consortium agreement expires or joint programming is scheduled to conclude. Nullable for open-ended arrangements. Critical for compliance reporting and closeout planning.']
  funding_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the total funding envelope (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Aligns with IATI currency reporting standards and SAP S/4HANA fund accounting.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic coverage of consortium operations, including countries, regions, or administrative zones. May reference GIS-coded areas or OCHA P-codes for field operations.']
  governance_structure varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the consortium\'s governance model including steering committee composition, decision-making protocols, voting rights, and escalation procedures as defined in the consortium agreement.']
  grand_bargain_localization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consortium is formally counted toward Ngo\'s Grand Bargain localization commitments, specifically the 25% funding to local and national actors target.']
  iasc_working_group varchar [note: 'Name of the IASC working group or task force with which this consortium coordinates, relevant for inter-agency humanitarian response planning and joint needs assessments.']
  iati_identifier varchar [note: 'Globally unique IATI activity identifier for the consortium, enabling public transparency reporting on aid flows, partner contributions, and results per IATI Standard requirements.']
  localization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total consortium funding envelope directed to local and national CSOs or CBOs as sub-partners, tracked against Grand Bargain 25% localization commitment.']
  logframe_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document title of the consortium\'s Logical Framework (LogFrame) used for results-based management (RBM), KPI tracking, and donor reporting.']
  mel_framework_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document title of the consortium\'s joint MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning) or MEAL (Monitoring Evaluation Accountability and Learning) framework used for collective results measurement.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Total number of partner organizations formally participating in the consortium, including lead agency and all sub-partners (INGOs, CSOs, CBOs, UN agencies, government bodies).']
  ngo_funding_share decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the total consortium funding envelope allocated to Ngo, whether as lead agency managing sub-awards or as a sub-partner receiving a sub-grant. Used for BvA (Budget versus Actual) reporting and ICR/NICRA calculations.']
  ngo_role varchar [note: 'Specific role that Ngo plays within this consortium arrangement. Lead_agency means Ngo holds the prime award and manages sub-awards; co_lead means shared leadership; sub_partner means Ngo receives a sub-award from the lead.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contextual information about the consortium not captured in structured fields, including coordination challenges, strategic rationale, or historical context.']
  ocha_cluster_alignment varchar [note: 'Name of the OCHA humanitarian cluster(s) or sector working group(s) to which this consortium is formally aligned or co-leads (e.g., WASH Cluster, Food Security Cluster, Health Cluster, Protection Cluster).']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the consortium. Pipeline indicates pre-formation; active indicates ongoing joint programming; suspended indicates temporary halt; closed indicates completed; dissolved indicates formally terminated with no further obligations.']
  pooled_fund_mechanism varchar [note: 'Name of the pooled funding mechanism associated with the consortium if applicable (e.g., CERF, Country-Based Pooled Fund (CBPF), ECHO, BHA). Supports ODA tracking and joint donor reporting.']
  reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the consortium by the lead donor, OCHA, or internal grant management system (GMS) for cross-system identification and reporting.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Agreed frequency of joint donor reporting and SitRep (Situation Report) submissions for the consortium, as stipulated in the consortium agreement or donor grant conditions.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of UN Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) numbers to which the consortium\'s programming contributes (e.g., \'2,3,6\' for Zero Hunger, Good Health, Clean Water). Supports IATI and donor reporting.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used name or acronym for the consortium used in SitReps, donor reports, and inter-agency communications.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the consortium agreement becomes effective and joint programming operations commence. Used for grant financial management period-of-performance tracking.']
  steering_committee_composition varchar [note: 'Description of the steering committee membership including designated representatives from each partner organization, their roles (chair, co-chair, member), and meeting frequency.']
  thematic_focus varchar [note: 'Primary humanitarian or development sector(s) addressed by the consortium (e.g., WASH, GBV, food security, health, education, PSS). Aligns with OCHA cluster system and IASC sector classifications.']
  theory_of_change_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document title of the consortium\'s Theory of Change (ToC) that articulates the causal pathway from inputs and activities to intended outcomes and impact. Used in MEL and MEAL frameworks.']
  total_funding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total funding envelope committed to the consortium across all member organizations and donor sources, expressed in the reporting currency. Used for Grand Bargain localization tracking and joint donor reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consortium record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking and data governance.']

  Note: 'Master record for multi-partner consortia and joint programming arrangements where Ngo acts as lead agency or member. Captures consortium name, lead organization, governance structure (steering committee composition, decision-making protocols), consortium agreement reference, geographic scope, thematic focus, total funding envelope, and operational status. Supports coordination with OCHA cluster leads, IASC working groups, and pooled fund mechanisms. Essential for Grand Bargain localization commitments and joint donor reporting.'
}

Table partnership.consortium_member {
  consortium_member_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the consortium member association record.']
  consortium_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consortium arrangement that this member belongs to.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner organization participating as a member of this consortium.']
  capacity_assessment_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent capacity assessment was conducted for this member organization.']
  capacity_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall capacity assessment score for this member organization at the time of consortium formation, typically on a scale of 0-100.']
  contribution_type varchar [note: 'The primary type of contribution this member provides to the consortium (financial resources, in-kind goods/services, technical assistance, capacity building, advocacy, coordination).']
  cost_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of total project costs that this member organization must contribute as cost-sharing or matching funds.']
  cost_share_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this member organization is required to provide cost-sharing or matching funds as part of their consortium participation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this consortium member record was first created in the system.']
  due_diligence_completion_date date [note: 'The date when the due diligence review for this member organization was completed.']
  due_diligence_status varchar [note: 'Status of the due diligence review conducted on this member organization (passed, conditional approval, failed, pending review, expired).']
  funding_allocation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The absolute monetary amount allocated to this member organization from the consortium budget.']
  funding_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of total consortium funding allocated to this member organization for their scope of work.']
  funding_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the funding allocation amount.']
  geographic_responsibility varchar [note: 'The geographic area, region, or administrative unit where this member has primary implementation responsibility within the consortium arrangement.']
  indirect_cost_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The negotiated indirect cost rate (ICR) or facilities and administration (F&A) rate applicable to this member organization\'s sub-award, expressed as a percentage.']
  member_role varchar [note: 'The functional role that this partner organization plays within the consortium (e.g., lead agency, co-implementer, technical advisor, sub-recipient, associate member, observer).']
  membership_end_date date [note: 'The date when this partner organization\'s membership in the consortium ended or is scheduled to end. Null for ongoing memberships.']
  membership_start_date date [note: 'The date when this partner organization officially joined the consortium and their membership became effective.']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this consortium membership (active, pending approval, suspended, withdrawn by member, terminated by consortium, inactive).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this consortium member record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this consortium member record was last modified or updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or contextual information about this consortium membership that does not fit into other structured fields.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Most recent performance rating assigned to this member organization based on their consortium deliverables, reporting quality, and compliance (excellent, satisfactory, needs improvement, unsatisfactory, not yet rated).']
  performance_review_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent performance review was conducted for this member organization.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which this member is required to submit progress and financial reports to the consortium lead (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, ad-hoc).']
  reporting_template_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or name of the standardized reporting template that this member must use for consortium reporting.']
  risk_mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Description of the risk mitigation measures and monitoring activities put in place for this member organization.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to this member organization based on capacity assessment, due diligence, and operational context (low, medium, high, critical).']
  signing_authority_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized representative from the member organization who signed the consortium agreement or MoU.']
  signing_authority_title varchar [note: 'Official title or position of the signing authority within the member organization (e.g., Executive Director, Country Director, Chief Executive Officer).']
  signing_date date [note: 'The date when the member organization\'s authorized representative signed the consortium agreement or MoU.']
  steering_committee_representative varchar [note: 'Name of the individual representing this member organization on the consortium steering committee or governance body.']
  sub_award_agreement_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or reference number of the sub-award agreement or sub-grant issued to this member organization.']
  sub_award_type varchar [note: 'The type of legal instrument governing this member\'s participation (sub-grant, sub-contract, MoU - Memorandum of Understanding, LoA - Letter of Agreement, SoW - Scope of Work).']
  technical_working_group_participation varchar [note: 'List or description of technical working groups, IASC clusters, or OCHA coordination mechanisms in which this member actively participates on behalf of the consortium.']
  thematic_responsibility varchar [note: 'The thematic area, sector, or technical domain where this member has primary responsibility (e.g., WASH, health, protection, education, nutrition).']
  voting_rights boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this member organization has voting rights in consortium governance decisions and steering committee meetings.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this consortium member record.']

  Note: 'Association record linking partner organizations to consortia, capturing each member\'s role (lead, co-implementer, technical advisor), contribution type, funding allocation percentage, geographic responsibility, and membership status. Tracks the full composition and role distribution within each consortium arrangement.'
}

Table partnership.coordination_meeting {
  coordination_meeting_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each inter-agency or cluster coordination meeting record. Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  crisis_communication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to communication.crisis_communication. Business justification: Coordination meetings during emergencies feed directly into crisis communication protocols, sitreps, and stakeholder updates. Ensures meeting decisions, action items, and inter-agency agreements are r']
  ngo_lead_representative_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Coordination meetings require tracking which NGO staff member represented the organization at inter-agency/cluster coordination forums. Essential for accountability, follow-up action assignment, and h']
  system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Virtual coordination meetings hosted on specific platforms (Zoom, Teams, WebEx). Business need to track platform for license cost allocation, capacity planning, technical support, security compliance ']
  volunteer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to volunteer.volunteer. Business justification: Volunteers often represent the NGO in cluster/sector coordination meetings, especially volunteer coordinators or technical specialists. Essential for tracking volunteer participation in coordination m']
  action_items_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of agreed action items arising from the meeting, including responsible parties and target completion dates where recorded. Supports follow-up tracking and accountability.']
  agenda_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path reference to the pre-circulated meeting agenda document. Supports pre-meeting preparation and post-meeting institutional memory.']
  agenda_summary varchar [note: 'Structured summary of the meeting agenda items as circulated prior to or during the meeting. Captures the key topics discussed for institutional memory and follow-up tracking.']
  associated_response_plan varchar [note: 'Name or code of the Humanitarian Response Plan (HRP), Flash Appeal, or other inter-agency response framework that this meeting is associated with. Contextualizes the meeting within the broader humanitarian response architecture.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or locality where the coordination meeting took place. Supports geographic analysis of coordination activity and field presence.']
  cluster_sector varchar [note: 'The OCHA humanitarian cluster or sector to which this meeting belongs (e.g., WASH, Health, Nutrition, Shelter, Protection, Food Security, Education, Logistics, Emergency Telecommunications). Null for bilateral or non-cluster meetings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wash|health|nutrition|shelter|protection|food_security|education|logistics|emergency_telecom|camp_coordination|early_recovery — promote to reference product]']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level of the meeting record and its contents. Determines access controls and sharing permissions for meeting minutes, decisions, and attendee lists.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country where the meeting was held. Enables geographic filtering and country-level coordination reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this coordination meeting record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the coordination meeting in minutes. Supports analysis of coordination effort and time investment across clusters and working groups.']
  end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp marking the scheduled or actual end of the coordination meeting. Used to calculate meeting duration and assess time investment in coordination activities.']
  follow_up_status varchar [note: 'Current status of post-meeting follow-up actions. Tracks whether action items from the meeting have been addressed, are in progress, or are overdue.']
  hosting_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that hosted or convened the coordination meeting. May be Ngo itself, a UN agency, government body, or another INGO acting as cluster lead.']
  humanitarian_context varchar [note: 'Operational context in which the coordination meeting takes place. Distinguishes acute emergency response from protracted crisis, recovery, development, or preparedness phases.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this coordination meeting is part of a recurring series (e.g., monthly cluster meetings, weekly working group calls). Supports scheduling and series-level analysis.']
  key_decisions varchar [note: 'Narrative record of the principal decisions made during the coordination meeting. Essential for institutional memory, accountability, and follow-up action tracking per CHS and HAP standards.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or 639-2 language code for the primary language in which the meeting was conducted (e.g., \'en\', \'fr\', \'ar\'). Supports multilingual coordination environments.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this coordination meeting record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data freshness monitoring.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the meeting was conducted in-person, virtually (e.g., via video conferencing), or in a hybrid format combining both modalities.']
  meeting_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the coordination meeting took place or is scheduled to take place. Principal real-world event date for this transaction.']
  meeting_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to the meeting for cross-system tracking and institutional memory. Used in SitReps, donor reports, and OCHA coordination logs.']
  meeting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the coordination meeting record. Tracks whether the meeting is planned, has occurred, was cancelled, or is in draft preparation.']
  meeting_title varchar [note: 'Official title or name of the coordination meeting as recorded in the meeting notice or invitation. Provides human-readable identification for institutional memory and reporting.']
  meeting_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the coordination meeting by its inter-agency structure. Cluster meetings align with OCHA cluster system; working groups are thematic; bilateral meetings are between two organizations; consortium meetings involve prime and sub-award partners. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cluster|working_group|bilateral|consortium|task_force|inter_agency|steering_committee|donor_review — promote to reference product]']
  minutes_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path reference to the official meeting minutes document stored in the document management system. Enables direct access to the full minutes for institutional memory.']
  minutes_recorded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal meeting minutes were recorded and are available for this coordination meeting. Supports institutional memory and accountability requirements.']
  next_meeting_date date [note: 'Scheduled date of the next meeting in the series or the follow-up meeting agreed upon at this meeting. Supports coordination planning and scheduling.']
  ngo_attendees_count varchar [note: 'Number of Ngo staff members who attended the coordination meeting. Supports workforce engagement analysis and coordination effort reporting.']
  ngo_role varchar [note: 'The role played by Ngo in this coordination meeting. Distinguishes whether Ngo hosted, co-hosted, presented, or attended as a participant or observer. Critical for accountability and stakeholder engagement reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: host|co_host|attendee|presenter|observer|cluster_lead|co_lead — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contextual notes, observations, or supplementary information about the coordination meeting not captured in structured fields.']
  organizations_represented varchar [note: 'Number of distinct organizations represented at the coordination meeting. Indicates breadth of inter-agency engagement and cluster participation.']
  recurrence_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this recurring coordination meeting series convenes. Null for one-off or ad hoc meetings. Used for scheduling and coordination cadence analysis.']
  related_sitrep_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or identifier of the OCHA Situation Report (SitRep) that this meeting contributed to or is referenced in. Links coordination activity to humanitarian situation reporting.']
  sdg_alignment varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of UN Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) numbers that the meeting agenda topics align with (e.g., \'3|6|16\'). Supports SDG-aligned reporting to donors and governing bodies.']
  series_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference code linking this meeting instance to its parent recurring series. Enables series-level aggregation and trend analysis across multiple meeting instances.']
  start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp marking the scheduled or actual start of the coordination meeting, including timezone offset. Used for scheduling, conflict detection, and attendance tracking.']
  total_attendees varchar [note: 'Total number of individuals who attended the coordination meeting. Used for participation analytics, cluster engagement metrics, and donor reporting on coordination activities.']
  venue_name varchar [note: 'Name of the physical venue or facility where the in-person or hybrid meeting was held (e.g., OCHA compound, NGO coordination hub, government ministry). Null for fully virtual meetings.']
  virtual_platform varchar [note: 'The digital conferencing platform used for virtual or hybrid meetings (e.g., Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Webex). Null for fully in-person meetings.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for inter-agency and cluster coordination meetings attended or hosted by Ngo. Captures meeting type (cluster, working group, bilateral, consortium), date, location, agenda items, attendees, key decisions, action items, and follow-up status. Supports accountability and institutional memory for stakeholder engagement.'
}

  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the report submitted by the partner. Narrative reports cover programmatic progress; financial reports cover expenditure against budget (BvA); SitRep (Situation Report) covers operational context; PDM (Post-Distribution Monitoring) covers distribution outcomes; combined covers both narrative and financial. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: narrative|financial|situation_report|post_distribution_monitoring|combined|other — promote to reference product]']
  report_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the report submission, incremented each time the partner resubmits a revised version following feedback or a revision request. Version 1 is the initial submission.']

  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the period covered by this report submission, as defined in the sub-award or agreement reporting schedule.']

  review_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal review of the submitted report was initiated by the grants management team.']

  revision_deadline date [note: 'The deadline by which the implementing partner must resubmit a revised report following a revision request or feedback issuance. Null if no revision has been requested.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the partner submitted this report to the organization. Used to calculate timeliness and compliance with reporting obligations.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The channel or mechanism through which the partner submitted the report, such as email, online portal, GMS (Grant Management System) upload, hard copy, or KoboToolbox.']

  target_beneficiaries varchar [note: 'The planned number of beneficiaries the partner was expected to reach during the reporting period as defined in the sub-award agreement or LogFrame targets.']
  total_expenditure_reported decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total financial expenditure reported by the implementing partner for the reporting period, as stated in the financial report. Used for Budget versus Actual (BvA) analysis and donor financial reporting.']

  Note: 'Tracks all programmatic and financial reports submitted by implementing partners under their sub-award or agreement obligations. Table partnership.capacity_building_plan {
  capacity_building_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the capacity building plan record. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — this product represents a structured, time-bound plan (agreement-like container) governing capacity-building interventions for a partner organization.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grant or sub-award under which this capacity building plan is funded and implemented. Links to the grant master record in the grant domain.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Capacity building plans require formal budget allocation for training, technical assistance, and organizational development activities. Links plan to approved budget for expenditure tracking, variance']
  capacity_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal capacity assessment record that identified the gaps and informed the design of this capacity building plan. Ensures traceability from assessment findings to planned interventions.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner organization (Civil Society Organization, Community-Based Organization, or local INGO) for whom this capacity building plan has been developed. Links to the partner organization master record in the partnership domain.']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Ngo staff member (e.g., Partnership Officer, Capacity Building Advisor) who is the primary owner and accountable lead for this plan\'s implementation.']
  training_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safeguarding.training_program. Business justification: Capacity building plans specify safeguarding training programs to address partner capacity gaps identified in assessments. Links planned interventions to standardized training curriculum for implement']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this capacity building plan was formally approved by the designated authority at Ngo, authorizing commencement of activities and budget expenditure.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the Ngo staff member or management authority who formally approved this capacity building plan for implementation. Supports governance and accountability documentation.']
  baseline_capacity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the partner organization\'s overall organizational capacity at the time of the baseline assessment, prior to plan implementation. Typically scored on a standardized scale (e.g., 1–5 or 0–100) per the assessment tool used.']
  capacity_score_scale varchar [note: 'The scoring scale used for baseline, target, and current capacity scores in this plan (e.g., \'1_to_5\', \'0_to_100\'). Ensures consistent interpretation of scores across different assessment tools and reporting periods.']
  chs_standard_aligned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity building plan is designed in alignment with the Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) on Quality and Accountability, ensuring the partner is supported to meet CHS commitments.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the partner organization operates and where this capacity building plan is being implemented (e.g., \'SOM\', \'SDN\', \'ETH\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this capacity building plan record was first created in the system. Corresponds to the MASTER_AGREEMENT RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local currency in which the plan budget is also tracked (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'KES\'). Supports multi-currency financial reporting for field operations.']
  current_capacity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Most recently recorded organizational capacity score for the partner, updated following periodic reassessments or milestone reviews during plan implementation. Enables tracking of progress toward the target capacity score.']
  donor_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether progress on this capacity building plan must be reported to the donor as part of grant compliance obligations. True = donor reporting required; False = internal plan only.']
  end_date date [note: 'The planned or actual date on which the capacity building plan concludes and all activities are expected to be completed. Nullable for open-ended plans. Corresponds to the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  expenditure_to_date_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual expenditure incurred against this capacity building plan to date, expressed in US Dollars (USD). Used for Budget versus Actual (BvA) tracking and financial stewardship reporting.']
  gender_mainstreaming_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether gender mainstreaming and Gender-Based Violence (GBV) prevention components are explicitly included in this capacity building plan, in line with donor gender policies and SDG (Sustainable Development Goal) 5 commitments.']
  grand_bargain_commitment_ref varchar [note: 'Reference code or description of the specific Grand Bargain commitment (e.g., Workstream 2 — Localization) that this capacity building plan contributes to. Supports donor accountability and IATI transparency reporting.']
  implementation_region varchar [note: 'Sub-national region, state, or province where the partner organization and this capacity building plan are primarily based. Supports geographic disaggregation in MEL dashboards and GIS (Geographic Information System) reporting.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review or progress assessment of this capacity building plan, during which activity completion, capacity scores, and budget utilization were evaluated.']
  localization_strategy_aligned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity building plan is formally aligned with Ngo\'s localization strategy and Grand Bargain commitments to strengthen local and national actors. True = aligned and documented; False = not formally aligned.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the next formal review of this capacity building plan is scheduled. Drives workflow reminders and ensures timely monitoring of plan progress.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contextual notes, observations, or qualitative commentary on the capacity building plan, including lessons learned, contextual challenges, or coordination notes with OCHA cluster leads.']
  overall_objective varchar [note: 'High-level statement of the primary objective of this capacity building plan, describing the intended organizational capability improvement to be achieved by the plan\'s end date.']
  overall_progress_status varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the overall implementation progress of the capacity building plan relative to the planned timeline and milestones. Distinct from plan_status (lifecycle state); this reflects delivery performance.']
  partner_focal_point_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the designated focal point or primary contact person at the partner organization who is responsible for coordinating and participating in capacity building activities under this plan.']
  partner_focal_point_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the partner organization\'s designated focal point for this capacity building plan (e.g., \'Executive Director\', \'Finance Manager\', \'MEL Officer\').']
  plan_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this capacity building plan, used in donor reports, partner correspondence, and IATI disclosures. Format: CBP-[PartnerCode]-[Year]-[Sequence].']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capacity building plan. \'draft\' = under development; \'active\' = approved and in implementation; \'on_hold\' = temporarily suspended; \'completed\' = all activities delivered and closed; \'cancelled\' = plan discontinued.']
  plan_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title of the capacity building plan, typically referencing the partner organization name and the primary functional area targeted (e.g., \'Financial Management Strengthening Plan — Partner XYZ 2024\').']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary focus area of the capacity building plan. \'organizational\' = overall organizational development; \'technical\' = sector-specific technical skills; \'financial\' = financial management and accountability; \'governance\' = board and leadership strengthening; \'mel\' = Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL) systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organizational|technical|financial|governance|mel|procurement|hr|communications|safeguarding — promote to reference product]']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which progress updates on this capacity building plan must be reported internally or to the donor. Drives scheduling of review meetings and SitRep (Situation Report) contributions.']
  safeguarding_component_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity building plan includes a dedicated safeguarding (Protection from Sexual Exploitation and Abuse — PSEA) component. Required for compliance with donor safeguarding policies and CHS commitments.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the capacity building plan officially commences and activities are scheduled to begin. Corresponds to the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.']
  target_capacity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the desired organizational capacity level to be achieved by the partner organization upon successful completion of this plan. Used to measure progress and plan effectiveness.']
  targeted_functional_areas varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of organizational functional areas targeted by this plan for strengthening, as identified in the capacity assessment. Common values include: financial management, human resources, procurement, governance, MEL (Monitoring Evaluation and Learning), safeguarding, communications, ICT4D (Information and Communication Technology for Development).']
  theory_of_change_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the Theory of Change (ToC) underpinning this capacity building plan, describing the causal pathway from planned interventions to expected organizational capability improvements and ultimate program outcomes.']
  total_activities_completed varchar [note: 'Count of capacity building activity line-items that have been fully delivered and marked as completed under this plan. Used to calculate overall plan completion rate for MEL dashboards and donor reporting.']
  total_activities_planned varchar [note: 'Total number of capacity building activity line-items (trainings, mentoring sessions, technical assistance visits, peer learning events) planned under this plan. Used for progress tracking and workload planning.']
  total_budget_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget allocated for the implementation of this capacity building plan, expressed in US Dollars (USD). Covers all activity costs including training, technical assistance, materials, and facilitator fees.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this capacity building plan record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Master record with embedded activity line-items for structured capacity-building interventions developed for partner organizations based on capacity assessment findings. Plan-level data captures objectives, targeted functional areas (financial management, HR, procurement, governance, MEL), timeline, responsible staff, budget allocation, and overall progress status. Activity-level line-items capture individual trainings, mentoring sessions, technical assistance visits, and peer learning events with delivery dates, facilitators, participant lists, topics covered, pre/post assessment scores, and completion status. Supports localization strategies, partner strengthening, and Grand Bargain commitments. This is the SSOT for all capacity-building planning and delivery tracking in the partnership domain.'
}

  capacity_building_activity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each capacity-building activity record delivered to a partner organization. Business justification: Individual capacity building activities (workshops, training sessions) incur direct costs that must be charged to specific cost centers for activity-based costing and financial tracking. Required for ']
  impact_story_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to communication.impact_story. Business justification: Capacity building outcomes (trained staff, improved systems, organizational transformation) are compelling impact narratives for donors and advocates. Links stories to specific capacity interventions ']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the program or project under which this capacity-building activity is funded and implemented. Enables program-level aggregation of partner strengthening efforts.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner organization (CSO, CBO, INGO, government body, UN agency, or consortium member) receiving this capacity-building activity. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category.']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal staff member or consultant who facilitated or led this capacity-building activity. Used for workload tracking and quality assurance reviews.']
  system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Capacity building training delivered via LMS, webinar platforms, or e-learning systems. Tracking platform enables participant access management, completion tracking, certificate generation, technical ']
  volunteer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to volunteer.volunteer. Business justification: Experienced volunteers frequently serve as trainers, facilitators, or peer mentors in partner capacity building activities. This is a core localization pattern where volunteer expertise builds partner']
  activity_cost_usd decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total direct cost incurred to deliver this capacity-building activity, expressed in US Dollars. Includes facilitator fees, venue, materials, and travel. Used for Budget versus Actual (BvA) tracking and cost-effectiveness analysis under OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.']
  activity_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this capacity-building activity, used in partner communications, donor reports, and IATI disclosures. Format: CBA-{COUNTRY}-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  activity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the capacity-building activity. Drives workflow management, MEL dashboard filtering, and completion reporting to donors. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  activity_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the capacity-building activity (e.g., \'Financial Management Training for Partner Finance Officers\', \'MEAL System Technical Assistance Visit\'). Used in reports and partner communications.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the modality or format of the capacity-building activity. Drives reporting disaggregation and MEL indicator tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: training|mentoring|technical_assistance|peer_learning|coaching|workshop|on_the_job_support|e_learning — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The real-world date on which the capacity-building activity was delivered or commenced. The principal business event date for this transaction. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The real-world date on which the capacity-building activity concluded. Used to calculate actual duration and compare against planned schedule.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to evaluate participant knowledge or competency before and after the activity. Informs the validity and comparability of pre/post assessment scores across activities.']
  capacity_domain varchar [note: 'High-level domain of organizational capacity being strengthened through this activity, aligned with standard capacity assessment frameworks. Used for portfolio-level gap analysis and Theory of Change (ToC) alignment.']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the accrediting or certifying body that issued the certificate for this activity (e.g., \'CHS Alliance\', \'SPHERE Association\', \'USAID\', \'Internal\'). Populated only when is_certified is True.']
  completion_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of registered participants who completed the full capacity-building activity (attended all sessions or met minimum attendance threshold). Key MEL indicator for activity quality and partner engagement.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the location where the capacity-building activity was delivered. Enables geographic disaggregation in MEL dashboards and OCHA cluster reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity-building activity record was first created in the system. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Mode through which the capacity-building activity was delivered to participants. Distinguishes face-to-face field visits from remote or blended approaches, relevant for cost analysis and ICT4D reporting.']
  duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of contact hours delivered during this capacity-building activity. Used for MEL indicator reporting (e.g., total training hours delivered to partners) and cost-per-hour analysis.']
  facilitator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead facilitator or trainer who delivered this capacity-building activity. Captured for quality assurance, follow-up, and facilitator performance tracking. Classified confidential as it identifies a specific individual in a professional context.']
  facilitator_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity or individual who facilitated the capacity-building activity. Distinguishes internal staff-led sessions from externally contracted consultants or peer-learning facilitated by other NGOs or UN agencies.']
  feedback_collected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether participant feedback or a post-distribution monitoring (PDM) equivalent survey was collected for this capacity-building activity. Supports accountability and learning (MEAL) requirements.']
  female_participants varchar [note: 'Number of female participants in this capacity-building activity. Required for gender disaggregation reporting per USAID Gender Equality Policy, DFID guidelines, and SDG 5 indicators.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the follow-up action identified after this capacity-building activity should be completed. Populated only when follow_up_required is True.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up visit, mentoring session, or technical assistance is required after this activity to reinforce learning or address outstanding gaps. Drives scheduling of subsequent capacity-building activities.']
  is_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a certificate of completion or competency was issued to participants upon successful completion of this capacity-building activity. Relevant for accredited training programs and professional development tracking.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity-building activity record was most recently modified. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and incremental ETL processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  linked_capacity_gap varchar [note: 'Description or reference code of the specific capacity gap identified in the partner\'s capacity assessment that this activity is designed to address. Enables traceability from gap identification to intervention delivery.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific city, district, or venue where the capacity-building activity took place (e.g., \'Nairobi Regional Office\', \'Kampala Training Centre\'). Complements country code for field-level geographic reporting.']
  male_participants varchar [note: 'Number of male participants in this capacity-building activity. Required for gender disaggregation reporting per USAID Gender Equality Policy and SDG 5 indicators.']
  materials_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether training materials, toolkits, manuals, or job aids were distributed to participants as part of this capacity-building activity. Relevant for resource tracking and follow-up support planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing qualitative observations, lessons learned, contextual factors, or any other relevant information about the capacity-building activity not captured in structured fields. Supports MEL narrative reporting.']
  participant_satisfaction_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Average satisfaction rating provided by participants in post-activity feedback forms, typically on a scale of 1–5 or 1–10. Used for activity quality improvement and facilitator performance assessment.']
  post_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average knowledge or competency assessment score recorded for participants after the capacity-building activity concluded. Compared against pre-assessment score to measure learning gain and activity effectiveness.']
  pre_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average knowledge or competency assessment score recorded for participants before the capacity-building activity commenced. Used to establish a baseline for measuring learning gain. Expressed as a percentage (0–100). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category (non-monetary).']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the capacity-building activity was originally scheduled to conclude. Used for duration planning and workplan management.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the capacity-building activity was originally scheduled to begin. Used for planning, calendar management, and variance analysis against actual delivery.']
  thematic_area varchar [note: 'Primary thematic or sectoral focus of the capacity-building activity (e.g., \'Financial Management\', \'MEAL\', \'WASH\', \'GBV Prevention\', \'Procurement\', \'Safeguarding\', \'Proposal Writing\'). Used for portfolio analysis and gap identification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: financial_management|meal|wash|gbv_prevention|procurement|safeguarding|proposal_writing|hr_management|logistics|governance — promote to reference product]']
  topics_covered varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of the specific topics, modules, or competencies addressed during the activity (e.g., \'Budget vs Actual reporting, SAP navigation, donor compliance rules\'). Supports curriculum documentation and knowledge management.']
  total_participants varchar [note: 'Total number of individuals from the partner organization who participated in this capacity-building activity. Core MEL indicator for reach and coverage reporting to donors.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for individual capacity-building activities delivered to partner organizations including trainings, mentoring sessions, technical assistance visits, and peer learning events. Captures activity type, delivery date, facilitator, participants, topics covered, pre/post assessment scores, and completion status. Tracks progress against the capacity-building plan.'
}

Table partnership.partner_risk_register {
  partner_risk_register_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each risk entry in the partner risk register. Primary key for this data product.']
  due_diligence_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.due_diligence_record. Business justification: partner_risk_register stores capacity_assessment_score and capacity_assessment_date but has no FK to due_diligence_record. Risks are identified through due diligence processes. Adding due_diligence_re']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner organization for which this risk entry is recorded. Links to the partner master record.']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member record of the designated risk owner responsible for monitoring and managing this risk. Enables automated escalation workflows and accountability tracking.']
  subaward_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific sub-award agreement under which this risk is being assessed, if applicable. Links to the sub-award management record.']
  composite_risk_score varchar [note: 'Calculated product of likelihood_rating multiplied by impact_rating (range 1–25), yielding the overall risk severity score used to prioritise mitigation actions and sub-award oversight decisions. Stored as a business-recorded value from the risk assessment process, not a real-time derived metric.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk register entry was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker required for data governance and compliance reporting.']
  donor_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the risk event or materialisation requires formal notification to the donor per grant agreement terms (True). Relevant for USAID, DFID/FCDO, CERF, and other institutional donors with mandatory incident reporting clauses.']
  donor_notified_date date [note: 'Date on which the donor was formally notified of this risk or incident. Null if notification has not yet occurred or is not required. Used for compliance tracking against donor agreement notification timelines.']
  due_diligence_level varchar [note: 'Level of due diligence applied to this partner in relation to this risk: standard (routine checks), enhanced (additional scrutiny for high-risk partners), simplified (low-risk, minimal checks). Aligns with donor-mandated partner vetting requirements.']
  escalated_to varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual, committee, or external body to whom this risk was escalated (e.g., \'Country Director\', \'Board Risk Committee\', \'USAID Agreement Officer\'). Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which this risk was formally escalated to senior management, the board, or the donor. Null if escalation has not occurred. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  escalation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk has been flagged for escalation to senior management, the board, or the donor (True). Set automatically when risk_level is \'critical\' or when safeguarding_risk_flag is True.']
  fiduciary_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk entry has been classified as a fiduciary risk (True) involving financial management, fund accountability, or procurement integrity concerns. Triggers enhanced financial monitoring protocols.']
  financial_exposure_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated maximum financial exposure in US Dollars associated with this risk, representing the value of funds at risk if the risk materialises (e.g., sub-award value at risk of misappropriation). Used in fiduciary risk prioritisation and BvA oversight.']
  impact_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric score (1–5) representing the severity of consequences if the risk materialises: 1=Negligible, 2=Minor, 3=Moderate, 4=Major, 5=Catastrophic. Assessed across financial, programmatic, reputational, and safeguarding dimensions.']
  inherent_impact_rating varchar [note: 'Impact rating (1–5) assessed BEFORE any mitigation controls are applied. Together with inherent_likelihood_rating, establishes the gross risk score prior to control application.']
  inherent_likelihood_rating varchar [note: 'Likelihood rating (1–5) assessed BEFORE any mitigation controls are applied. Captures the gross/inherent risk level to benchmark the effectiveness of mitigation measures.']
