  last_review_date date [note: 'The date on which this risk entry was most recently reviewed and updated by the risk owner or oversight team. Used to flag stale entries requiring re-assessment.']
  likelihood_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric score (1–5) representing the probability that the risk will materialise: 1=Rare, 2=Unlikely, 3=Possible, 4=Likely, 5=Almost Certain. Used in composite risk scoring per standard NGO risk matrices.']
  mitigation_measures varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific controls, actions, and safeguards implemented or planned to reduce the likelihood or impact of the risk. May include capacity-building, enhanced financial monitoring, spot-checks, or escalation protocols.']
  mitigation_status varchar [note: 'Current implementation status of the mitigation measures: planned (not yet started), in_progress (underway), completed (fully implemented), overdue (past target date without completion).']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this risk entry is actively monitored and reviewed: weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi_annual, annual, or ad_hoc. Drives scheduling of oversight activities and MEL review cycles.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this risk entry. Drives automated reminders and compliance with periodic risk review obligations under donor agreements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, observations, or supplementary information related to this risk entry that does not fit into structured fields. May include references to related incident reports, audit findings, or SitRep entries.']
  oversight_mechanism varchar [note: 'Description of the specific oversight mechanism applied to manage this risk (e.g., \'Monthly financial spot-checks\', \'Quarterly field monitoring visits\', \'Third-party audit\', \'PDM surveys\'). Documents the control environment for donor compliance.']
  risk_appetite_alignment varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether this risk falls within the organisation\'s defined risk appetite: within_appetite (acceptable), at_tolerance (borderline, requires monitoring), exceeds_appetite (unacceptable, requires immediate action or sub-award suspension).']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the risk type: fiduciary (financial mismanagement, fraud), programmatic (delivery failure, quality gaps), reputational (media, political exposure), safeguarding (protection of beneficiaries and staff), or compliance (regulatory, donor-condition breaches).']
  risk_closure_date date [note: 'The date on which this risk was formally closed, either because it was fully mitigated, the sub-award ended, or the risk was deemed no longer applicable. Null if the risk remains open.']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the nature of the risk, its potential causes, and the context in which it may materialise. Supports due diligence documentation and donor compliance reporting.']
  risk_identification_date date [note: 'The date on which this risk was first formally identified and entered into the risk register. Marks the start of the risk lifecycle and is used for ageing analysis.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk band derived from the composite risk score: low (1–4), medium (5–9), high (10–16), critical (17–25). Used for traffic-light reporting in MEL dashboards, donor SitReps, and sub-award oversight decisions.']
  risk_materialisation_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk was confirmed to have materialised into an actual incident or adverse event. Null if the risk has not materialised. Used for incident timeline analysis and donor reporting.']
  risk_materialised boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the identified risk has actually materialised into an incident or adverse event (True). When True, triggers incident management workflows and may require sub-award suspension or termination review.']
  risk_owner varchar [note: 'Name or role title of the individual within the NGO responsible for monitoring and managing this risk entry. Typically a Program Manager, Grants Manager, or Country Director.']
  risk_register_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to this risk entry for cross-system reference and reporting (e.g., RR-FISC-2024-0012). Used in donor reports, audit trails, and compliance filings.']
  risk_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the risk entry: open (identified, not yet addressed), under_review (being assessed), mitigated (controls applied), escalated (elevated to senior management or donor), closed (resolved or no longer applicable).']
  risk_subcategory varchar [note: 'Granular classification within the risk category (e.g., \'Procurement Fraud\' under fiduciary, \'GBV Incident\' under safeguarding, \'FCPA Violation\' under compliance). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: procurement_fraud|financial_misreporting|conflict_of_interest|gbv_incident|child_protection|data_breach|political_interference|media_exposure|delivery_failure|quality_failure|regulatory_breach|donor_condition_breach — promote to reference product]']
  risk_title varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable title summarising the risk (e.g., \'Inadequate financial controls at field office level\'). Used as the primary label in risk dashboards and SitRep summaries.']
  safeguarding_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk entry involves safeguarding concerns (True), including GBV, child protection, or protection of beneficiaries and staff from exploitation and abuse. Triggers mandatory escalation per CHS safeguarding protocols.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk register entry was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Master record maintaining the risk register for each partner organization covering fiduciary risk, programmatic risk, reputational risk, safeguarding risk, and compliance risk. Captures risk category, risk description, likelihood rating, impact rating, composite risk score, mitigation measures, risk owner, and review date. Feeds into sub-award management and oversight decisions.'
}

Table partnership.mou_obligation {
  mou_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each obligation, deliverable, or commitment record defined within an MoU or partnership agreement. Primary key for this entity.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: MOU obligations frequently derive from specific award compliance requirements, donor special conditions, and regulatory flow-down clauses. Tracking which award drives each obligation is essential for ']
  field_monitoring_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.field_monitoring_visit. Business justification: mou_obligation has verified_date and verified_by_name but no link to the field_monitoring_visit where verification occurred. Obligations are often verified during field monitoring visits. Adding field']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.intervention. Business justification: MOU obligations (deliverables, milestones, reporting requirements) are tied to specific program interventions for performance monitoring, results verification, and intervention-level obligation tracki']
  partnership_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent MoU, LoA, SoW, or sub-award agreement under which this obligation is defined. Links the obligation to its governing partnership instrument.']
  achieved_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantitative result achieved against the target value at the time of completion or latest reporting. Enables performance tracking and BvA (Budget versus Actual) style analysis for non-financial obligations.']
  amendment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or description of the partnership agreement amendment that introduced or modified this obligation. Null for obligations defined in the original agreement. Supports change management and audit trail.']
  capacity_building_component boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this obligation includes a capacity building or institutional strengthening component for the partner organization (e.g., training, mentoring, systems development). Supports partner capacity assessment tracking.']
  cluster_lead_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the OCHA cluster lead agency (e.g., UNICEF for WASH, WFP for Food Security, UNHCR for Shelter) under whose coordination framework this obligation falls. Relevant for obligations arising from cluster coordination commitments.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the obligation was fulfilled and accepted by the receiving party. Null if the obligation has not yet been completed. Used to calculate timeliness and compliance metrics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this obligation record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with record-keeping requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the financial_value_amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required when financial_value_amount is populated.']
  days_overdue varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days this obligation is past its due_date without completion, as of the last system refresh. Populated only when obligation_status is \'overdue\'. Used for escalation prioritization and partner performance scoring.']
  donor_reference_number varchar [note: 'The donor\'s own reference number or grant award number associated with this obligation, where the obligation is tied to a specific donor-funded grant. Enables traceability from obligation to donor grant for compliance reporting.']
  due_date date [note: 'The agreed deadline by which the obligation must be fulfilled, as specified in the partnership instrument. Drives automated reminders, escalation triggers, and compliance monitoring.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which this obligation was formally escalated to senior management, the donor, or a dispute resolution mechanism due to non-fulfillment or contested completion.']
  escalation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the reason for escalation, actions taken, and resolution steps. Captures the escalation history for audit and learning purposes.']
  escalation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation has been escalated due to non-compliance or dispute: none (no escalation), flagged (internally noted as at-risk), escalated (formally raised to senior management or donor), or resolved (escalation closed after remediation).']
  evidence_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the evidence submitted to demonstrate fulfillment of this obligation (e.g., \'Quarterly narrative report submitted via email on 2024-03-31\', \'Training attendance register and photos uploaded to SharePoint\'). Supports audit and compliance review.']
  evidence_document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, URL, or unique document identifier pointing to the supporting evidence artifact (report, photo, attendance list, financial statement) that demonstrates fulfillment of this obligation.']
  financial_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value associated with this obligation where it involves a financial commitment (e.g., sub-grant tranche disbursement, in-kind contribution valuation, or cost-sharing commitment). Null for non-financial obligations.']
  geographic_location varchar [note: 'Sub-national geographic scope of the obligation (e.g., region, district, camp, or settlement name) where the deliverable is to be executed. Supports GIS-based field operations monitoring.']
  indicator_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific KPI or MEL indicator that this obligation contributes to, as defined in the partnership\'s MEAL framework or donor reporting requirements. Supports DHIS2 indicator tracking and MEL dashboard reporting.']
  is_critical_obligation boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this obligation is classified as critical to the partnership\'s core deliverables or donor compliance requirements. Critical obligations trigger priority escalation workflows and are highlighted in SitRep and donor reporting.']
  is_donor_reportable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether fulfillment of this obligation must be reported to the donor (e.g., USAID BHA, DFID, CERF). Drives inclusion in donor compliance reports and IATI activity updates.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this obligation record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  logframe_output_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or label linking this obligation to a specific output, outcome, or activity in the partnership\'s Logical Framework (LogFrame) or Theory of Change (ToC). Enables Results-Based Management (RBM) tracking and MEL alignment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, clarifications, or internal commentary related to this obligation that does not fit into structured fields. Used by partnership managers for operational notes and follow-up actions.']
  obligation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the obligation, deliverable, or commitment as defined in the partnership instrument. Captures the full scope, quality standards, and acceptance criteria agreed upon by both parties.']
  obligation_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code or clause reference identifying this obligation within the partnership instrument (e.g., \'MOU-2024-001-OBL-03\', or clause reference \'Section 4.2.1\'). Used in partner communications and compliance reporting.']
  obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the obligation: pending (not yet started), in_progress (work underway), completed (fulfilled and verified), overdue (past due date without completion), waived (formally excused by mutual agreement), or disputed (fulfillment contested by either party).']
  obligation_title varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable title summarizing the obligation or deliverable (e.g., \'Quarterly Program Progress Report\', \'WASH Training for 50 Community Health Workers\'). Used in dashboards and partner communications.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the obligation by its nature: deliverable (tangible output), reporting (narrative or data submission), financial (fund transfer or accounting), capacity_building (training or institutional strengthening), coordination (attendance at cluster/working group meetings), or compliance (regulatory or donor requirement). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: deliverable|reporting|financial|capacity_building|coordination|compliance — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  operational_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the country where this obligation is to be fulfilled. Enables geographic filtering and country-level compliance reporting.']
  program_sector varchar [note: 'The humanitarian or development sector to which this obligation relates (e.g., WASH, GBV, Health, Education, Food Security, Shelter, PSS, Nutrition). Aligns with OCHA cluster system and IATI sector codes for cross-program reporting.']
  recurrence_frequency varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation is a one-time commitment or a recurring one, and if recurring, the frequency: monthly, quarterly, semi_annual, annual, or ad_hoc. Drives automated scheduling of future obligation instances.']
  reminder_days_before_due varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before the due_date that an automated reminder notification should be triggered to the responsible focal point and partnership manager. Configurable per obligation based on complexity and risk.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the reporting or performance period to which this obligation applies. Together with reporting_period_start_date, defines the window for periodic compliance obligations.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the reporting or performance period to which this obligation applies. Used for periodic obligations (e.g., quarterly reports, monthly financial submissions) to align with donor reporting cycles.']
  responsible_focal_point_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual designated as the primary focal point accountable for fulfilling this obligation on behalf of the responsible party. Used for escalation and follow-up communications.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Indicates which party bears primary responsibility for fulfilling this obligation: lead_organization (the NGO itself), partner_organization (the CSO, CBO, INGO, or government counterpart), both_parties (joint obligation), or third_party (a designated sub-contractor or consortium member).']
  target_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the target value (e.g., \'beneficiaries\', \'households\', \'training sessions\', \'metric tons\', \'reports\'). Required when target_value is populated to give the quantity business meaning.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantitative target associated with this obligation (e.g., number of beneficiaries to be reached, number of training sessions to be conducted, quantity of NFIs to be distributed). Null for qualitative obligations.']
  verified_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the staff member or authorized representative who verified and accepted the fulfillment evidence for this obligation on behalf of the receiving party.']
  verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the receiving party formally verified and accepted the evidence of fulfillment for this obligation. Distinct from completion_date, which is when the responsible party submitted the deliverable.']
  waiver_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of why this obligation was formally waived by mutual agreement of both parties. Required when obligation_status is \'waived\'. Documents the business rationale for audit and compliance purposes.']

  Note: 'Tracks specific obligations, deliverables, and commitments defined within MoUs and partnership agreements. Captures obligation description, responsible party, due date, completion status, evidence of fulfillment, and escalation status. Enables systematic monitoring of whether both parties are meeting their agreed commitments under each partnership instrument.'
}

Table partnership.partner_accreditation {
  partner_accreditation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each partner accreditation record. Primary key for the partner_accreditation data product.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner organization that holds this accreditation. Links to the partner_org master record.']
  accreditation_code varchar [note: 'Standardized short code or identifier for the accreditation scheme (e.g., \'CHS\', \'SPHERE\', \'HAP\', \'ISO9001\', \'ISO27001\', \'NGO-REG\'). Used for filtering, reporting, and cross-partner comparisons in MEL dashboards.']
  accreditation_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the accreditation, certification, or standard (e.g., \'Core Humanitarian Standard Certification\', \'ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management\', \'Sphere Humanitarian Standards Compliance\', \'HAP Membership\', \'National NGO Registration – Kenya\'). Human-readable label used in due diligence reports and partner dashboards.']
  accreditation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score or rating assigned by the accrediting body upon assessment, where applicable (e.g., CHS self-assessment score out of 100, ISO audit score). Null if the accreditation is pass/fail with no numeric score. Used in partner capacity assessment scoring and due diligence risk rating calculations.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accreditation record. \'active\' indicates the accreditation is valid and in force; \'expired\' indicates the validity period has lapsed; \'suspended\' indicates temporary withdrawal by the accrediting body; \'pending_renewal\' indicates renewal application submitted; \'withdrawn\' indicates permanent revocation; \'under_review\' indicates the accrediting body is conducting a review.']
  accreditation_type varchar [note: 'Broad category of the accreditation record. Distinguishes between formal certifications (e.g., CHS, ISO), membership bodies (e.g., HAP), national NGO registrations, compliance standards (e.g., Sphere), licenses, and awards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: certification|membership|registration|compliance_standard|license|award — promote to reference product if additional values emerge]']
  accrediting_body_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the accrediting body is domiciled or where the registration is issued (e.g., \'CHE\' for Switzerland-based CHS Alliance, \'GBR\' for UK Charity Commission). Supports geographic compliance analysis.']
  accrediting_body_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the organization or authority that issued the accreditation or certification (e.g., \'CHS Alliance\', \'Sphere Association\', \'Bureau Veritas\', \'Kenya NGO Coordination Board\', \'ISO Certification Body\'). Critical for verification and audit trail purposes.']
  applicable_standard_version varchar [note: 'Version or edition of the standard or framework against which the accreditation was assessed (e.g., \'CHS 2018\', \'Sphere 2018\', \'ISO 9001:2015\'). Important for tracking compliance against evolving standards and identifying partners certified to outdated versions.']
  assessment_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the rigor and methodology of the accreditation assessment. \'full\' denotes a comprehensive third-party audit; \'partial\' denotes assessment of selected components; \'self_assessment\' denotes partner-reported compliance without external verification; \'third_party\' denotes independent external audit; \'peer_review\' denotes assessment by peer organizations. Informs the weight given to the accreditation in due diligence scoring.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official certificate or registration number issued by the accrediting body. Serves as the externally-known unique identifier for the accreditation document. Used for verification with the issuing body and referenced in due diligence reports and donor compliance submissions.']
  chs_commitment_level varchar [note: 'For CHS-specific accreditations, records the highest CHS commitment level achieved by the partner organization (Commitments 1–9 per the CHS framework). Null for non-CHS accreditations. Supports CHS-aligned partner quality benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commitment_1|commitment_2|commitment_3|commitment_4|commitment_5|commitment_6|commitment_7|commitment_8|commitment_9 — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this accreditation record was first created in the system. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or URL pointing to the scanned certificate or official accreditation document stored in Ngo\'s document repository. Enables auditors and compliance staff to retrieve the source document for verification.']
  document_upload_date date [note: 'The date on which the accreditation certificate document was uploaded to Ngo\'s document management system. Used to assess document currency and flag records where the uploaded document may be outdated relative to the most recent renewal. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  donor_reference varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the donor that mandates this accreditation as a grant or sub-award condition (e.g., \'USAID/BHA\', \'FCDO\', \'CERF\'). Populated only when donor_required is True. Supports grant compliance reporting and sub-award management.']
  donor_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specific accreditation is required by a donor as a condition of a grant or sub-award agreement. True if mandated by a donor (e.g., USAID, DFID/FCDO, CERF); False otherwise. Supports grant compliance tracking and sub-award due diligence.']
  due_diligence_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric weight assigned to this accreditation in Ngo\'s partner due diligence scoring model (e.g., 0.25 for 25% weight). Reflects the relative importance of this accreditation type in the overall partner risk rating calculation. Configured by the Partnership team in alignment with donor requirements.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the accreditation, certification, or registration expires and must be renewed. Null for accreditations with no fixed expiry. Used to trigger renewal workflows and flag partners with lapsed credentials in due diligence reviews. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  geographic_applicability varchar [note: 'Country or region where this accreditation is valid and recognized. May be a specific ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (e.g., \'KEN\', \'ETH\'), a regional designation, or \'GLOBAL\' for internationally recognized accreditations. Supports geographic due diligence and partner deployment decisions.']
  iati_activity_identifier varchar [note: 'IATI activity identifier associated with the partnership or program context in which this accreditation was assessed or required. Supports IATI-compliant transparency reporting and donor traceability.']
  is_mandatory_for_partnership boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this accreditation is a mandatory prerequisite for Ngo to enter into or maintain a partnership agreement with this organization. True if required by Ngo\'s partnership policy, donor conditions, or regulatory requirements; False if optional or informational. Drives partnership eligibility checks.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the accrediting body formally issued or granted the accreditation, certification, or registration. Marks the start of the accreditation\'s validity period. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  max_possible_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum score achievable under the accreditation assessment framework, used to contextualize the accreditation_score (e.g., 100 for a percentage-based CHS assessment). Null if the accreditation is pass/fail. Enables normalized cross-partner comparisons.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, conditions, limitations, or observations related to this accreditation record. May include details of any conditions attached to the accreditation, findings from the assessment, or follow-up actions required. Used by partnership officers during due diligence reviews.']
  renewal_alert_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the accreditation expiry_date at which an automated renewal alert should be triggered for the partnership management team. Configurable per accreditation type (e.g., 90 days for CHS certification, 30 days for national NGO registration). Supports proactive compliance management.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date on which the partner organization submitted or is scheduled to submit a renewal application for this accreditation. Distinct from expiry_date (when it lapses) and issue_date (when originally granted). Supports proactive renewal tracking and partner capacity management workflows.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the scope, coverage, or applicability of the accreditation. Specifies which programs, sectors, geographic areas, or operational activities the accreditation covers (e.g., \'WASH and shelter programs in East Africa\', \'Financial management systems only\'). Supports targeted due diligence assessments.']
  thematic_sector varchar [note: 'The humanitarian or development sector to which this accreditation primarily applies (e.g., \'WASH\', \'GBV\', \'Health\', \'Education\', \'Shelter\', \'Food Security\', \'PSS\'). Supports sector-specific partner capability mapping and program design. Uses common humanitarian sector taxonomy aligned with OCHA cluster system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this accreditation record was most recently modified. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit trail purposes.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which Ngo staff last verified the accreditation with the issuing body. Used to assess how current the verification is and to schedule re-verification cycles. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether Ngo staff have independently verified the accreditation with the issuing body. \'verified\' means the certificate has been confirmed as authentic and current; \'unverified\' means no verification has been performed; \'pending_verification\' means verification is in progress; \'verification_failed\' means the accrediting body could not confirm the certificate. Critical for due diligence quality assurance.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or staff identifier of the Ngo staff member who performed the most recent verification of this accreditation with the issuing body. Supports audit trail and accountability for due diligence processes.']

  Note: 'Tracks formal accreditations, certifications, and standards compliance held by partner organizations including CHS (Core Humanitarian Standard) certification, Sphere compliance, HAP membership, ISO certifications, and national NGO registration status. Captures accreditation body, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, and verification status. Supports due diligence and quality assurance.'
}

Table partnership.field_monitoring_visit {
  field_monitoring_visit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each field monitoring and supportive supervision visit record. Primary key for the field_monitoring_visit data product.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: Field monitoring visits verify partner compliance with specific award terms, donor conditions, and programmatic requirements. Award context determines visit scope, checklist items, and whether finding']
  capacity_building_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.capacity_building_plan. Business justification: field_monitoring_visit has corrective_action_plan_required (BOOLEAN) and corrective_action_plan_status (STRING) but no FK to an actual plan. When field visits identify issues, corrective action plans ']
  compliance_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.incident. Business justification: Field monitoring visits may identify compliance incidents (fraud, safeguarding violations, financial irregularities). Direct discovery-to-incident link for audit trail. Critical for incident reporting']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Field monitoring visits incur costs (travel, accommodation, per diem, local transport) that must be charged to specific cost centers for expense tracking and budget management. Essential for monitorin']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to program.intervention. Business justification: Field monitoring visits assess partner implementation quality of specific interventions. Linking visit to intervention enables program-level quality assurance, intervention-specific findings aggregati']
  it_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.it_asset. Business justification: Field monitoring visits use specific equipment (tablets, GPS devices, cameras) for data collection. Linking to it_asset enables equipment accountability, maintenance scheduling, insurance claims for d']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the implementing partner organization being monitored during this visit. Links to the partner_org master record in the partnership domain.']
  partnership_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sub-award agreement, MoU, LoA, or SoW under which this monitoring visit is conducted, linking oversight activity to the specific binding instrument.']
  prior_visit_field_monitoring_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous field monitoring visit record for the same partner and site, enabling longitudinal tracking of compliance trends and verification of prior corrective actions.']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Ngo staff member who led and is responsible for this field monitoring visit. Links to the workforce staff_member record.']
  volunteer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to volunteer.volunteer. Business justification: Volunteers with specialized expertise (technical advisors, monitoring specialists) routinely participate in partner monitoring visits. Standard practice in consortium arrangements and quality assuranc']
  assets_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether donor-funded assets (equipment, vehicles, NFI stocks, infrastructure) were physically verified against the partner\'s asset register during this monitoring visit.']
  beneficiaries_interviewed_count varchar [note: 'Number of program beneficiaries or Persons of Concern (PoC) directly interviewed or consulted during the monitoring visit as part of accountability and feedback mechanisms.']
  corrective_action_plan_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the partner is required to submit or fully implement the Corrective Action Plan (CAP) addressing findings from this monitoring visit.']
  corrective_action_plan_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the monitoring visit findings require the partner to develop and submit a formal Corrective Action Plan (CAP) to address identified non-compliance issues.']
  corrective_action_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Corrective Action Plan (CAP) associated with this monitoring visit, tracking partner response from submission through acceptance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this field monitoring visit record was first created in the grant management system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as critical severity (requiring immediate corrective action, potential sub-award suspension, or donor notification) identified during the visit.']
  donor_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the severity of findings from this monitoring visit triggers a mandatory notification obligation to the prime donor (e.g., USAID, DFID/FCDO, CERF) under the terms of the prime award.']
  financial_findings_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative description of financial management findings from the spot-check component of the visit, including observations on expenditure documentation, procurement compliance, BvA accuracy, and fund accounting practices.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of key findings from the monitoring visit, capturing the most significant observations across programmatic, financial, safeguarding, and operational dimensions for inclusion in monitoring reports.']
  follow_up_visit_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the follow-up verification visit must be completed to confirm corrective action implementation and close outstanding findings.']
  follow_up_visit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a subsequent field monitoring visit is required to verify implementation of corrective actions or to conduct additional oversight based on findings from this visit.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording the most recent modification to this field monitoring visit record, supporting change tracking and audit compliance.']
  major_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as major severity (significant non-compliance requiring corrective action within a defined timeframe) identified during the visit.']
  minor_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as minor severity (observations or recommendations for improvement that do not constitute material non-compliance) identified during the visit.']
  monitoring_checklist_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the standardized monitoring checklist or tool used during the visit (e.g., KoboToolbox form version, internal checklist version number), ensuring reproducibility and comparability across visits.']
  overall_compliance_rating varchar [note: 'Aggregate compliance rating assigned to the partner following the monitoring visit, summarizing the overall level of adherence to sub-award terms, donor requirements, and organizational standards.']
  partner_acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner organization formally acknowledged receipt of the monitoring visit report and findings, initiating the corrective action response timeline.']
  partner_rep_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the senior partner organization representative who received the monitoring team and participated in the opening and closing meetings during the visit.']
  program_sector varchar [note: 'Humanitarian or development sector of the program being monitored (e.g., WASH, GBV, Nutrition, Education, Shelter, Health, Livelihoods, PSS). Aligns with OCHA cluster system and IATI sector codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wash|gbv|nutrition|education|shelter|health|livelihoods|pss|food_security|protection — promote to reference product]']
  safeguarding_findings_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative description of safeguarding and protection findings from the visit, including observations on GBV prevention measures, child protection protocols, complaint and feedback mechanisms, and CHS compliance.']
  site_admin1_name varchar [note: 'First-level administrative division (state, province, region, governorate) where the monitored project site is located, aligned with OCHA Common Operational Datasets (COD) administrative boundaries.']
  site_admin2_name varchar [note: 'Second-level administrative division (district, county, department, woreda) where the monitored project site is located, supporting geographic disaggregation in SitRep and donor reporting.']
  site_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country where the monitored project site is located.']
  site_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the monitored project site in decimal degrees (WGS84), enabling GIS mapping and spatial analysis of monitoring coverage.']
  site_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the monitored project site in decimal degrees (WGS84), enabling GIS mapping and spatial analysis of monitoring coverage.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific partner project site, field office, distribution point, health facility, or community location visited during the monitoring exercise.']
  staff_interviewed_count varchar [note: 'Number of partner organization staff members interviewed or consulted during the monitoring visit, supporting assessment of organizational capacity and program implementation quality.']
  total_findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct findings documented during the monitoring visit across all severity levels, providing a quantitative measure of monitoring intensity and partner compliance issues.']
  visit_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the field monitoring visit was conducted at the partner project site. Principal real-world event date for this transaction.']
  visit_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the field monitoring visit concluded at the partner site, enabling duration tracking and staff time reporting.']
  visit_purpose varchar [note: 'Primary objective classification of the field monitoring visit. Determines the scope of review activities, checklists applied, and reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: programmatic_monitoring|financial_spot_check|safeguarding_audit|asset_verification|capacity_building|closeout_review — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  visit_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to the visit for tracking in grant management systems, donor reports, and partner correspondence. Format: FMV-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  visit_report_submitted_date date [note: 'Date on which the completed field monitoring visit report was formally submitted to the partnership management team and/or uploaded to the grant management system.']
  visit_report_url varchar [note: 'URL or document management system path to the full field monitoring visit report, including all findings, recommendations, and supporting evidence uploaded to the GMS or document repository.']
  visit_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the field monitoring visit commenced at the partner site, used for scheduling, duration calculation, and audit trail purposes.']
  visit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the field monitoring visit record, tracking progression from planning through completion and any required follow-up actions.']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Modality of the monitoring visit indicating whether the partner was notified in advance (announced), visited without prior notice (unannounced), conducted remotely, or performed as a desk review of submitted documentation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for field monitoring and supportive supervision visits conducted by Ngo staff to implementing partner project sites as part of sub-award oversight obligations. Captures visit date, site location, visit purpose (programmatic monitoring, financial spot-check, safeguarding audit, asset verification), findings categorized by severity, recommendations, corrective action plans with deadlines, and follow-up verification status. Distinct from MEL domain programmatic monitoring: this product focuses on partner compliance and sub-award oversight rather than program outcome measurement. Supports donor compliance requirements for partner monitoring under USAID ADS 303 and 2 CFR 200.332.'
}

Table partnership.partner_compliance {
  partner_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a partner compliance assignment record. Primary key.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to compliance.obligation. Identifies which compliance obligation this partner must fulfill.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to partnership.partner_org. Identifies which partner organization this compliance obligation applies to.']
  assigned_date date [note: 'The date when this obligation was assigned to this partner. Used to track when the compliance requirement became applicable to this partner relationship.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The date when this partner successfully completed or submitted this obligation. Null if not yet completed. Used for compliance reporting and performance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this partner compliance assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which this partner must fulfill this obligation. May differ from the obligation\'s global next_due_date if partner-specific extensions or custom schedules apply. Used for monitoring and escalation.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this partner compliance assignment record was most recently modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes specific to this partner\'s fulfillment of this obligation. May include partner-specific instructions, exceptions, submission details, or follow-up actions. Supplements the global obligation notes with partner-specific context.']
  partner_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this partner\'s fulfillment of this specific obligation. Tracks lifecycle from assignment through completion. Values: assigned (obligation applies but work not started), in_progress (partner actively working on fulfillment), submitted (partner submitted but pending review), completed (successfully fulfilled), overdue (past due date without completion), waived (obligation waived for this partner), not_applicable (determined not to apply to this partner).']
  responsible_focal_point varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the specific individual at the partner organization responsible for fulfilling this obligation. May reference a contact from partner_org embedded contact records or be a free-text name. Used for accountability and follow-up communications.']
  waiver_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether a waiver has been requested or granted for this partner for this obligation. Values: none (no waiver), requested (partner requested waiver, pending decision), approved (waiver granted, obligation does not apply), denied (waiver request rejected, obligation still applies). Used when partners seek exemptions from specific obligations.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Assignment between partner_org and obligation. It captures the assignment of specific compliance obligations to partner organizations, tracking partner-specific compliance status, responsible focal points, and completion tracking. Each record links one partner organization to one compliance obligation with attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship: which partner must fulfill which obligation, who is responsible, when it is due, and whether it has been completed.. Existence Justification: In nonprofit operations, partner organizations are subject to multiple compliance obligations (IRS filings, donor reporting, audit requirements, registration renewals), and each compliance obligation applies to multiple partner organizations. The NGO actively manages partner compliance tracking as an operational process: assigning obligations to partners, monitoring due dates, tracking completion status, and escalating overdue items. This is not an analytical correlation but an operational business process where compliance officers create, update, and monitor partner-specific compliance assignments.'
}

Table partnership.campaign_participation {
  campaign_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a specific partner organization\'s participation in a specific advocacy campaign.']
  advocacy_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to communication.advocacy_campaign identifying which campaign the partner is participating in.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to partnership.partner_org identifying which partner organization is participating.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The current approval status of the partner organization\'s participation in this campaign. Pending indicates invitation sent but not yet confirmed; approved indicates partner has confirmed participation; declined indicates partner declined the invitation; withdrawn indicates partner initially approved but later withdrew. This tracks the lifecycle of each participation relationship.']
  contribution_type varchar [note: 'The type of contribution the partner organization provides to this specific campaign. Examples include advocacy (policy influence), content (materials and messaging), funding (financial support), field implementation (on-ground activities), technical expertise (specialized knowledge), media amplification (press and social reach), or coalition building (network mobilization). A partner may have different contribution types across different campaigns.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this participation record was created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  participation_end_date date [note: 'The date when the partner organization\'s active participation in this campaign ended or is planned to end. May differ from the campaign end date if the partner\'s involvement is time-limited. Null for ongoing participation. Used for tracking partner engagement duration and post-campaign evaluation.']
  participation_start_date date [note: 'The date when the partner organization\'s active participation in this campaign began. May differ from the campaign start date if the partner joined mid-campaign. Used for tracking partner engagement duration and coordination timelines.']
  partner_role varchar [note: 'The specific role the partner organization plays in this campaign. Distinguishes between lead partners (primary coordinators), supporting partners (secondary contributors), implementing partners (field execution), technical partners (subject matter expertise), financial partners (funding contributors), and media partners (amplification and outreach). This attribute exists only in the context of a specific partner-campaign relationship.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this participation record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  visibility_level varchar [note: 'The level of public visibility and attribution the partner organization receives for their participation in this campaign. Public means prominently featured in campaign materials; acknowledged means listed as a partner but not prominently featured; confidential means partner involvement is tracked internally but not publicly disclosed; anonymous means partner requested no attribution. Visibility level varies by partner preference and campaign context.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the participation relationship between partner organizations and advocacy campaigns. It captures each partner\'s role, contribution type, visibility level, and approval status for their involvement in specific campaigns. Each record links one partner organization to one advocacy campaign with attributes that exist only in the context of this participation relationship. This is the operational record of partner engagement in advocacy, awareness, and fundraising campaigns managed by the communications team.. Existence Justification: In nonprofit operations, partner organizations participate in multiple advocacy campaigns simultaneously (e.g., a CSO may be involved in a humanitarian access campaign, a localization advocacy initiative, and a policy reform campaign at the same time). Each advocacy campaign involves multiple partner organizations with distinct roles (lead, supporting, implementing). The communications team actively manages these participation relationships, tracking each partner\'s role, contribution type, visibility level, and approval status per campaign for coordination, attribution, and reporting purposes.'
}

Table partnership.consortium_communication {
  consortium_communication_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the consortium_communication association']
  consortium_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the consortium record. Identifies which multi-partner consortium arrangement this communication plan assignment relates to.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the communication plan record. Identifies which communication plan or deliverable (strategic plan, SitRep, briefing note) is associated with this consortium.']
  reviewed_by_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member from this consortium who conducted the review and approval. Captures accountability for consortium governance compliance in joint communications.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'The level of approval authority this consortium holds over the communication plan content. Required indicates consortium steering committee must approve before publication; advisory indicates consortium provides input but does not have veto power; none indicates no formal approval process for this consortium.']
  assigned_date date [note: 'Date when this consortium was formally assigned to or included in the communication plan scope. Used for tracking when consortium coordination requirements were established.']
  branding_guidelines varchar [note: 'Specific branding and logo usage guidelines for this consortium in the communication plan. Captures consortium logo placement requirements, member organization logo protocols, lead agency branding hierarchy, pooled fund mechanism branding requirements, and any donor-mandated branding restrictions for joint communications.']
  consortium_role varchar [note: 'The specific role this consortium plays in the communication plan execution. Lead_communicator indicates the consortium is the primary author/distributor; contributing_partner indicates input/review responsibilities; review_only indicates approval rights without authorship; information_recipient indicates the consortium receives but does not contribute to the communication.']
  review_completed_date date [note: 'Date when this consortium completed its review and approval process for the communication plan. Used for tracking consortium governance cycle times and coordination efficiency.']
  review_deadline date [note: 'Deadline by which this consortium must complete its review and approval process for the communication plan. Critical for time-sensitive humanitarian coordination reporting such as OCHA SitReps and cluster updates.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing consortium feedback, revision requests, or approval conditions for the communication plan. Documents consortium steering committee input and ensures transparency in multi-partner coordination.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this consortium\'s review and approval process for the communication plan. Tracks the workflow state of consortium steering committee review, ensuring governance protocols are followed for multi-partner communications.']
  visibility_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific visibility and acknowledgment requirements for this consortium in the communication deliverables. Captures donor visibility clauses, OCHA cluster reporting requirements, IASC working group acknowledgment protocols, and Grand Bargain localization visibility commitments that must be reflected in the communication materials.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment of communication plans to consortia in multi-partner humanitarian coordination contexts. It captures the specific role each consortium plays in a communication plan, approval authority for consortium-branded materials, visibility requirements for donor and cluster reporting, branding guidelines for joint communications, and review status for coordinated multi-stakeholder messaging. Each record links one consortium to one communication plan with attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship, supporting Grand Bargain transparency commitments and IASC coordination protocols.. Existence Justification: In humanitarian multi-partner coordination, a single consortium can be referenced in multiple communication plans (donor-specific reports, OCHA SitReps, advocacy campaigns, emergency response briefings), and a single communication plan (especially strategic plans or crisis SitReps) can cover multiple consortia or joint programming arrangements. The business actively manages consortium-specific communication requirements including approval authority, branding protocols, visibility commitments, and review workflows for each consortium-plan combination.'
}

Table finance.finance_fund {
  finance_fund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fund record. Primary key for the finance_fund product.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Restricted funds must track applicable compliance obligations (donor reporting schedules, audit requirements, IATI publication). Fund managers need to know which obligations apply to each fund for com']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the donor or funding source that provided the resources for this fund. Links to Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud constituent records.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  finance_nicra_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.nicra_rate. Business justification: Funds are governed by NICRA agreements that define allowable indirect cost rates. This FK enables joining to get the rate percentage and agreement details.']
  funding_source_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific funding source record that channels resources into this fund. May represent government agencies, foundations, or institutional donors.']
  parent_fund_finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent fund if this fund is a sub-fund or component of a larger fund structure. Enables hierarchical fund accounting and roll-up reporting.']
  allowable_cost_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of cost categories that are allowable charges to this fund per donor restrictions or OMB Uniform Guidance. Guides expense posting and audit compliance.']
  audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fund is subject to external audit requirements under OMB Uniform Guidance Single Audit provisions or donor-specific audit clauses.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budgeted amount allocated to the fund for the current fiscal period. Used as the baseline for Budget versus Actual (BvA) variance analysis.']
  chart_of_accounts_segment varchar [note: 'The fund dimension segment code in the organization\'s Chart of Accounts structure. Used for General Ledger posting and financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the fund was officially closed. Marks the completion of the fund lifecycle and triggers closeout procedures per OMB Uniform Guidance.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount committed through purchase orders, contracts, or other encumbrances but not yet expended. Used for fund availability calculations.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fund is currently in compliance with all donor conditions, OMB Uniform Guidance requirements, and internal policies. False triggers compliance review workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fund record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fund\'s primary currency. Essential for multi-currency fund management and foreign exchange reporting.']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current available balance in the fund after all posted transactions. Updated in real-time by the ERP system for fund availability checking.']
  donor_reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which financial reports must be submitted to the donor or funding source. Drives reporting calendar and compliance workflows.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when the fund expires or is scheduled to close. Critical for grant closeout planning and restricted fund compliance.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when the fund becomes active and available for transactions. Aligns with grant award start dates or board approval dates.']
  expended_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount expended from the fund during the current fiscal period. Tracks actual spending against budget for BvA reporting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (YYYYMM format) for fund activity tracking. Supports monthly and quarterly financial reporting cycles.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this fund record applies. Enables year-over-year fund performance analysis and multi-year grant tracking.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the fund for identification in financial systems and reporting. Used as the business identifier across SAP S/4HANA and Unit4 ERP.']
  fund_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the fund as it appears in financial statements and donor reports.']
  fund_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the fund in the financial system. Determines whether transactions can be posted to the fund.']
  fund_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fund based on restriction type and purpose. Determines how funds can be used and reported per FASB ASC 958 net asset classification requirements.']
  indirect_cost_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA) percentage applicable to this fund. Used for Facilities and Administration (F&A) cost allocation per OMB Uniform Guidance.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent external audit that included this fund. Used for audit cycle tracking and compliance monitoring.']
  last_report_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent financial report was submitted to the donor for this fund. Tracks reporting compliance and timeliness.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staff member or department responsible for managing and overseeing the fund. Accountable for fund stewardship and compliance.']
  match_fulfilled_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount of matching funds or in-kind contributions fulfilled to date. Tracked for grant reporting and compliance verification.']
  match_requirement_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of matching funds or cost share required by the donor or grant agreement. Critical for tracking cost share commitments and compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fund record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next external audit covering this fund. Supports audit planning and documentation preparation.']
  next_report_due_date date [note: 'The due date for the next scheduled financial report to the donor. Critical for compliance and relationship management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or contextual information about the fund. Used by fund managers for operational guidance.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The fund balance at the beginning of the current fiscal year or at fund inception. Represents the starting financial position for Budget versus Actual (BvA) tracking.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'The program or project code linked to the fund. Enables program-level financial reporting and Monitoring Evaluation and Learning (MEL) integration.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the intended use and purpose of the fund, including any donor-specified restrictions or programmatic objectives.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Specific nature of restrictions placed on the fund by donors or the board. Governs allowable expenditures and compliance requirements under OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.']
  unallowable_cost_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of cost categories that are explicitly unallowable for this fund. Prevents non-compliant expense posting.']

  Note: 'Master record for each restricted or unrestricted fund managed by the NGO. Captures fund name, fund code, restriction type (donor-restricted, board-designated, unrestricted), associated grant or donor source, fund purpose, opening balance, currency, fiscal year, fund status, and compliance flags per OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 and FASB ASC 958. The SSOT for fund identity used across grant financial management, cost allocation, and BvA reporting in Unit4 ERP and SAP S/4HANA.'
}

Table finance.cost_center {
  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cost center. Primary key for the cost center master record.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Cost centers (programs/field offices) have specific compliance obligations (CHS commitments, sphere standards, donor-specific requirements). Program managers track obligations by cost center for opera']
  parent_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent cost center in the organizational hierarchy. Enables roll-up reporting from field offices to regional hubs to headquarters. Nullable for top-level cost centers.']
  primary_cost_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this cost center record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member who is accountable for budget performance, expense approval, and financial stewardship of this cost center. Used in RACI (Responsible Accountable Consulted Informed) matrices and budget versus actual (BvA) reporting.']
  system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Cost centers track IT platform-specific expenses (ERP licenses, grants management subscriptions) for budget allocation and indirect cost recovery. Required for platform cost reports in NICRA calculati']
  tertiary_cost_approved_by_user_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this cost center for activation. Typically a finance director, controller, or Chief Financial Officer (CFO). Nullable for cost centers not yet approved.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'The primary activity or service provided by this cost center (e.g., beneficiary registration, field assessment, grant management, financial reporting, volunteer coordination). Used for activity-based costing and internal service billing.']
  annual_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total approved budget amount for this cost center for the current fiscal year. Used in Budget versus Actual (BvA) variance analysis and financial stewardship reporting.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status for new or modified cost centers. Draft cost centers are under construction; pending approval cost centers await finance or executive review; approved cost centers are active; rejected cost centers require revision.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cost center was approved for activation. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting. Nullable for cost centers not yet approved.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost center\'s budget and financial reporting. Used for multi-currency organizations with country offices operating in local currencies.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cost center was permanently closed and all balances were transferred or reconciled. Used for grant closeout, project completion, and organizational restructuring. Nullable for active cost centers.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA or Unit4 ERP company code representing the legal entity to which this cost center belongs. Used for multi-entity consolidation and statutory reporting.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA controlling area (CO module) to which this cost center is assigned. Controlling areas represent organizational units for internal cost accounting and management reporting.']
  cost_allocation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to allocate shared or indirect costs to this cost center. Direct allocation assigns costs explicitly; proportional uses a percentage driver; headcount allocates by staff count; square footage uses physical space; activity-based costing (ABC) uses cost drivers; not allocated for cost centers that do not receive shared cost allocations.']
  cost_center_category varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA cost center category classification. Production cost centers deliver direct program services; service cost centers provide internal support; administrative cost centers manage overhead; auxiliary cost centers support shared services; project cost centers are time-bound; temporary cost centers are short-term or pilot initiatives.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the cost center in SAP S/4HANA and Unit4 ERP. Used in general ledger posting, budget tracking, and financial reporting.']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the cost center\'s purpose, scope, and responsibilities. Includes information on program activities, geographic coverage, donor restrictions, and organizational context.']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'The full descriptive name of the cost center as it appears in financial reports and organizational charts.']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active cost centers accept expense postings; inactive cost centers are temporarily disabled; suspended cost centers are under review; closed cost centers are permanently retired; pending activation cost centers are awaiting approval.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center by functional expense category per GAAP for nonprofits. Program cost centers support direct mission delivery; administrative cost centers support organizational management; fundraising cost centers support donor cultivation and gift processing; general and administrative (G&A) support overall operations; mission support provides enabling services; field operations manage country office and site-level activities.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary geographic location of this cost center. Used for country office cost centers, field operations, and regional program management. Nullable for headquarters or global cost centers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cost center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  department varchar [note: 'The functional department or division to which this cost center belongs (e.g., Health Programs, WASH, Education, Finance, Human Resources, Monitoring Evaluation and Learning). Used for cross-cutting organizational reporting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this cost center ceases to be active. Nullable for open-ended cost centers. Used for project-specific or time-bound organizational units.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this cost center becomes active and can accept expense allocations and budget assignments.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this cost center in external systems such as donor grant management systems (GMS), partner organization ERPs, or government reporting portals. Used for cross-system reconciliation and International Aid Transparency Initiative (IATI) reporting.']
  functional_expense_classification varchar [note: 'GAAP functional expense category per FASB ASC 958-720. Program services are mission-delivery activities; management and general (M&G) are administrative overhead; fundraising supports donor cultivation; membership development supports constituent engagement. Required for Form 990 and audited financial statements.']
  fund_restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of donor-imposed restrictions on funds allocated to this cost center per FASB ASC 958-205. Unrestricted funds have no donor-imposed restrictions; temporarily restricted funds have time or purpose restrictions that will lapse; permanently restricted funds (endowments) must be maintained in perpetuity.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The depth of this cost center in the organizational hierarchy tree. Level 1 represents headquarters or top-level divisions; higher numbers represent nested field offices, country offices, or project sites.']
  indirect_cost_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether expenses posted to this cost center are eligible for indirect cost recovery under the organization\'s Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA). True for direct program cost centers; false for administrative or already-indirect cost centers. Used in Facilities and Administration (F&A) cost allocation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cost center record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  nicra_base_category varchar [note: 'The indirect cost base category used for calculating indirect cost recovery for this cost center per the organization\'s NICRA with the federal government. Modified Total Direct Costs (MTDC) is the most common base; direct salaries and wages is used for some research organizations; total direct costs includes all direct expenses. Not applicable for cost centers that do not participate in indirect cost allocation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, donor conditions, compliance requirements, or historical context relevant to this cost center.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'The profit center code for organizations using profit center accounting in SAP S/4HANA. Used for segment reporting and internal performance measurement. Nullable for nonprofits not using profit center structures.']
  program_area varchar [note: 'The thematic program area or sector this cost center supports (e.g., WASH - Water Sanitation and Hygiene, GBV - Gender-Based Violence, Health, Education, Livelihoods, Emergency Response). Used for Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) alignment and donor reporting.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region for multi-country or regional cost centers. Used for regional program offices, humanitarian response hubs, and Development Assistance Committee (DAC) reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: africa|asia_pacific|europe|latin_america_caribbean|middle_east|north_america|global — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this cost center master data originated. Used for data lineage tracking and reconciliation between SAP S/4HANA, Unit4 ERP, and legacy systems.']
  year_to_date_actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative actual expenses posted to this cost center from the start of the fiscal year to the current reporting period. Used in BvA reporting and burn rate analysis.']

  Note: 'Master record for organizational cost centers used to allocate expenses across programs, departments, country offices, and projects. Captures cost center code, name, type (program, administrative, fundraising), responsible manager, parent cost center (for hierarchy), organizational hierarchy level, associated country or region, active status, and functional expense classification per GAAP. The SSOT for organizational cost structure used in SAP S/4HANA and Unit4 ERP for cost allocation, indirect cost recovery, NICRA base calculations, and Budget vs. Actual reporting.'
}

Table finance.gl_account {
  gl_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the general ledger account. Primary key for the GL account master data.']
  account_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the GL account purpose, usage guidelines, and what types of transactions should be posted to this account.']
  account_group varchar [note: 'The account group or category within the Chart of Accounts structure (e.g., \'Current Assets\', \'Fixed Assets\', \'Restricted Contributions\', \'Program Expenses\').']
  account_name varchar [note: 'The human-readable descriptive name of the GL account (e.g., \'Cash - Operating Account\', \'Grant Revenue - USAID\', \'Program Salaries - WASH\').']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique account number within the Chart of Accounts (CoA) structure. This is the business identifier used in SAP S/4HANA and Unit4 ERP for all financial transactions and reporting.']
  account_owner varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the business owner or responsible party for this GL account (e.g., Finance Director, Program Manager, Grant Manager).']
  account_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the GL account: active (available for posting), inactive (temporarily suspended), blocked (posting prohibited), or closed (permanently retired).']
  account_type varchar [note: 'The fundamental accounting classification of the GL account: asset, liability, net asset (equity for nonprofits per FASB ASC 958), revenue, or expense.']
  budget_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is subject to budget control and Budget versus Actual (BvA) monitoring. When true, transactions require budget availability checking before posting.']
  coa_structure_code varchar [note: 'The Chart of Accounts structure identifier or version code. Nonprofits may maintain multiple CoA structures for different legal entities or reporting requirements.']
  consolidation_account_number varchar [note: 'The GL account number used for consolidated financial reporting across multiple legal entities or country offices. Used when the nonprofit has a multi-entity structure.']
  cost_allocation_base_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account serves as an allocation base for distributing indirect costs across programs and grants (e.g., direct salaries, total direct costs).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this GL account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for this GL account (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Nonprofits operating internationally may maintain accounts in multiple currencies.']
  dac_sector_code varchar [note: 'The OECD Development Assistance Committee sector classification code for international development activities. Required for International Aid Transparency Initiative (IATI) reporting.']
  donor_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account tracks donor-restricted funds requiring specific usage compliance and separate reporting per donor agreements and FASB ASC 958.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this GL account was closed or became inactive. Null for currently active accounts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this GL account became active and available for transaction posting in the Chart of Accounts.']
  external_reporting_code varchar [note: 'The mapping code used for external regulatory reporting (e.g., IRS Form 990 line item, IATI activity code, donor-specific reporting category).']
  financial_statement_classification varchar [note: 'The primary financial statement where this account appears: balance sheet (statement of financial position), statement of activities (income statement equivalent for nonprofits), statement of functional expenses, statement of cash flows, or off-balance-sheet.']
  functional_expense_category varchar [note: 'The functional expense classification required by FASB ASC 958 for expense accounts: program services, management and general (administrative), fundraising, or not applicable (for non-expense accounts). Used for Form 990 reporting and functional expense allocation.']
  fund_restriction_class varchar [note: 'The net asset restriction classification per FASB ASC 958: unrestricted (no donor restrictions), temporarily restricted (time or purpose restrictions), or permanently restricted (endowment principal). Critical for nonprofit fund accounting and donor compliance.']
  grant_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether expenses posted to this account are eligible for reimbursement under federal grants per OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200. Critical for grant financial management and cost allocation.']
