  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Key lessons learned from the crisis response, including what worked well, what could be improved, and recommendations for future crisis communications.']
  media_inquiries_received_count varchar [note: 'Total number of media inquiries received from journalists, news outlets, and media organizations during this crisis event.']
  media_inquiries_responded_count varchar [note: 'Total number of media inquiries that received an official response from the organization during this crisis event.']
  post_crisis_review_date date [note: 'The date when the post-crisis review meeting or assessment was conducted.']
  post_crisis_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the post-crisis review and lessons learned process: not started, scheduled, in progress, completed (review done), published (findings shared).']
  press_releases_issued_count varchar [note: 'Total number of official press releases issued to media during this crisis event.']
  regulatory_report_submitted_date date [note: 'The date when required regulatory reports were submitted to relevant authorities regarding the crisis event.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether regulatory reporting to government agencies, charity commissions, or oversight bodies was required for this crisis event.']
  response_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the communications response actions taken during the crisis, including press releases issued, social media posts, stakeholder briefings, town halls, and other communication activities.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Analysis of the underlying root causes that led to or exacerbated the crisis, used for organizational learning and prevention of future incidents.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'The assessed severity of the crisis event: low (minimal impact, localized), medium (moderate impact, regional), high (significant impact, multi-country), critical (catastrophic impact, organization-wide threat).']
  social_media_posts_count varchar [note: 'Total number of social media posts published across all organizational channels during this crisis event.']
  spokesperson_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the designated spokesperson for quick reference in communications materials and media coordination.']
  stakeholder_briefings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of stakeholder briefings conducted with donors, partners, board members, and other key stakeholders during this crisis event.']

  Note: 'Master record for crisis and emergency communications events managed by the communications team during humanitarian responses, organizational incidents, or reputational risks. Captures crisis name, crisis type (natural disaster, security incident, reputational), activation date, deactivation date, spokesperson assigned, key messages approved, media inquiries received, response actions taken, and post-crisis review status. Supports rapid response communications and organizational resilience.'
}

Table communication.campaign_media_outreach {
  campaign_media_outreach_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this campaign-media contact engagement record. Primary key.']
  advocacy_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the advocacy campaign for which media outreach is being conducted']
  media_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the media contact being engaged for this campaign']
  contact_role varchar [note: 'The role or capacity in which the media contact is engaged for this campaign (e.g., primary journalist, backup contact, spokesperson, technical expert for interviews)']
  coverage_publication_date date [note: 'The date when media coverage resulting from this engagement was published or broadcast']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'The type of media coverage resulting from this engagement (e.g., news article, feature story, interview, broadcast segment, social media post, opinion piece, or none if no coverage resulted)']
  coverage_url varchar [note: 'URL or link to the published media coverage resulting from this campaign-contact engagement']
  embargo_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an embargo agreement was established for this specific campaign-contact engagement (true if embargo terms apply to this outreach)']
  engagement_date date [note: 'The date when the media contact was first engaged or contacted for this specific campaign']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether follow-up action is required with this media contact for this campaign (true if follow-up needed, false if engagement is complete)']
  interview_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an interview with NGO spokesperson or subject matter expert was granted to this media contact for this campaign (true if interview occurred)']
  media_kit_sent_date date [note: 'The date when the campaign media kit, press release, or briefing materials were sent to this media contact']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the engagement interaction, conversation highlights, commitments made, or other relevant details about this campaign-contact relationship']
  outreach_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the media outreach activity for this campaign-contact pair (e.g., planned, contacted, responded, coverage secured, declined, no response, closed)']
  response_date date [note: 'The date when the media contact responded to the campaign outreach, if applicable']
  response_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the media contact responded to the campaign outreach (true if response received, false if no response)']

  Note: 'This association product represents the media relations activity between advocacy campaigns and media contacts. It captures the proactive outreach, engagement tracking, and relationship management that communications teams perform when engaging journalists and media influencers for specific campaigns. Each record links one advocacy campaign to one media contact with attributes that track the engagement lifecycle, response outcomes, and follow-up activities.. Existence Justification: In nonprofit communications operations, media relations teams actively manage which journalists and media contacts to engage for each advocacy campaign. A single campaign requires outreach to multiple media contacts (regional journalists, national outlets, specialized beat reporters), and a single media contact is engaged across multiple campaigns over time. The relationship is operationally managed with tracking of engagement dates, response outcomes, coverage types, and follow-up requirements.'
}

Table communication.message_thread {
  message_thread_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for message_thread']
  assigned_to_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team or department responsible for handling this message thread.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advocacy or fundraising campaign associated with this message thread, if applicable.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the humanitarian program or project related to this message thread, if applicable.']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary contact or party involved in this message thread (donor, beneficiary, media contact, staff member).']
  parent_message_thread_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on message_thread (parent_message_thread_id)']
  user_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or user currently responsible for managing and responding to this message thread.']
  account_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational account associated with this message thread, if applicable.']
  archived_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the message thread was archived. Null if not archived.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the message thread was marked as closed or resolved. Null if still open.']
  communication_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or medium through which the message thread is conducted (email, SMS, WhatsApp, web portal, mobile app, phone).']
  complaint_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the complaint type if this thread is marked as a complaint (service quality, staff conduct, discrimination, safety concern, fraud, other).']
  consent_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit consent was obtained from the contact to store and process their communication data, required for data protection compliance.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country context for this message thread.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this message thread record was first created in the system.']
  data_protection_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to the message thread content based on sensitivity and privacy requirements.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric indicator of how many times this thread has been escalated to higher management or specialized teams. Zero indicates no escalation.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from an external system or platform where this message thread originated or is synchronized.']
  first_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the first response was provided to the initiating party, used to measure response time performance.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'The geographic region or operational area associated with this message thread, relevant for field operations and beneficiary communications.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the message thread was first created or initiated by the originating party.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the message thread has been archived and moved out of active communication management.']
  is_complaint boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this message thread represents a formal complaint or grievance requiring special handling per accountability standards.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the message thread contains confidential or sensitive information requiring restricted access and special handling.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the primary language used in the message thread.']
  last_message_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the most recent message was added to this thread.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this message thread record was last updated or modified.']
  message_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual messages exchanged within this thread.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The urgency or importance level assigned to the message thread, determining response time requirements.']
  requires_translation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the message thread requires translation services to facilitate communication between parties speaking different languages.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting how the message thread was resolved, actions taken, and outcomes achieved.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the issue or inquiry in the message thread was resolved to the satisfaction of all parties.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'The target date by which a response is expected based on service level agreements or accountability commitments.']
  retention_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this message thread should be archived or deleted according to data retention policies and regulatory requirements.']
  satisfaction_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric satisfaction rating provided by the contact after thread resolution, typically on a scale of 1 to 5, used to measure service quality.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Automated sentiment analysis score for the overall thread, ranging from -1.00 (very negative) to +1.00 (very positive), used to identify satisfaction or concerns.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or platform from which this message thread data was captured (e.g., Salesforce, email server, mobile app).']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords or tags applied to the message thread for categorization, search, and reporting purposes.']
  thread_reference_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the message thread, used in external communications and reporting.']
  thread_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the message thread indicating its state in the communication workflow.']
  thread_subject varchar [note: 'The subject or title of the message thread, summarizing the topic or purpose of the conversation.']
  thread_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the message thread based on its purpose and audience (donor communication, advocacy campaign, beneficiary feedback, complaint, media inquiry, internal communication).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for message_thread. Referenced by thread_id.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_filing {
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory filing record. Primary key for the regulatory filing entity.']
  filing_system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Regulatory filings are submitted through specific platforms (IRS e-file, state charity portals, IATI registry). Nonprofits must track which system was used for each filing for audit trail, troubleshoo']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Each regulatory filing satisfies a specific compliance obligation from the master obligation catalog. One filing is for one obligation; one obligation generates many filings over time (recurring oblig']
  original_filing_regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original regulatory filing record that this amendment corrects or supersedes. Null if this is an original filing.']
  statutory_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.statutory_registration. Business justification: Regulatory filings are often associated with specific statutory registrations (e.g., annual charity returns for a registration, IRS 990 for a 501(c)(3) registration). One filing is for one registratio']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority formally accepted the filing as complete and compliant. Marks successful completion of the filing obligation.']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority acknowledged receipt of the filing. Confirms that the submission was received and entered into the processing queue.']
  amendment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing is an amendment or correction of a previously submitted filing. True if this is an amended return, false if original filing.']
  authorized_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual authorized to sign and submit the regulatory filing on behalf of the organization. Typically an officer or director with legal authority.']
  authorized_signatory_title varchar [note: 'Official title or position of the authorized signatory. Examples include Executive Director, Board Chair, President, or Chief Executive Officer (CEO).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this regulatory filing record was first created in the system. Marks the beginning of the filing lifecycle for audit trail purposes.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Web address or file path where the submitted filing document is stored for retrieval and audit purposes. May point to internal document management system or external regulatory portal.']
  due_date date [note: 'Statutory or regulatory deadline by which the filing must be submitted to avoid penalties or loss of status. Calculated based on fiscal year end and jurisdiction-specific rules.']
  extended_due_date date [note: 'New filing deadline after an approved extension. Replaces the original due date for compliance tracking purposes.']
  extension_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requested filing deadline extension was granted by the regulatory authority. True if extension was approved, false if denied or not applicable.']
  extension_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an extension of the filing deadline was requested from the regulatory authority. True if extension was requested, false otherwise.']
  filing_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary fee charged by the regulatory authority for processing the filing. Amount in the organizations functional currency.']
  filing_fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the filing fee amount. Examples include USD, GBP, EUR.']
  filing_fee_payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the filing fee was paid to the regulatory authority. Used to confirm payment and avoid processing delays.']
  filing_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, comments, or special circumstances related to the filing. Used to document unusual situations, preparer communications, or follow-up actions required.']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier or confirmation number assigned by the regulatory authority or filing system upon submission. Examples include IRS e-file confirmation number, Charity Commission submission reference, or state registration number.']
  filing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the fiscal or reporting period covered by this regulatory filing. Defines the conclusion of the time span for which financial and operational data are reported.']
  filing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the fiscal or reporting period covered by this regulatory filing. Defines the beginning of the time span for which financial and operational data are reported.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory filing. Tracks progression from initial draft through submission, acknowledgment, and final acceptance or rejection by the regulatory authority. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|acknowledged|accepted|rejected|resubmitted|withdrawn|pending_review — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this regulatory filing record was most recently updated. Tracks all changes throughout the filing lifecycle from draft to final acceptance.']
  preparer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or firm responsible for preparing the regulatory filing. May be internal staff or external consultant/accountant.']
  preparer_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or firm employing the preparer, if applicable. Identifies external accounting firms, consultancies, or legal advisors engaged to prepare the filing.']
  preparer_ptin varchar [note: 'IRS-issued Preparer Tax Identification Number for the individual who prepared the filing. Required for paid tax return preparers in the United States.']
  public_disclosure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing is subject to public disclosure requirements. True for filings that must be made available for public inspection (e.g., IRS Form 990), false for confidential regulatory reports.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority rejected the filing due to errors, omissions, or non-compliance. Triggers resubmission workflow.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code assigned by the regulatory authority indicating the specific reason for filing rejection. Used to identify and correct errors for resubmission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IRS Reject Code 0001|0002|0003|0004|0005|0006|... — promote to reference product]']
  rejection_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the filing was rejected by the regulatory authority. Provides context and guidance for corrective action.']
  resubmission_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this filing has been resubmitted after rejection. Tracks the iteration history for audit and quality improvement purposes.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the internal review and approval of the filing was completed. Marks the point at which the filing was deemed ready for submission.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual responsible for reviewing and approving the regulatory filing before submission. Typically a senior finance officer, legal counsel, or executive director.']
  reviewer_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the individual who reviewed and approved the filing. Examples include Chief Financial Officer (CFO), Executive Director, or Board Treasurer.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Method or channel through which the filing was submitted to the regulatory authority. Distinguishes between electronic filing systems, paper mail, online portals, and third-party filing services.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the filing was submitted to the regulatory authority. Used to determine timeliness and compliance with due date requirements.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the filing was transmitted to the regulatory authority or filing portal. Provides audit trail for electronic submissions.']

  Note: 'Master record of all statutory and regulatory filings including full submission audit trail. Covers IRS Form 990, Charity Commission annual returns, state charitable solicitation registrations, and foreign agent registration filings. Tracks filing type, jurisdiction, due date, submission date, channel (electronic/paper/portal), confirmation number, status lifecycle (draft → submitted → acknowledged → accepted/rejected), preparer, reviewer, filing period, rejection reason codes, and resubmission history. Each submission attempt is recorded for complete audit trail.'
}

Table compliance.obligation {
  obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for obligation']
  required_system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Compliance obligations often mandate specific technology platforms (IATI publication requires IATI registry, 990 filing requires e-file system, grant reporting requires donor portals). Nonprofits must']
  chs_self_assessment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation requires a self-assessment against the Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) commitments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this compliance obligation record was first created in the system.']
  documentation_required varchar [note: 'Description of the documents, reports, or evidence that must be prepared or submitted to fulfill this obligation (e.g., \'Audited financial statements\', \'Program impact report\', \'Beneficiary data summary\', \'Board meeting minutes\').']
  donor_funder_name varchar [note: 'The name of the donor, funder, or granting organization that requires this obligation, if applicable (e.g., \'USAID\', \'DFID\', \'Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation\').']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when this obligation first became applicable or enforceable for the organization.']
  escalation_threshold_days varchar [note: 'The number of days before the due date when an alert or escalation should be triggered to ensure timely completion.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date when this obligation ceases to be applicable or enforceable, if applicable. Null for ongoing obligations.']
  fiscal_year_applicable varchar [note: 'The fiscal year or reporting period to which this obligation applies (e.g., \'FY2024\', \'2024\', \'Q1-2024\').']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the obligation must be fulfilled: annual, quarterly, monthly, semi-annual, biennial, one-time (single occurrence), ad-hoc (irregular), event-driven (triggered by specific events). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual|quarterly|monthly|semi-annual|biennial|one-time|ad-hoc|event-driven — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority, oversight body, or standard-setting organization that mandates or oversees this obligation (e.g., \'Internal Revenue Service\', \'Charity Commission\', \'OCHA\', \'CHS Alliance\', \'USAID\').']
  grant_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the grant agreement, contract, or Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) that establishes this obligation, if applicable.']
  iati_publication_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation requires publication of data to the IATI Registry for transparency and accountability.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The geographic or organizational scope where this obligation applies (e.g., \'United States\', \'United Kingdom\', \'European Union\', \'Global\', \'State of California\', \'Country Office Kenya\').']
  last_completed_date date [note: 'The date when this obligation was most recently fulfilled or submitted successfully.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The number of days in advance that preparation or submission must begin to meet the obligation deadline.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'The specific law, regulation, standard, or contractual clause that establishes this obligation (e.g., \'IRC Section 501(c)(3)\', \'2 CFR 200.501\', \'CHS Commitment 1\', \'Grant Agreement Clause 12.3\').']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this compliance obligation record was last updated or modified.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'The next scheduled date by which this obligation must be fulfilled or submitted.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, instructions, or context related to this obligation, including special handling requirements or historical context.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Internal or external reference code for the obligation (e.g., \'IRS-990\', \'CC-AR\', \'IATI-PUB\', \'CHS-SA\').']
  obligation_name varchar [note: 'The official name or title of the compliance obligation (e.g., \'IRS Form 990 Annual Return\', \'Charity Commission Annual Return\', \'IATI Publication\').']
  obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the obligation: active (in force and must be fulfilled), pending (upcoming or not yet effective), completed (fulfilled for current period), overdue (past due date and not fulfilled), waived (exempted or not applicable), suspended (temporarily not enforced).']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the obligation by its source or nature: regulatory (government-mandated), donor (funder-specific requirement), voluntary (self-imposed accountability framework), contractual (grant or partnership agreement condition), statutory (legal requirement).']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Description of the consequences, penalties, or sanctions that may result from non-compliance (e.g., \'Loss of tax-exempt status\', \'Fines up to $50,000\', \'Grant suspension\', \'Reputational damage\').']
  responsible_person varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the individual accountable for ensuring this obligation is fulfilled (e.g., \'Chief Financial Officer\', \'Compliance Manager\', \'MEL Director\').']
  responsible_unit varchar [note: 'The department, team, or organizational unit responsible for fulfilling this obligation (e.g., \'Finance Department\', \'Compliance Office\', \'MEL Team\', \'Legal Affairs\', \'Country Office\').']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'The severity of risk to the organization if this obligation is not fulfilled: critical (severe legal or reputational consequences), high (significant impact), medium (moderate impact), low (minimal impact).']
  single_audit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation requires a Single Audit under 2 CFR 200 Subpart F for US federal awards exceeding the threshold.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The channel or mechanism through which the obligation must be fulfilled or submitted (e.g., online portal, email, postal mail, in-person delivery, API, FTP).']
  submission_url varchar [note: 'The web address or portal URL where the obligation must be submitted, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all recurring and one-time compliance obligations the organization must fulfill across regulatory, donor, and voluntary accountability frameworks. Includes IRS 990 filings, Charity Commission returns, IATI publications, CHS self-assessments, Single Audit requirements (2 CFR 200), state registrations, OCHA reporting, and donor-specific conditions. Captures obligation name, governing body, legal basis, frequency, jurisdiction, responsible unit, lead time, and risk rating. Overdue obligations escalate to incident records via obligation_schedule monitoring.'
}

Table compliance.obligation_schedule {
  obligation_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance obligation schedule entry. Primary key for the obligation schedule product.']
  execution_system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Scheduled compliance tasks are executed through specific platforms (e.g., filing system, reporting portal). Nonprofits need this link for workflow automation, access provisioning before due dates, and']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fiscal period (month, quarter, year) to which this obligation schedule applies. Enables period-based compliance tracking.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent compliance obligation that this schedule entry is tracking. Links to the master obligation registry.']
  primary_obligation_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member (compliance officer, finance manager, or program director) assigned primary responsibility for completing this obligation.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Obligation schedules track when compliance filings are due. When the obligation is completed, the schedule should reference the actual regulatory filing that satisfied it. One schedule entry results i']
  tertiary_obligation_assigned_officer_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.staff_member']
  user_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the system user who last modified this obligation schedule entry. Supports accountability and change tracking.']
  actual_effort_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual number of staff hours spent completing this obligation. Used for capacity planning and process improvement. Null if not yet completed.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Overall completion status of the scheduled obligation. Indicates whether the obligation is pending, actively being worked, completed on time, overdue, waived by authority, or deferred to a future period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this obligation schedule entry was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and compliance history tracking.']
  effective_due_date date [note: 'The actual due date in effect for this obligation, taking into account any approved extensions. Equals extended_due_date if extension granted, otherwise equals planned_due_date.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the obligation was escalated to senior management or executive leadership due to risk of non-compliance. Null if no escalation has occurred.']
  escalation_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the effective due date when the obligation should be escalated to senior management if not yet completed. Enables proactive deadline monitoring.']
  escalation_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this obligation has triggered an escalation alert due to approaching deadline or overdue status. True if escalation has been triggered, False otherwise.']
  estimated_effort_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated number of staff hours required to complete this obligation, including data collection, preparation, review, and submission activities.']
  extended_due_date date [note: 'Revised due date if an extension has been granted by the regulatory authority or approved internally. Null if no extension applies.']
  extension_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the requested extension was approved by the regulatory authority. True if extension granted, False if denied or not applicable.']
  extension_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or reason provided when requesting an extension (e.g., \'Awaiting final audit report\', \'Staff transition\', \'System migration delay\').']
  extension_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether an extension has been formally requested from the regulatory authority. True if extension requested, False otherwise.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction or regulatory authority under which this obligation falls (e.g., \'United States - IRS\', \'United Kingdom - Charity Commission\', \'International - IATI\').']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this obligation schedule entry was last updated. Tracks changes to due dates, assignments, status, or other schedule attributes.']
  non_compliance_risk varchar [note: 'Assessment of the organizational risk if this obligation is not met on time. Severe risk includes loss of tax-exempt status, funding restrictions, or legal penalties.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty or fine amount assessed for late or non-compliant submission. Null if no penalty applies or obligation completed on time.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  planned_due_date date [note: 'Original scheduled due date for the compliance obligation as defined by the regulatory authority or internal policy.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this obligation based on regulatory impact, financial risk, and organizational importance. Critical obligations have severe non-compliance penalties.']
  recurrence_pattern varchar [note: 'Frequency pattern for this obligation. Indicates whether it is a one-time requirement, recurring annually, quarterly, monthly, biennially, or triggered by specific events.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory framework or standard governing this obligation (e.g., \'IRS 501(c)(3)\', \'OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200\', \'IATI Standard\', \'CHS\', \'Sphere Standards\').']
  reviewer_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and comments from compliance reviewers, auditors, or executive leadership regarding this obligation schedule entry. Used for quality assurance and process improvement.']
  supporting_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents, attachments, or exhibits required or submitted with this obligation (e.g., financial statements, audit reports, program narratives).']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why this obligation was waived or exempted by the regulatory authority or internal policy (e.g., \'Below revenue threshold\', \'Disaster relief exemption\').']
  workflow_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the compliance workflow process. Tracks progression from initial data collection through final submission and acknowledgment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|data_collection|draft_preparation|internal_review|executive_review|final_approval|submission_ready|submitted|acknowledged — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Operational schedule linking compliance obligations to specific fiscal periods, deadlines, and responsible staff. Tracks planned due date, extended due date (if extension granted), assigned compliance officer, review workflow stage, escalation thresholds, and completion status. Enables proactive compliance calendar management and deadline monitoring across all jurisdictions and reporting frameworks.'
}

This is a require']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Single Audits (OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200) satisfy federal audit obligations for organizations expending $750K+ in federal awards. The obligation table tracks single_audit_required flag. One audit']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the nonprofit organization undergoing the Single Audit.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Single Audit results must be filed with the Federal Audit Clearinghouse (FAC) as a regulatory filing. One Single Audit generates one regulatory filing submission. The filing details (submission date, ']
  source_system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform.   audit_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost paid or payable to the auditor firm for conducting the Single Audit engagement.']

  major_program_count varchar [note: 'Number of federal programs identified as major programs requiring detailed compliance testing under the risk-based approach.']
  material_weakness_identified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the auditor identified any material weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting or compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the Single Audit engagement, including special circumstances, follow-up actions, or internal observations.']
  questioned_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of questioned costs identified by the auditor across all federal programs.']

  significant_deficiency_identified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the auditor identified any significant deficiencies in internal control over financial reporting or compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this Single Audit record was last updated in the system.']

  Note: 'Master record for Single Audit engagements required under OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 for organizations expending $750,000 or more in US federal awards in a fiscal year. Captures audit period, auditor firm, engagement type (financial statement audit, compliance audit), federal expenditure threshold, Schedule of Expenditures of Federal Awards (SEFA) reference, audit opinion type, and submission to the Federal Audit Clearinghouse (FAC).'
}

Table compliance.audit_finding {
  audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for audit_finding']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent audit engagement or Single Audit under which this finding was identified.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific grant or award under which this finding was identified, if applicable.']
  corrective_action_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective action plan developed to address this finding. Links to the detailed remediation plan with responsible parties, timelines, and action steps.']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the federal program under which this finding was identified, if applicable to a specific program.']
  safeguarding_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safeguarding.safeguarding_incident. Business justification: Audit findings document safeguarding incidents discovered during compliance audits (single audit, donor audits). Real business process: audit documentation, questioned costs, and corrective action tra']
  single_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.single_audit. Business justification: Audit findings discovered during Single Audit engagements (OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200) should reference the specific Single Audit. The existing audit_id points to safeguarding.audit (cross-domain)']
  vulnerability_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.vulnerability. Business justification: Compliance audit findings may identify technical vulnerabilities (weak access controls, unpatched systems, data exposure). Nonprofits must link findings to vulnerabilities for integrated risk manageme']
  actual_resolution_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the finding was fully resolved and remediated, as verified by management or subsequent audit. Null if the finding is still open or in remediation.']
  audit_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the audit period during which this finding was identified. Typically the end of the organization\'s fiscal year under audit.']
  audit_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the audit period during which this finding was identified. Typically the beginning of the organization\'s fiscal year under audit.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external audit firm or auditor who identified this finding during the audit engagement.']
  cause_description varchar [note: 'Explanation of the underlying reason or root cause that led to the condition. This identifies why the deficiency occurred (e.g., lack of training, inadequate controls, resource constraints).']
  cfda_number varchar [note: 'The five-digit CFDA number (now known as Assistance Listings number) identifying the federal program under which the finding was identified. Format is XX.XXX where the first two digits represent the federal agency and the last three represent the specific program.']
  compliance_requirement_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of compliance requirement that was violated or not met, as defined in the OMB Compliance Supplement (e.g., Activities Allowed or Unallowed, Allowable Costs/Cost Principles, Cash Management, Eligibility, Equipment and Real Property Management, Matching, Period of Performance, Procurement, Program Income, Reporting, Subrecipient Monitoring, Special Tests and Provisions).']
  condition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the actual condition or deficiency found during the audit. This describes what the auditor observed or identified as problematic.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this audit finding record was first created in the system.']
  criteria_description varchar [note: 'Description of the required standard, regulation, policy, or grant provision that was not met. This establishes the benchmark against which the condition is measured.']
  effect_description varchar [note: 'Description of the actual or potential impact or consequence of the finding. This explains what harm or risk resulted from the condition (e.g., misstated financial statements, unallowable costs charged to grant, noncompliance with donor restrictions).']
  expected_resolution_date date [note: 'The target date by which the organization expects to fully resolve and remediate this finding based on the corrective action plan.']
  fac_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the audit report containing this finding was submitted to the Federal Audit Clearinghouse. Null if not yet submitted.']
  federal_agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the federal agency that provided the funding for the program under which this finding was identified (e.g., USAID, BHA, Department of State, HHS).']
  federal_award_identification_number varchar [note: 'The unique Federal Award Identification Number assigned by the federal agency to the specific award under which this finding was identified.']
  finding_identified_date date [note: 'The date on which the auditor formally identified and documented this finding during the audit fieldwork or reporting process.']
  finding_reference_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique reference number assigned to this audit finding by the auditor, typically following the format YYYY-NNN or similar audit-specific numbering convention.']
  finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit finding. Open indicates newly identified and not yet addressed. In remediation indicates corrective action plan is being implemented. Resolved indicates corrective actions completed and pending verification. Closed indicates finding fully remediated and verified. Repeat finding indicates this issue was identified in a prior audit period.']
  finding_title varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title or summary of the audit finding, providing a high-level overview of the issue identified.']
  finding_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit finding based on severity and nature. Material weakness indicates a deficiency or combination of deficiencies in internal control such that there is a reasonable possibility that a material misstatement will not be prevented or detected. Significant deficiency is less severe than a material weakness but important enough to merit attention. Questioned cost represents costs that do not comply with grant terms. Noncompliance indicates violation of laws, regulations, or grant provisions.']
  is_fraud_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this finding includes indicators of potential fraud, waste, or abuse. True if fraud indicators are present and require further investigation.']
  is_material_weakness boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this finding represents a material weakness in internal control over financial reporting or compliance. True if the finding is classified as a material weakness.']
  is_repeat_finding boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this finding was previously identified in a prior audit period and has not been fully resolved. True if this is a repeat finding.']
  is_significant_deficiency boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this finding represents a significant deficiency in internal control. True if the finding is classified as a significant deficiency.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this audit finding record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  management_response varchar [note: 'The organization\'s management response to the audit finding, including agreement or disagreement with the finding and planned corrective actions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context related to this audit finding, including follow-up actions, communications with auditors, or internal discussions.']
  prior_finding_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the prior audit finding if this is a repeat finding. Links to the original finding from a previous audit period.']
  questioned_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount of costs questioned by the auditor due to noncompliance with grant terms, unallowable costs, or lack of supporting documentation. Null if the finding does not involve questioned costs.']
  questioned_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the questioned cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  recommendation_description varchar [note: 'Auditor\'s recommendation for corrective action to address the finding. This provides guidance on steps the organization should take to remediate the deficiency.']
  reported_to_federal_audit_clearinghouse boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this finding has been reported to the Federal Audit Clearinghouse as part of the Single Audit submission. True if reported to FAC.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'The internal department or functional area responsible for addressing and remediating this finding (e.g., Finance, Program Operations, Grants Management, Human Resources).']
  responsible_person_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual assigned primary responsibility for implementing the corrective action plan and resolving this finding.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of risk this finding represents (e.g., Financial Risk, Compliance Risk, Operational Risk, Reputational Risk, Fraud Risk).']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Internal classification of the finding\'s severity based on organizational risk assessment. Critical findings pose immediate risk to funding or compliance. High findings require urgent attention. Medium findings should be addressed in the normal course. Low findings are minor issues.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each finding, material weakness, significant deficiency, or questioned cost identified during a Single Audit or internal compliance audit. Captures finding reference number, finding type (material weakness, significant deficiency, questioned cost, noncompliance), federal program CFDA number, finding description, condition, criteria, cause, effect, and recommendation. Links to corrective action plans.'
}

These improvement actions are formalized as corrective action plans. One CAP ']
  implementing_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.change_request. Business justification: Corrective actions for compliance findings often require system changes (access controls, workflow modifications, reporting enhancements). Nonprofits must link CAPs to change requests for implementati']
  internal_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.internal_review. Business justification: Internal compliance reviews generate findings that require corrective action plans. One CAP addresses one internal review finding; one review may generate multiple CAPs (one per finding). This paralle']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or project associated with the finding, if the corrective action relates to program operations or service delivery.']
  modified_by_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the corrective action plan record.']
  primary_corrective_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or staff member accountable for implementing the corrective action plan.']
  safeguarding_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safeguarding.safeguarding_incident. Business justification: CAPs remediate safeguarding incidents (SEA, abuse, exploitation). Real business process: incident response workflow requires documented corrective actions for donor reporting and organizational learni']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the corrective action plan record.']
  tertiary_corrective_responsible_manager_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.staff_member']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the corrective action plan was fully implemented and verified as complete. Null if still in progress.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual financial cost incurred to implement the corrective action plan. Null if not yet completed or cost not tracked.']
  audit_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit report document that contains the original finding, including report number and page reference.']
  cap_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the corrective action plan, typically formatted as CAP-YYYY-NNN or similar organizational convention.']
  cap_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the corrective action plan, tracking progress from initiation through closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|in_progress|pending_verification|closed|overdue|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the specific corrective actions to be taken to address the finding, including process changes, policy updates, training, or system enhancements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the corrective action plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for estimated and actual costs.']
  donor_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the donor or funding agency was notified of the finding and corrective action plan. Null if notification not required or not yet sent.']
  donor_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the donor or funding agency must be notified of the finding and corrective action plan per grant agreement terms.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action plan was escalated to higher authority. Null if no escalation occurred.']
  escalation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the corrective action plan requires escalation to senior management, board of directors, or external parties due to severity or complexity.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial cost to implement the corrective action plan, including staff time, system changes, training, and other resources.']
  finding_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit finding, CHS non-conformity, or compliance issue that triggered this corrective action plan.']
  finding_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number from the audit report, CHS verification report, or donor compliance review that documented the original finding.']
  finding_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the finding as assessed by auditors or compliance reviewers, indicating urgency and risk exposure.']
  finding_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the underlying issue that necessitated the corrective action plan.']
  management_response varchar [note: 'Official management response to the finding, documenting agreement or disagreement with the finding and planned corrective actions.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the corrective action plan record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context related to the corrective action plan, including progress updates, challenges encountered, or lessons learned.']
  preventive_measures varchar [note: 'Description of preventive measures implemented to reduce the risk of recurrence, such as policy changes, training programs, or system controls.']
  recurrence_risk varchar [note: 'Assessment of the risk that the finding could recur if corrective actions are not sustained or are inadequately implemented.']
  regulatory_reporting_date date [note: 'Date when the finding and corrective action plan were reported to regulatory authorities. Null if reporting not required or not yet submitted.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finding and corrective action plan must be reported to regulatory authorities such as IRS, Charity Commission, or OCHA.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department or organizational unit responsible for executing the corrective action plan.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Analysis of the underlying root cause(s) that led to the finding, supporting effective remediation and prevention of recurrence.']
  sphere_standard_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Sphere Humanitarian Charter or Minimum Standard that was not met, if the finding relates to humanitarian quality standards.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the corrective action plan is expected to be fully implemented and closed.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was verified as complete and effective by auditors, compliance officers, or management.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the corrective action has been effectively implemented and the finding has been remediated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: document_review|site_visit|testing|management_attestation|external_audit|internal_audit|chs_verification — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  verification_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes from the verification process, documenting evidence reviewed and conclusions reached regarding corrective action effectiveness.']

  Note: 'Master record of corrective action plans (CAPs) developed in response to audit findings, CHS non-conformities, donor compliance issues, or internal control deficiencies. Captures finding reference, corrective action description, responsible manager, target completion date, actual completion date, verification method, and status (open, in progress, closed, overdue). Supports management response documentation required by 2 CFR 200 and CHS Alliance.'
}

Table compliance.chs_self_assessment {
  chs_self_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the CHS self-assessment cycle record.']
  assessment_system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: CHS self-assessments use specific platforms for data collection, evidence management, and verification (quality management systems, survey tools). Nonprofits must track which platform houses assessmen']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the country office that is the subject of this assessment, if the scope is country_office.']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the program that is the subject of this assessment, if the scope is program.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: CHS (Core Humanitarian Standard) self-assessments satisfy CHS compliance obligations. The obligation table tracks chs_self_assessment_required flag. One assessment satisfies one obligation instance; o']
  psea_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safeguarding.psea_policy. Business justification: CHS Commitment 5 requires PSEA policy implementation and assessment. Real business process: humanitarian standards compliance verification where CHS self-assessments evaluate organizational PSEA polic']
  assessment_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the self-assessment cycle, typically formatted as CHS-[Country]-[Year]-[Sequence].']
  assessment_methodology varchar [note: 'The methodology used to conduct the CHS self-assessment: internal self-assessment (staff-led), peer review (peer organization review), independent verification (third-party auditor), hybrid (combination of methods).']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'General notes, observations, and contextual information about the self-assessment process and findings.']
  assessment_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period being assessed for CHS conformity.']
  assessment_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period being assessed for CHS conformity.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the self-assessment: planned (scheduled but not started), in_progress (data collection underway), under_review (internal review), completed (finalized), verified (third-party verified), archived (historical record).']
  assessment_team_members varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of names of team members who participated in the self-assessment process.']
  certification_target_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this self-assessment is being conducted as part of a formal CHS certification process (True) or for internal improvement purposes only (False).']
  commitment_1_assessor_notes varchar [note: 'Assessor observations, findings, and rationale for the conformity rating assigned to CHS Commitment 1.']
  commitment_1_evidence varchar [note: 'Summary of evidence provided to support the conformity rating for CHS Commitment 1, including documentation references and examples.']
  commitment_1_improvement_actions varchar [note: 'Recommended improvement actions and corrective measures to enhance conformity with CHS Commitment 1.']
  commitment_1_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Conformity rating for CHS Commitment 1 (Humanitarian assistance is appropriate and relevant): numeric score from 1.00 (non-conforming) to 5.00 (fully conforming).']
  commitment_2_assessor_notes varchar [note: 'Assessor observations, findings, and rationale for the conformity rating assigned to CHS Commitment 2.']
  commitment_2_evidence varchar [note: 'Summary of evidence provided to support the conformity rating for CHS Commitment 2, including documentation references and examples.']
  commitment_2_improvement_actions varchar [note: 'Recommended improvement actions and corrective measures to enhance conformity with CHS Commitment 2.']
  commitment_2_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Conformity rating for CHS Commitment 2 (Humanitarian response is effective and timely): numeric score from 1.00 (non-conforming) to 5.00 (fully conforming).']
  commitment_3_assessor_notes varchar [note: 'Assessor observations, findings, and rationale for the conformity rating assigned to CHS Commitment 3.']
  commitment_3_evidence varchar [note: 'Summary of evidence provided to support the conformity rating for CHS Commitment 3, including documentation references and examples.']
  commitment_3_improvement_actions varchar [note: 'Recommended improvement actions and corrective measures to enhance conformity with CHS Commitment 3.']
  commitment_3_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Conformity rating for CHS Commitment 3 (Communities and people affected by crisis are not negatively affected and are more prepared, resilient and less at-risk): numeric score from 1.00 (non-conforming) to 5.00 (fully conforming).']

  commitment_4_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Conformity rating for CHS Commitment 4 (Communication with communities and people affected by crisis is frequent, accessible and two-way): numeric score from 1.00 (non-conforming) to 5.00 (fully conforming).']
  commitment_5_assessor_notes varchar [note: 'Assessor observations, findings, and rationale for the conformity rating assigned to CHS Commitment 5.']
  commitment_5_evidence varchar [note: 'Summary of evidence provided to support the conformity rating for CHS Commitment 5, including documentation references and examples.']

  commitment_5_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Conformity rating for CHS Commitment 5 (Complaints are welcomed and addressed): numeric score from 1.00 (non-conforming) to 5.00 (fully conforming).']
  commitment_6_assessor_notes varchar [note: 'Assessor observations, findings, and rationale for the conformity rating assigned to CHS Commitment 6.']
  commitment_6_evidence varchar [note: 'Summary of evidence provided to support the conformity rating for CHS Commitment 6, including documentation references and examples.']

  commitment_6_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Conformity rating for CHS Commitment 6 (Humanitarian response is coordinated and complementary): numeric score from 1.00 (non-conforming) to 5.00 (fully conforming).']
  commitment_7_assessor_notes varchar [note: 'Assessor observations, findings, and rationale for the conformity rating assigned to CHS Commitment 7.']
  commitment_7_evidence varchar [note: 'Summary of evidence provided to support the conformity rating for CHS Commitment 7, including documentation references and examples.']
  commitment_7_improvement_actions varchar [note: 'Recommended improvement actions and corrective measures to enhance conformity with CHS Commitment 7.']
  commitment_7_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Conformity rating for CHS Commitment 7 (Humanitarian actors continuously learn and improve): numeric score from 1.00 (non-conforming) to 5.00 (fully conforming).']
