Business justification: Safeguarding incidents occurring under partnership agreements must be tracked for partner performance reviews, donor reporting obligations, and agreement renewal decisions. Essential for accountabilit']
  survivor_registrant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to beneficiary.registrant. Business justification: Safeguarding incidents often involve beneficiaries as survivors/witnesses. Linking incident to registrant enables integrated protection case management, coordinates with protection_flag tracking, ensu']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.warehouse. Business justification: Safeguarding incidents frequently occur at warehouse facilities (staff misconduct, beneficiary contact at storage sites). Required for incident location tracking, facility-specific risk assessment, an']
  alleged_perpetrator_name varchar [note: 'The name of the alleged perpetrator, if known. Highly sensitive and access-restricted during investigation.']
  alleged_perpetrator_type varchar [note: 'The category of the alleged perpetrator: staff member, volunteer, partner organization personnel, contractor, beneficiary, external party, or unknown. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: staff|volunteer|partner|contractor|beneficiary|external|unknown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  anonymization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident report has been anonymized to protect the identity of the reporter or survivor. True if anonymized, False if identifiable information is retained.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Description of organizational corrective actions, policy changes, or systemic improvements implemented in response to this incident to prevent recurrence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this safeguarding incident record was first created in the system.']
  disciplinary_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of disciplinary or corrective action taken as a result of the investigation (e.g., termination, suspension, warning, training, policy change).']
  donor_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether donor notification is required per grant agreement terms for this type and severity of safeguarding incident. True if notification required, False otherwise.']
  donor_notified_date date [note: 'The date when the donor was formally notified of the safeguarding incident, if notification was required.']
  incident_date date [note: 'The date when the safeguarding incident occurred or is alleged to have occurred. This is the business event date, distinct from reporting date.']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the safeguarding incident, including circumstances, alleged actions, and context. Highly sensitive and access-restricted.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique reference number for the safeguarding incident, used for tracking and communication with stakeholders.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the safeguarding incident in the investigation and resolution workflow.']
  incident_time timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the safeguarding incident occurred, if known. Provides time-of-day precision beyond the incident date.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the safeguarding incident: Sexual Exploitation and Abuse (SEA), child safeguarding violations, harassment, fraud, staff misconduct, or other protection breaches.']
  investigation_completion_date date [note: 'The date when the investigation was completed and findings were documented.']
  investigation_outcome varchar [note: 'The final outcome of the investigation: substantiated (evidence supports allegation), unsubstantiated (insufficient evidence), inconclusive, withdrawn by reporter, or pending completion.']
  investigation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal investigation is required for this incident. True if investigation needed, False if incident can be resolved through other means.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'The date when the formal investigation of the safeguarding incident was initiated.']
  investigator_name varchar [note: 'The name of the lead investigator assigned to this safeguarding incident case.']
  law_enforcement_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether local law enforcement or legal authorities were notified of this incident. True if notified, False otherwise.']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Key lessons learned from this safeguarding incident and its investigation, used for organizational learning and safeguarding policy improvement.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the safeguarding incident occurred.']
  location_district varchar [note: 'The district or locality where the incident occurred, providing more granular geographic context.']
  location_region varchar [note: 'The administrative region, province, or state where the incident occurred within the country.']
  location_site varchar [note: 'The specific site, facility, or field location where the incident occurred (e.g., distribution center, health clinic, office, camp).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this safeguarding incident record was last updated or modified.']
  referral_made_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the survivor or case was referred to external services (health, legal, protection, psychosocial support). True if referral made, False otherwise.']
  reported_date date [note: 'The date when the safeguarding incident was first reported to the organization through any channel.']
  reporter_contact varchar [note: 'Contact information (phone or email) for the reporter, if provided and consent given for follow-up communication.']
  reporter_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the person who reported the safeguarding incident, if known and consent provided. May be null for anonymous reports.']
  reporter_type varchar [note: 'The category of person who reported the incident: beneficiary, staff member, volunteer, partner organization staff, community member, or anonymous reporter.']
  reporting_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or mechanism through which the safeguarding incident was reported (hotline, direct report to manager, community feedback mechanism, email, anonymous reporting box, third-party reporting).']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Initial severity classification of the safeguarding incident based on potential harm, organizational risk, and urgency of response required.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Detailed sub-classification of the incident type providing additional specificity (e.g., sexual harassment, physical abuse, financial fraud, conflict of interest).']
  survivor_age_group varchar [note: 'Age group classification of the survivor to support child safeguarding analysis without revealing precise age. Used for statistical reporting and risk assessment.']
  survivor_gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the survivor, used for Gender-Based Violence (GBV) analysis and safeguarding pattern identification.']
  survivor_involved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether there is an identified survivor or victim involved in this safeguarding incident. True if survivor identified, False if no survivor or incident does not involve a victim.']
  survivor_support_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether survivor support services (medical, psychosocial, legal, safety) were provided. True if support provided, False otherwise.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record for every reported safeguarding incident including sexual exploitation and abuse (SEA), child safeguarding violations, harassment, staff misconduct, and protection breaches. Captures incident date, location, incident type (SEA, child abuse, harassment, fraud, other), reporting channel (hotline, direct report, community feedback), initial severity classification, anonymization flag, and current investigation status. SSOT for all safeguarding incident intake across the organization.'
}

Table safeguarding.investigation {
  investigation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the safeguarding investigation record. Primary key.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: Investigations of safeguarding incidents in grant contexts require award linkage for donor notification obligations, compliance with special award conditions, and tracking investigation costs against ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Investigations are conducted by specific departments/units represented as cost centers. Investigation costs (staff time, travel, external investigators) must be allocated to the responsible cost cente']
  donor_constituent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.constituent. Business justification: Investigations may involve donors as subjects or witnesses. Critical for managing reputational risk, informing gift acceptance decisions, and coordinating between safeguarding and development teams wh']
  investigation_last_modified_by_user_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the investigation record.']
  investigation_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created the investigation record in the system.']
  linked_investigation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on investigation (linked_investigation_id)']
  safeguarding_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the safeguarding incident that triggered this investigation. Links to the incident intake record.']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or external investigator assigned as the lead for this investigation.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.warehouse. Business justification: Investigations of warehouse-based safeguarding incidents require facility-specific context. Required for facility security reviews, staff conduct investigations at storage sites, and warehouse-level c']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the investigation was completed and final findings were documented. Null if investigation is still in progress.']
  closure_notes varchar [note: 'Final notes and comments recorded at the time of investigation closure, including any outstanding issues, limitations, or contextual information.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification level for the investigation record and findings: public (anonymized summary), internal (staff access), confidential (management only), or restricted (need-to-know basis).']
  cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of conducting the investigation in USD, including investigator fees, travel, translation, legal counsel, and administrative expenses. Confidential business information.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the investigation record was first created in the system.']
  disciplinary_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of disciplinary actions taken against subjects of the investigation, including warnings, suspension, termination, or referral to law enforcement. Confidential business information.']
  evidence_items_collected_count varchar [note: 'Total number of evidence items collected and catalogued during the investigation, including documents, photographs, recordings, and physical evidence.']
  external_reporting_date date [note: 'Date when investigation findings were reported to external parties, if required. Null if no external reporting was required or if not yet reported.']
  external_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigation findings must be reported to external parties such as donors, UN agencies, law enforcement, or regulatory bodies.']
  final_determination varchar [note: 'Final determination of the investigation: substantiated (allegations proven), unsubstantiated (allegations not proven), inconclusive (insufficient evidence), or partially substantiated (some allegations proven).']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Executive summary of the investigation findings, including key facts established, credibility assessments, and analysis of evidence. Confidential business information.']
  follow_up_actions_required varchar [note: 'Description of ongoing follow-up actions required after investigation closure, including monitoring of corrective actions, policy implementation, or survivor support continuation.']
  interviews_conducted_count varchar [note: 'Total number of formal interviews conducted during the investigation, including complainants, witnesses, subjects, and key informants.']
  investigation_category varchar [note: 'Primary safeguarding category under investigation: Protection from Sexual Exploitation and Abuse (PSEA), child safeguarding, staff misconduct, Gender-Based Violence (GBV), fraud, or harassment.']
  investigation_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the boundaries and focus areas of the investigation, including specific allegations, time periods, locations, and individuals involved.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the investigation: pending assignment of investigator, active investigation in progress, suspended pending additional information, completed with findings, or closed.']
  investigation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigation approach: internal (conducted by organizational staff), external (third-party investigator), joint with UN/OCHA, joint with partner organization, or independent external review.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the investigation record was last updated or modified.']
  law_enforcement_referral_date date [note: 'Date when the case was referred to law enforcement authorities. Null if no referral was made.']
  law_enforcement_referral_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigation findings were referred to law enforcement or criminal justice authorities for potential prosecution.']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Key lessons learned from the investigation process and findings, used to inform organizational learning, policy updates, and prevention strategies.']
  methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the investigative approach and methods used, including interviews, document review, site visits, forensic analysis, and evidence collection procedures.']
  policy_violations_identified varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specific organizational policies, codes of conduct, or standards that were found to have been violated during the investigation.']
  recommended_actions varchar [note: 'Detailed recommendations for corrective actions, disciplinary measures, policy changes, or systemic improvements based on investigation findings. Confidential business information.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number for the investigation, used in all official documentation and correspondence. Format: INV-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  report_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the formal investigation report document stored in the document management system. Confidential business information.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the formal investigation was officially opened and investigative activities commenced.']
  survivor_support_provided varchar [note: 'Description of support services provided to survivors or complainants, including Psychosocial Support (PSS), medical care, legal assistance, and safe accommodation.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned or target date by which the investigation is expected to be completed and findings delivered, based on organizational policy or donor requirements.']
  team_members varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of staff IDs or names of additional team members supporting the investigation, including subject matter experts, translators, and support staff.']
  terms_of_reference varchar [note: 'Formal Terms of Reference (ToR) document outlining the investigation mandate, objectives, methodology, timeline, reporting requirements, and confidentiality protocols.']

  Note: 'Master record for each formal safeguarding investigation opened in response to a reported incident. Captures investigation reference number, investigation type (internal, external, joint with UN/OCHA), lead investigator assignment, investigation scope, terms of reference (ToR), start date, target completion date, actual completion date, findings summary, and final determination (substantiated, unsubstantiated, inconclusive). Distinct from the incident intake record — represents the formal investigative process lifecycle.'
}

Table safeguarding.investigation_action {
  investigation_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each investigation action record. Primary key for the investigation action entity.']
  alleged_perpetrator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safeguarding.alleged_perpetrator. Business justification: Investigation actions often target or involve specific alleged perpetrators (interviews, evidence collection, interim measures). Currently investigation_action only links to investigation but not to t']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Individual investigation actions (interviews, site visits, evidence collection) incur direct costs tracked via action_cost_amount. These costs must be allocated to the cost center conducting the actio']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent safeguarding investigation case to which this action belongs. Links action to the overarching investigation.']
  predecessor_investigation_action_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on investigation_action (predecessor_investigation_action_id)']
  primary_investigation_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or investigator responsible for executing this investigation action. Links to workforce or investigator master data.']
  primary_investigation_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created this investigation action record. Supports accountability and audit trail.']
  survivor_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safeguarding.survivor_record. Business justification: Investigation actions frequently involve survivors (witness interviews, support coordination, consent collection). Currently investigation_action has witness_statement_flag and survivor_support_provid']
  action_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Direct cost incurred for this investigation action (e.g., travel expenses, expert consultation fees, translation services). Supports budget tracking and financial accountability.']
  action_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the action cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  action_date date [note: 'The date on which the investigation action was performed or is scheduled to be performed. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this action.']
  action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the investigation action taken. Captures the specifics of what was done, who was involved, and any relevant context.']
  action_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the investigation action was completed. Used for duration calculation and workflow tracking.']
  action_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the investigation action took place (e.g., field office, remote interview, headquarters, community site).']
  action_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the investigation action, typically formatted as a sequential or hierarchical code within the investigation (e.g., INV-2024-001-A01).']
  action_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the investigation action commenced. Used for detailed audit trail and duration tracking.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the investigation action. Tracks whether the action is planned, in progress, completed, cancelled, deferred, or pending review.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigation action taken. Defines the nature of the investigative step performed (e.g., interview conducted, evidence collected, witness statement taken, referral made, interim measure applied). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: interview_conducted|evidence_collected|witness_statement_taken|referral_made|interim_measure_applied|site_visit|document_review|expert_consultation|risk_assessment|follow_up_action — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the investigation action was formally approved. Supports audit trail and compliance verification.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this investigation action requires formal approval before execution or closure. True if approval is needed, false otherwise.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes documenting compliance with organizational safeguarding policies, donor requirements, or regulatory standards during this action.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for this investigation action record. Determines access controls and handling requirements (public, internal, confidential, restricted).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this investigation action record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the investigation action in minutes. Calculated from start and end timestamps or manually recorded for resource tracking and workload analysis.']
  evidence_collected_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether physical or documentary evidence was collected during this investigation action. True if evidence was gathered, false otherwise.']
  evidence_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for evidence collected during this action. Links to evidence management system or document repository.']
  external_authority_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether external authorities (e.g., law enforcement, regulatory bodies, United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA)) were notified as part of this action.']
  interim_measure_applied varchar [note: 'Description of any interim protective or administrative measures applied during this action (e.g., suspension, reassignment, access restriction, no-contact order).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this investigation action record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data governance.']
  next_action_description varchar [note: 'Brief description of the next planned action or step in the investigation workflow. Provides continuity and planning context.']
  next_action_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the next investigation action or follow-up step is due. Supports case management workflow and ensures timely progression of the investigation.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which external authorities or stakeholders were notified. Supports regulatory compliance and audit trail.']
  outcome_notes varchar [note: 'Confidential notes documenting the outcome, findings, or results of the investigation action. Captures key observations, evidence gathered, or conclusions reached.']
  participant_count varchar [note: 'Number of individuals who participated in or were present during the investigation action (e.g., number of interviewees, witnesses, or observers).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the investigation action. Indicates urgency and resource allocation needs (critical, high, medium, low).']
  referral_made_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a referral to external services or authorities was made as part of this action (e.g., referral to Psychosocial Support (PSS), legal services, or law enforcement).']
  referral_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or service provider to which a referral was made (e.g., Civil Society Organization (CSO), Community-Based Organization (CBO), government agency).']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team responsible for the investigation action. Provides human-readable identification of the action owner.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk assessment classification for this investigation action. Indicates potential harm, reputational risk, or safety concerns associated with the action.']
  survivor_support_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether survivor-centered support services were provided during or as a result of this investigation action (e.g., Psychosocial Support (PSS), medical care, legal assistance).']
  witness_statement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal witness statement was taken during this investigation action. True if statement was recorded, false otherwise.']

  Note: 'Transactional log of individual actions, steps, and milestones taken within a safeguarding investigation. Captures action type (interview conducted, evidence collected, witness statement taken, referral made, interim measure applied), action date, responsible party, outcome notes, and next action due date. Provides the operational audit trail for investigation progress and supports case management workflows.'
}

Table safeguarding.survivor_record {
  survivor_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the survivor record. Primary key for confidential safeguarding case tracking.']
  beneficiary_registrant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to beneficiary.registrant. Business justification: When consent and confidentiality protocols permit, linking survivor_record to registrant enables integrated case management across safeguarding and program systems, coordinates protection flags, ensur']
  duplicate_of_survivor_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on survivor_record (duplicate_of_survivor_record_id)']
  primary_survivor_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member assigned as the primary support focal point for this survivor. Responsible for coordinating survivor-centered support services and case management.']
  safeguarding_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the safeguarding incident record associated with this survivor. Links survivor support to the formal incident investigation and response.']
  survivor_created_by_staff_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who created this survivor record. Supports accountability and audit trail for sensitive data handling.']
  survivor_last_modified_by_staff_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who last modified this survivor record. Supports accountability and audit trail for sensitive data handling.']
  access_restriction_level varchar [note: 'Level of access control applied to this survivor record. Determines which staff roles can view or edit the record, enforcing need-to-know principle.']
  age_group varchar [note: 'Age bracket of the survivor at the time of incident reporting. Stored as range rather than exact age to reduce re-identification risk while enabling age-appropriate support planning.']
  case_closure_notes varchar [note: 'Confidential notes documenting the circumstances and outcomes at case closure. Supports case management quality assurance and learning.']
  case_closure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for closing the survivor support case. Provides accountability and learning for case management quality.']
  confidentiality_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a confidentiality breach has been identified in the handling of this survivor\'s case. Triggers corrective action and accountability measures.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date when the survivor\'s consent status was documented or last updated. Essential for audit trail and ensuring time-bound consent validity.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current status of survivor\'s consent for information sharing with internal teams, external partners, or authorities. Governs data access and case coordination boundaries per survivor-centered approach.']
  data_retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this survivor record is scheduled for review or deletion per data retention policy. Ensures compliance with data minimization and survivor privacy obligations.']
  economic_support_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether economic assistance (cash, livelihood support, etc.) has been provided to the survivor. Supports survivor resilience and recovery.']
  external_referral_made boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the survivor has been referred to external service providers (health, legal, psychosocial, etc.). Tracks multi-agency coordination.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self-identified gender of the survivor. Critical for Gender-Based Violence (GBV) analysis and ensuring gender-sensitive support services.']
  legal_support_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether legal assistance or referral to legal services has been provided to the survivor. Tracks access to justice pathways.']
  medical_support_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether medical or health services have been provided or facilitated for the survivor. Essential for comprehensive survivor care tracking.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'Survivor\'s preferred method for follow-up communication. Respects survivor autonomy and safety considerations in contact planning.']
  psychosocial_support_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Psychosocial Support (PSS) services have been provided to the survivor as part of the response. Critical for tracking holistic survivor care.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survivor record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data governance and case timeline tracking.']
  record_last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survivor record was last updated. Critical for audit trail and ensuring data currency in active cases.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Channel or source through which the survivor case was reported or referred to the safeguarding team. Important for understanding reporting pathways and community awareness.']
  safe_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address designated by survivor as safe for contact. Used only with explicit consent and when email is the preferred contact method.']
  safe_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number designated by survivor as safe for contact. May differ from primary phone to protect confidentiality and safety.']
  safety_plan_in_place boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal safety plan has been developed and agreed with the survivor. Critical for survivor protection and risk mitigation.']
  support_end_date date [note: 'Date when survivor support services were concluded or case was closed. Nullable for ongoing support cases.']
  support_start_date date [note: 'Date when formal survivor support services were initiated. Marks the beginning of the organization\'s survivor-centered response.']
  support_status varchar [note: 'Current stage of survivor support services. Tracks progression through the survivor-centered response pathway from initial contact through case closure.']
  survivor_code varchar [note: 'Pseudonymized or anonymized identifier assigned to protect survivor identity while enabling case tracking across systems. Used in place of real name to maintain confidentiality per PSEA protocols.']
  survivor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the survivor\'s relationship to the organization at the time of the incident. Determines applicable safeguarding protocols and support pathways.']
  vulnerability_notes varchar [note: 'Confidential notes documenting specific vulnerabilities, risks, or special considerations for this survivor. Used to inform tailored support planning and risk mitigation.']

  Note: 'Confidential master record for each survivor or alleged victim involved in a safeguarding incident. Captures anonymized or pseudonymized identity (survivor code), age group, gender, survivor type (beneficiary, staff, community member, child), consent status for information sharing, preferred contact method, assigned survivor support focal point, and current support status. Maintained separately from beneficiary and staff records to enforce strict data access controls and confidentiality obligations under PSEA standards.'
}

Table safeguarding.survivor_support_plan {
  survivor_support_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the survivor support plan record. Primary key.']

  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Support plans are managed by specific organizational units (protection teams, case management units) tracked as cost centers. Costs for delivering support services must be allocated to the responsible']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the country office responsible for overseeing this survivor support plan and coordinating local service delivery.']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Survivor support services (psychosocial, medical, legal, housing) are funded from specific restricted or unrestricted funds. Donors require detailed tracking of fund usage for survivor assistance to e']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the safeguarding or protection program under which this survivor support plan is managed and funded.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary organization or partner responsible for delivering support services to the survivor.']
  primary_survivor_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff user who initially created this survivor support plan record.']
  revised_survivor_support_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on survivor_support_plan (revised_survivor_support_plan_id)']
  safeguarding_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the safeguarding incident that triggered this support plan.']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary case manager or social worker responsible for coordinating the survivor support plan and ensuring continuity of care.']
  survivor_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the survivor beneficiary receiving support services. Confidential identifier linking to beneficiary registry.']
  closure_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes documenting the circumstances and outcomes at the time of plan closure, including final status of services and any follow-up recommendations.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for closing or completing the survivor support plan, documenting the circumstances of plan conclusion.']

  consent_date date [note: 'Date when informed consent was obtained from the survivor for the support plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survivor support plan record was first created in the system.']
  education_service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of education support services to be provided, including school enrollment assistance, tutoring, vocational training, and educational materials.']
  education_support_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether educational services and support are included in the survivor support plan.']
  housing_service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of safe housing services to be provided, including temporary shelter, relocation assistance, or long-term housing support.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this survivor support plan record was most recently updated or modified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the survivor support plan was most recently reviewed and updated.']

  livelihood_support_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether economic empowerment and livelihood assistance services are included in the survivor support plan.']
  medical_referral_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether medical care or health services referral is included in the survivor support plan.']
  medical_service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of medical services to be provided or referred, including clinical care, reproductive health services, and ongoing treatment needs.']
  needs_assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the comprehensive needs assessment was conducted with the survivor to identify required support services.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of the survivor support plan with the survivor and service providers.']
  plan_end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when the survivor support plan is scheduled to conclude or was completed.']
  plan_goals varchar [note: 'Documented goals and objectives of the survivor support plan as agreed with the survivor, outlining desired outcomes and recovery milestones.']
  plan_reference_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique reference number for the survivor support plan, formatted as SSP-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  plan_start_date date [note: 'Date when the survivor support plan becomes active and service delivery begins.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the survivor support plan. Tracks progression from initial draft through active service delivery to completion.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the support plan based on scope and duration of services provided.']
  progress_notes varchar [note: 'Ongoing narrative documentation of progress toward plan goals, service delivery updates, and any adjustments made to the support plan.']
  pss_service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of psychosocial support services to be provided, including counseling, peer support groups, and mental health interventions.']
  psychosocial_support_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether psychosocial support services are included in the survivor support plan.']
  referral_pathway_used varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the inter-agency referral pathway or service directory used to connect the survivor with appropriate service providers.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for reviewing and updating the survivor support plan to ensure services remain appropriate and effective.']
  safe_housing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether safe shelter or housing assistance is included in the survivor support plan.']
  safety_plan_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a personalized safety plan has been developed and included as part of the survivor support plan.']
  survivor_centered_approach boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the support plan was developed using survivor-centered principles, prioritizing the survivors autonomy, safety, and expressed needs.']
  survivor_consent_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether informed consent was obtained from the survivor for the support plan and associated service delivery.']

  Note: 'Structured support plan developed for each survivor following a safeguarding incident. Captures support needs assessment findings, agreed support services (psychosocial support, medical referral, legal aid, safe housing, livelihood support), service provider assignments, plan start and end dates, review schedule, and plan status. Tracks the holistic survivor-centered response and ensures continuity of care across service providers.'
}

Table safeguarding.support_service_referral {
  support_service_referral_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the support service referral record. Primary key for this entity.']
  case_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the safeguarding case record that generated this referral. Links the referral to the originating incident or survivor case.']
  onward_support_service_referral_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on support_service_referral (onward_support_service_referral_id)']
  registrant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the survivor or beneficiary being referred. Identifies the individual receiving support services.']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or case worker who initiated the referral. Used for accountability and follow-up coordination.']
  survivor_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safeguarding.survivor_record. Business justification: support_service_referral currently links to beneficiary.registrant (general beneficiary record) and beneficiary.case_record (general case management). However, in the safeguarding domain, survivors ha']
  actual_service_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the survivor began receiving the referred service. Null if service has not yet started.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification of confidentiality requirements for this referral. Safeguarding referrals typically require enhanced confidentiality controls.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date when informed consent was obtained from the survivor for this referral. Null if consent was not obtained or not applicable.']
  consent_method varchar [note: 'Method by which consent was obtained. Distinguishes between verbal, written, digital signature, or guardian consent for minors.']
  consent_obtained_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether informed consent was obtained from the survivor before making the referral. Critical for ethical safeguarding practice.']
  cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of the referred service if applicable. Used for budgeting and financial planning. Null if service is provided at no cost.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the referral was made and service will be delivered. Used for geographic reporting and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this referral record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost estimate. Null if no cost is associated with the referral.']
  decline_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided if the referral was declined by the service provider or the survivor. Used for learning and pathway improvement.']
  expected_service_start_date date [note: 'Anticipated date when the survivor will begin receiving the referred service. Used for planning and follow-up.']
  follow_up_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scheduled follow-up was completed. Used for monitoring and accountability.']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for follow-up contact with the survivor or service provider to assess progress and outcomes.']
  follow_up_outcome varchar [note: 'Summary of the follow-up findings, including survivor feedback and service delivery status. Confidential survivor information.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Identification of the grant, donor, or budget line covering the cost of the referred service. Used for financial tracking and donor reporting.']
  information_sharing_consent varchar [note: 'Level of consent provided by the survivor for sharing their information with the service provider. Governs what details can be disclosed.']
  interagency_referral_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this referral is part of an interagency coordination mechanism or referral pathway. Used for humanitarian coordination reporting.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last updated this referral record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this referral record was last updated in the system. Audit trail for record modifications.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Name of the city, district, or field location where the service will be provided. Supports geographic tracking and coordination.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional confidential notes or context about the referral. May include special considerations, survivor preferences, or coordination details.']
  receiving_organization_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external or internal organization receiving the referral and providing the service.']
  receiving_organization_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service provider organization. Distinguishes between internal departments, partner organizations, government services, and other provider types.']
  referral_channel varchar [note: 'Communication channel or mechanism used to transmit the referral to the service provider.']
  referral_date date [note: 'The date when the referral was formally made to the service provider. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  referral_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the referral. Used for tracking and communication with service providers.']
  referral_pathway_name varchar [note: 'Name of the formal referral pathway or protocol being followed. Common in coordinated humanitarian response contexts.']
  referral_reason varchar [note: 'Brief explanation of why the referral was made and what needs the service is intended to address. Confidential case information.']
  referral_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the referral. Tracks progression from initial referral through service delivery completion or closure.']
  referral_urgency varchar [note: 'Priority level of the referral indicating how quickly the survivor needs to access the service. Guides provider response time.']
  referring_department varchar [note: 'Department or program unit within the organization that initiated the referral. Used for internal coordination and reporting.']
  service_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the referred service was completed or concluded. Null if service is ongoing or not yet started.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific support service or intervention being referred. Provides context for the receiving provider.']
  service_provider_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific individual or focal point at the receiving organization responsible for this referral.']
  service_provider_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the service provider or focal point. Used for formal referral documentation and communication.']
  service_provider_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the service provider or focal point. Used for coordination and follow-up.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of support service being referred. Classifies the nature of assistance the survivor requires.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this referral record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each formal referral of a survivor to an external or internal support service provider. Captures referral date, service type (medical, psychosocial, legal, shelter, livelihood), receiving organization or service provider, referral urgency, consent obtained flag, referral status (pending, accepted, completed, declined), and follow-up date. Distinct from the general beneficiary referral — this entity is restricted to safeguarding survivor support pathways with enhanced confidentiality controls.'
}

Table safeguarding.alleged_perpetrator {
  alleged_perpetrator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the alleged perpetrator record. Primary key for the alleged perpetrator entity.']
  donor_constituent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.constituent. Business justification: Donors can be alleged perpetrators (e.g., harassment at fundraising events, misconduct during site visits). Essential for safeguarding enforcement, event access restrictions, and gift acceptance polic']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safeguarding.investigation. Business justification: Alleged perpetrators must be linked to the formal investigation(s) conducted about them. Currently alleged_perpetrator has investigation_status, investigation_start_date, investigation_completion_date']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner organization if the alleged perpetrator is partner staff or contractor. Nullable for internal staff or community members.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: Investigation scope determination and organizational risk assessment require linking alleged perpetrator to their position at incident time. Tracks supervision chain, role-specific vulnerabilities (be']
  primary_alleged_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Employee or volunteer identifier if the alleged perpetrator is or was a member of the workforce. Nullable for external perpetrators. Maintained separately from workforce records to enforce investigation integrity.']
  safeguarding_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the safeguarding incident in which this individual is alleged to be the perpetrator.']
  same_as_alleged_perpetrator_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on alleged_perpetrator (same_as_alleged_perpetrator_id)']
  access_restriction_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level controlling who can access this alleged perpetrator record. Strictly access-controlled to enforce investigation integrity and protect due process.']
  allegation_date date [note: 'Date when the allegation against this individual was formally recorded in the safeguarding system.']
  allegation_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the allegation based on organizational risk assessment framework.']
  allegation_type varchar [note: 'Primary category of the allegation against this individual. Aligns with organizational safeguarding policy categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sexual_exploitation|sexual_abuse|child_abuse|harassment|bullying|fraud|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  case_notes varchar [note: 'Confidential notes documenting key investigation milestones, decisions, and rationale. Strictly controlled access.']
  case_outcome varchar [note: 'Final organizational action taken against the alleged perpetrator following investigation conclusion. Cleared indicates no wrongdoing found. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dismissed|written_warning|final_warning|termination|criminal_referral|cleared|pending — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  country_office_at_incident varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the country office where the alleged perpetrator was based at the time of the incident.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this alleged perpetrator record was first created in the system.']
  criminal_referral_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the law enforcement agency or criminal justice authority to which the case was referred, if applicable.']
  criminal_referral_date date [note: 'Date when the case was referred to law enforcement, if applicable. Nullable if no referral made.']
  criminal_referral_made boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case was referred to law enforcement or criminal justice authorities.']
  disciplinary_action_date date [note: 'Date when disciplinary action was formally applied, if applicable. Nullable if no action taken or case pending.']
  employment_status_at_incident varchar [note: 'Employment or engagement status of the alleged perpetrator at the time the incident occurred. Critical for determining organizational liability and jurisdiction.']
  interim_measures_applied varchar [note: 'Administrative measures applied to the alleged perpetrator pending investigation outcome. Multiple measures may be concatenated.']
  investigation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation was formally concluded. Nullable if investigation is ongoing.']
  investigation_outcome varchar [note: 'Final determination of the investigation. Substantiated means evidence supports the allegation; unsubstantiated means insufficient evidence; inconclusive means investigation could not reach a determination.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the formal investigation into the allegations against this individual commenced.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the investigation into the allegations against this individual.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the individual who last modified this alleged perpetrator record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this alleged perpetrator record was last modified.']
  misconduct_database_report_date date [note: 'Date when the case was reported to inter-agency misconduct databases, if applicable.']
  misconduct_database_reported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case was reported to inter-agency misconduct disclosure schemes or databases (e.g., UN ClearCheck, Misconduct Disclosure Scheme).']
  perpetrator_code varchar [note: 'Anonymized unique code assigned to the alleged perpetrator to protect identity during investigation. Format: AP-NNNNNN.']
  rehire_eligibility varchar [note: 'Determination of whether the individual is eligible for future employment or engagement with the organization or its partners.']
  relationship_to_organization varchar [note: 'The alleged perpetrator\'s relationship to the organization at the time of the incident. Determines investigation jurisdiction and accountability mechanisms.']
  suspension_end_date date [note: 'Date when suspension or administrative leave ended, if applicable. Nullable if suspension is ongoing or not applied.']
  suspension_start_date date [note: 'Date when suspension or administrative leave commenced, if applicable. Nullable if no suspension applied.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date of employment or engagement termination if termination was the case outcome. Nullable if not terminated.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the individual who created this alleged perpetrator record.']

  Note: 'Confidential master record for each alleged perpetrator named in a safeguarding investigation. Captures perpetrator code (anonymized), relationship to organization (staff, volunteer, partner staff, contractor, community member), employment or engagement status at time of incident, interim administrative measures applied (suspension, restricted duties), and final case outcome (dismissed, disciplinary action, criminal referral, cleared). Strictly access-controlled and maintained separately from workforce records to enforce investigation integrity.'
}

Table safeguarding.disciplinary_outcome {
  disciplinary_outcome_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the disciplinary outcome record. Primary key for this entity.']
  alleged_perpetrator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safeguarding.alleged_perpetrator. Business justification: Disciplinary outcomes are applied TO alleged perpetrators following investigation findings. Currently disciplinary_outcome links to investigation but not to the perpetrator directly. Adding alleged_pe']
  appealed_disciplinary_outcome_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on disciplinary_outcome (appealed_disciplinary_outcome_id)']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the country office where this disciplinary outcome was issued and managed.']
  donor_constituent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to donor.constituent. Business justification: When donors are subject to disciplinary outcomes (banned from events, restricted field visit access, gift refusal), this link enables enforcement across fundraising operations and ensures consistent a']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the safeguarding investigation that resulted in this disciplinary outcome.']
  primary_disciplinary_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created this disciplinary outcome record.']
  separation_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.separation_event. Business justification: Disciplinary terminations must link to separation_event for final settlement calculation, rehire eligibility enforcement, reference check flagging, and mandatory donor/regulatory reporting (IRS Form 9']
  aggravating_factors varchar [note: 'Description of any aggravating circumstances or factors that influenced the disciplinary outcome decision toward greater severity.']
  appeal_decision_date date [note: 'The date on which the appeal decision was finalized and communicated, if applicable.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'The date on which the appeal was formally filed by the subject employee, if applicable.']
  appeal_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject employee filed a formal appeal against this disciplinary outcome.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'The result of the appeal process, if an appeal was filed. Includes upheld, denied, partially upheld, withdrawn, pending, or not applicable.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'The data classification level for this disciplinary outcome record, determining access and disclosure restrictions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this disciplinary outcome record was first created in the system.']
  criminal_charges_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether criminal charges were filed against the subject as a result of this case.']
  decision_authority varchar [note: 'The role or title of the individual or committee who authorized and issued this disciplinary outcome (e.g., Country Director, HR Director, Disciplinary Panel).']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the disciplinary action becomes effective and enforceable.']
  external_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether external reporting to regulatory bodies, law enforcement, or other authorities is required for this outcome.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this disciplinary outcome record was most recently updated or modified.']
  law_enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'The name of the law enforcement or criminal justice agency to which the case was referred, if applicable.']
  law_enforcement_referral_date date [note: 'The date on which the case was formally referred to law enforcement authorities, if applicable.']
  law_enforcement_referral_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case was referred to law enforcement or criminal justice authorities.']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Summary of organizational lessons learned from this case, used for continuous improvement of safeguarding policies and procedures.']
  mds_reference_number varchar [note: 'The unique reference number assigned by the Inter-Agency Misconduct Disclosure Scheme (MDS) for this reported case.']
  mds_report_date date [note: 'The date on which the case was reported to the Inter-Agency Misconduct Disclosure Scheme (MDS), if applicable.']
  mds_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this case was reported to the Inter-Agency Misconduct Disclosure Scheme (MDS) or equivalent external reporting mechanism.']
  mitigating_factors varchar [note: 'Description of any mitigating circumstances or factors that influenced the disciplinary outcome decision in favor of leniency.']
  outcome_date date [note: 'The date on which the disciplinary outcome decision was formally issued and communicated to the subject.']
  outcome_document_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL or file path to the formal disciplinary outcome decision document.']
  outcome_rationale varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the reasoning and justification for the disciplinary outcome decision, including mitigating and aggravating factors considered.']
  outcome_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number for this disciplinary outcome, used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  outcome_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the disciplinary outcome in its lifecycle, including pending, finalized, under appeal, appeal upheld, appeal denied, or overturned.']
  outcome_type varchar [note: 'The type of disciplinary or administrative action applied following the substantiated investigation. Includes written warning, final warning, demotion, suspension, termination, criminal referral, or no action. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: written_warning|final_warning|demotion|suspension|termination|criminal_referral|no_action — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  policy_violations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of the specific organizational policies that were violated, leading to this disciplinary outcome.']
  reference_check_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this outcome will be disclosed in future employment reference checks for the subject.']
  rehabilitation_plan_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal rehabilitation or performance improvement plan is required as part of the disciplinary outcome.']
  restitution_amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary amount of restitution required from the subject, expressed in United States Dollars (USD).']
  restitution_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject is required to provide financial or other restitution as part of the disciplinary outcome.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'The assessed severity level of the misconduct that led to this disciplinary outcome.']
  training_completion_deadline date [note: 'The deadline by which required training must be completed, if applicable.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mandatory training (e.g., safeguarding, code of conduct, ethics) is required as part of the disciplinary outcome.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of the formal disciplinary or administrative outcome applied following a substantiated safeguarding investigation. Captures outcome type (written warning, final warning, demotion, termination, criminal referral, no action), outcome date, decision authority, appeal filed flag, appeal outcome, and whether the case was reported to the Inter-Agency Misconduct Disclosure Scheme (MDS) or equivalent. Complements workforce.disciplinary_case by capturing the safeguarding-specific outcome details and external reporting obligations.'
}

Table safeguarding.training_program {
  training_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the safeguarding training program. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Training programs are delivered by specific departments (HR, safeguarding units) tracked as cost centers. Program development and delivery costs (cost_per_participant_usd) must be allocated to the res']
  delivery_platform_system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Safeguarding training programs (PSEA, code of conduct, child protection) are delivered through LMS platforms. Organizations need this link for user provisioning, completion tracking integration, certi']
  prerequisite_training_program_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on training_program (prerequisite_training_program_id)']
  primary_training_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this training program record.']
  required_for_job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_profile. Business justification: Training programs are mapped to job profiles to define mandatory safeguarding training by role type (all staff, managers, beneficiary-facing, designated roles). Enables systematic compliance tracking,']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member or department responsible for maintaining and updating this training program.']
  superseded_program_training_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the previous version of this training program that this version replaces. Null if this is the first version.']
  accrediting_body varchar [note: 'Name of the external organization or internal department that accredits or certifies this training program.']
  assessment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether participants must complete an assessment to receive certification. True if assessment required, False otherwise.']
  available_languages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of two-letter ISO 639-1 language codes for all languages in which this training is available.']
  certification_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal certificate is issued upon successful completion. True if certificate issued, False otherwise.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory or industry compliance framework that this training program addresses (e.g., Core Humanitarian Standard, PSEA Framework, Sphere Standards).']
  content_url varchar [note: 'Web address or file path where the training content, materials, or Learning Management System (LMS) course can be accessed.']
  cost_per_participant_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost in United States Dollars to deliver the training program per participant, including materials, instructor fees, and platform costs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training program record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_modality varchar [note: 'Method by which the training is delivered to participants.']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total duration of the training program measured in hours, including all modules and assessments.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the training program becomes active and available for enrollment.']
  enrollment_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of participants that can be enrolled in a single session or cohort of this training program. Null indicates unlimited capacity.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this version of the training program is retired and no longer available for new enrollments. Null indicates no expiration.']
  facilitator_qualifications varchar [note: 'Required qualifications, certifications, or experience for individuals authorized to facilitate this training program.']
  facilitator_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a trained facilitator or instructor is required to deliver this training. True if facilitator required, False for self-paced programs.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the primary language in which the training is delivered.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training program record was last updated.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the training program content was last reviewed for accuracy, relevance, and compliance.']
  learning_objectives varchar [note: 'Specific, measurable learning objectives that participants will achieve upon completion of the training.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of this training is mandatory for the target audience. True if mandatory, False if optional.']
  materials_description varchar [note: 'Description of the training materials provided to participants, including format (digital, printed) and content overview.']
  materials_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether training materials (handouts, guides, reference documents) are provided to participants. True if materials provided, False otherwise.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review of the training program content.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or administrative information about the training program not captured in other fields.']
  passing_score_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage score required to pass the assessment and receive certification. Null if no assessment required.']
  prerequisites varchar [note: 'Any prerequisite training programs, certifications, or experience required before enrolling in this program.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the training program, used for registration and tracking purposes.']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the training program content, objectives, and expected learning outcomes.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training program. Determines availability for enrollment.']
  program_title varchar [note: 'Full title of the safeguarding training program as displayed to learners and in catalogs.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which the training program content is reviewed and updated to ensure relevance and compliance.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Primary audience group for whom this training program is designed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all_staff|field_staff|management|volunteers|partners|new_hires|investigators — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  training_type varchar [note: 'Category of safeguarding training. Defines the primary focus area of the program.']
  translation_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training program is available in additional languages beyond the primary language. True if translations exist, False otherwise.']
  validity_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months the training certification remains valid before renewal or refresher training is required. Null indicates no expiration.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the training program content, following semantic versioning (major.minor format).']

  Note: 'Catalog of mandatory and optional safeguarding training programs available to staff, volunteers, and partners. Captures training title, training type (PSEA awareness, child safeguarding, code of conduct, bystander intervention, survivor-centered approach), delivery modality (in-person, e-learning, blended), duration, target audience, mandatory flag, validity period (months before renewal required), and accrediting body. Distinct from volunteer.training and workforce.learning_course — this catalog is owned by the safeguarding function and governs compliance training obligations.'
}

Table safeguarding.safeguarding_training_completion {
  safeguarding_training_completion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the training completion record. Primary key for this transactional record of safeguarding training completion.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: When safeguarding training is a donor condition or grant-funded activity, linking to the award enables compliance monitoring against special award conditions, cost allocation, and donor reporting of c']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Training delivery costs (cost_usd field) are allocated to the cost center responsible for conducting the training event. Essential for tracking training expenditures against budget and calculating tru']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the country office or field location where the participant is based. Used for geographic compliance reporting and regional safeguarding oversight.']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or project the participant is associated with at the time of training. Supports program-level safeguarding compliance tracking and donor reporting.']
  learning_course_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the safeguarding training course that was completed. Links to the course catalog entry defining the training content, duration, and requirements.']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Partner organization staff complete safeguarding training as partnership prerequisite. Tracks which partner\'s employees completed mandatory PSEA training for capacity assessment scoring and partnershi']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: Safeguarding training requirements are position-specific (beneficiary-facing roles, supervisory positions, field deployments). Links training compliance to position eligibility for deployment clearanc']
  primary_safeguarding_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user or administrator who created this training completion record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.']
  refresher_of_safeguarding_training_completion_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on safeguarding_training_completion (refresher_of_safeguarding_training_completion_id)']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual who completed the training. Links to staff, volunteer, or partner personnel record depending on participant type.']
  training_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safeguarding.training_program. Business justification: training_completion currently links to workforce.learning_course (general LMS course catalog). However, training_program is the safeguarding-specific catalog of PSEA and safeguarding training programs']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved by the participant on the training assessment or quiz. Expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00) to measure comprehension of safeguarding principles.']
  attempts_count varchar [note: 'Number of attempts the participant made to pass the training assessment. Used to identify individuals requiring additional support or remedial training.']
  certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the training certificate expires and the participant must complete refresher training. Critical for tracking mandatory recertification obligations under PSEA and safeguarding policies.']
  certificate_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the completion certificate was officially issued to the participant. May differ from completion date due to administrative processing time.']
  certificate_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a completion certificate was issued to the participant. True when certificate has been generated and delivered; false otherwise.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate identifier issued upon successful completion. Used for verification of training credentials by HR, partners, or external auditors.']
  completion_channel varchar [note: 'Delivery method or platform through which the training was completed. Distinguishes between Learning Management System (LMS) online courses, in-person classroom sessions, and other modalities.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the participant successfully completed the safeguarding training course. Principal business event timestamp for compliance tracking and certificate issuance.']
  completion_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for this training completion record. Used for audit trails, certificate references, and external reporting to donors or regulators.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the training completion record. Tracks whether the participant successfully completed, is still in progress, failed assessment, or had training waived by authorized personnel.']
  compliance_deadline_date date [note: 'Date by which the participant was required to complete the training to meet organizational or donor compliance obligations. Used to identify overdue training and non-compliance risk.']
  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Direct cost incurred for this training completion, including instructor fees, materials, venue, and participant travel. Expressed in United States Dollars for financial reporting and budget tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training completion record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the participant was enrolled or registered for the training course. Used to track time-to-completion and identify overdue training obligations.']
  external_provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external training provider or consultancy that delivered the course, if not conducted in-house. Null for internally-delivered training.']
  feedback_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments provided by the participant about the training experience. Captures suggestions for improvement, content gaps, or delivery issues.']
  feedback_rating varchar [note: 'Participant satisfaction rating for the training course, typically on a scale of 1 to 5. Used for continuous improvement of safeguarding training quality.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Grant, donor, or budget line that funded this training activity. Used for cost allocation and donor reporting on capacity-building investments.']
  instructor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the facilitator or instructor who delivered the training for in-person or virtual instructor-led sessions. Null for self-paced online courses.']
  language varchar [note: 'Language in which the training content was delivered to the participant. Supports multilingual training delivery and ensures comprehension in local contexts.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training completion record was last updated. Tracks changes to status, scores, or certificate information over the records lifecycle.']
  lms_course_code varchar [note: 'External identifier from the Learning Management System where the training was delivered. Used for integration and reconciliation with LMS platforms such as Workday Learning.']
  mandatory_training_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training was mandatory for the participant based on their role, location, or organizational policy. True for required safeguarding training; false for optional professional development.']
  next_refresher_due_date date [note: 'Date when the participant must complete refresher training to maintain current certification. Calculated based on certificate expiry date and organizational recertification policies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional contextual information about this training completion. May include special circumstances, accommodations provided, or follow-up actions required.']
  overdue_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training completion occurred after the compliance deadline. True when completed late or still incomplete past deadline; false when completed on time.']
  participant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the individual completing the training. Determines compliance obligations and reporting requirements under organizational safeguarding policies.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Binary outcome of the training assessment. Determines whether the participant met the minimum competency requirements for safeguarding knowledge.']
  passing_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to pass the training assessment at the time of completion. Captured to support historical audit when passing thresholds change over time.']
  refresher_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this completion record requires a future refresher training. True for certifications with expiry dates; false for one-time orientation training.']
  training_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of hours the participant spent completing the training. Used for professional development tracking and compliance with donor-mandated training hour requirements.']
  training_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or virtual platform where the training was conducted. Examples include country office name, city, or virtual meeting platform (Zoom, Teams).']
  waiver_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the training waiver was officially approved. Used for audit trail and compliance documentation.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authorized individual who approved the training waiver. Typically a safeguarding focal point, HR director, or country director.']
  waiver_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training requirement was waived for this participant by authorized personnel. True when waiver approved; false for standard completion path.']
