  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or context related to the PSEA assessment process and findings.']
  overall_capacity_rating varchar [note: 'Overall rating of the partner\'s safeguarding and PSEA capacity based on the assessment findings.']
  partnership_approval_status varchar [note: 'Decision on whether the partner is approved for partnership engagement based on PSEA assessment results.']
  policy_existence_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of whether the partner has adequate PSEA and safeguarding policies in place.']
  reassessment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the partner must undergo a follow-up PSEA assessment to verify capacity improvements.']
  reassessment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up reassessment is required to verify implementation of capacity-building actions.']
  recommendations varchar [note: 'Detailed recommendations for improving the partner\'s PSEA and safeguarding capacity based on assessment findings.']
  reporting_mechanism_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the adequacy and accessibility of the partner\'s safeguarding incident reporting mechanisms.']
  staff_training_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the partner\'s PSEA and safeguarding training programs for staff and volunteers.']
  survivor_support_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of the partner\'s capacity to provide survivor-centered support and referral services.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of safeguarding capacity assessments conducted on implementing partners prior to or during partnership engagement. Captures assessment date, assessment tool used (PSEA organizational assessment, safeguarding checklist, CHS self-assessment), overall safeguarding capacity rating, key gaps identified, capacity-building plan triggered flag, and reassessment due date. Complements partnership.capacity_assessment by focusing exclusively on safeguarding and PSEA dimensions with their own scoring methodology and follow-up obligations.'
}

Table safeguarding.community_awareness_session {
  community_awareness_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the community awareness session record. Primary key for this entity.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: Community safeguarding awareness sessions funded by specific grants must track the award for activity reporting, cost allocation, demonstrating community engagement requirements, and reporting benefic']
  community_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the beneficiary community or settlement where the awareness session was conducted.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Community awareness sessions are conducted by field offices/program units tracked as cost centers. Session costs (facilitator time, materials, venue) must be allocated to the responsible cost center f']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the country office responsible for organizing and conducting this community awareness session.']
  distribution_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_plan. Business justification: PSEA awareness sessions are conducted during distribution planning/execution to inform beneficiaries of complaint mechanisms and safeguarding standards. Required for beneficiary awareness, complaint m']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Community sessions are often funded by specific grants with safeguarding/protection components. Tracking which fund covers session costs is required for donor reporting on safeguarding activities and ']
  followup_community_awareness_session_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on community_awareness_session (followup_community_awareness_session_id)']
  intervention_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the program under which this community awareness session was conducted, linking the session to broader programmatic activities.']
  primary_community_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who facilitated or led the community awareness session.']
  quaternary_community_last_modified_by_staff_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who last updated or modified this community awareness session record.']
  tertiary_community_created_by_staff_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who created this community awareness session record in the system.']
  attendance_sheet_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the attendance sheet or participant registration document for audit and verification purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this community awareness session record was first created in the system.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the community awareness session measured in minutes from start to end.']
  feedback_received varchar [note: 'Summary of feedback, questions, concerns, or suggestions received from community participants during or after the session.']
  follow_up_actions_required varchar [note: 'Description of any follow-up actions, additional sessions, or support activities identified as necessary based on session outcomes and participant feedback.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which identified follow-up actions should be completed or reviewed.']
  incident_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any safeguarding incident or concern was reported by participants during the awareness session.']
  key_messages_delivered varchar [note: 'Summary of the key safeguarding messages, topics, and information delivered during the community awareness session.']
  language_used varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the community awareness session was conducted to ensure accessibility and understanding.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this community awareness session record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  location_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating the country where the community awareness session was conducted.']
  location_district varchar [note: 'District or sub-regional administrative area where the session was conducted.']
  location_region varchar [note: 'Administrative region or province where the community awareness session took place.']
  location_site_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific site, community center, camp, or settlement where the awareness session was held.']
  materials_distributed varchar [note: 'Description of information, education, and communication (IEC) materials distributed to participants during the session, such as brochures, posters, or handouts.']
  participant_satisfaction_rating varchar [note: 'Overall satisfaction rating provided by participants regarding the quality and relevance of the awareness session.']
  participants_adults_18_to_59 varchar [note: 'Number of adult participants aged 18 to 59 years who attended the session.']
  participants_children_under_18 varchar [note: 'Number of child participants under 18 years of age who attended the session, supporting age-disaggregated reporting.']
  participants_elderly_60_plus varchar [note: 'Number of elderly participants aged 60 years and above who attended the session.']
  participants_female varchar [note: 'Number of female participants who attended the session, supporting gender-disaggregated reporting.']
  participants_male varchar [note: 'Number of male participants who attended the session, supporting gender-disaggregated reporting.']
  participants_other_gender varchar [note: 'Number of participants who identify as other gender or prefer not to disclose, ensuring inclusive data collection.']
  participants_with_disabilities varchar [note: 'Number of participants with disabilities who attended the session, supporting inclusion monitoring.']
  referral_made_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any participant was referred to support services, case management, or other assistance as a result of the session.']
  session_date date [note: 'The date on which the community awareness session was conducted or is scheduled to be conducted.']
  session_end_time timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the community awareness session ended, capturing the precise completion time of the activity.']
  session_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or contextual information recorded by the facilitator about the session dynamics, challenges, or successes.']
  session_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number or code assigned to this community awareness session for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  session_report_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the detailed session report documenting activities, outcomes, and participant engagement.']
  session_start_time timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the community awareness session started, capturing the precise beginning time of the activity.']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the community awareness session indicating whether it is planned, completed, or cancelled.']
  session_type varchar [note: 'Type or category of safeguarding awareness session conducted with the community. Indicates the primary focus area of the sensitization activity.']
  total_participants varchar [note: 'Total number of community members who attended and participated in the awareness session.']
  translation_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether translation or interpretation services were provided during the session to accommodate multilingual participants.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of community-level safeguarding awareness and PSEA sensitization sessions conducted with beneficiary communities. Captures session date, location, facilitator, session type (PSEA awareness, child safeguarding, reporting channels orientation, rights and entitlements), number of participants (disaggregated by gender and age group), key messages delivered, feedback received, and follow-up actions. Supports accountability to affected populations (AAP) and community-based prevention programming.'
}

Table safeguarding.psea_network {
  psea_network_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for psea_network']
  parent_psea_network_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on psea_network (parent_psea_network_id)']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Accreditation or certification status of the PSEA network by relevant governing bodies.']
  community_engagement_approach varchar [note: 'Description of the PSEA network\'s approach to community engagement and awareness raising.']
  coordination_level varchar [note: 'Level of coordination provided by the PSEA network among member organizations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the PSEA network primarily operates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PSEA network record was first created in the system.']
  establishment_date date [note: 'Date when the PSEA network was officially established or formed.']
  focal_point_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the PSEA network focal point.']
  focal_point_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person or focal point for the PSEA network.']
  focal_point_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the PSEA network focal point.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Primary funding source or donor supporting the PSEA network operations.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic coverage area of the PSEA network operations.']
  investigation_capacity varchar [note: 'Level of investigation capacity available within the PSEA network.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the PSEA network record is currently active in the system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PSEA network record was last updated or modified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the PSEA network\'s operations, mandate, or effectiveness was last reviewed.']
  lead_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization serving as the lead or coordinator for the PSEA network.']
  mandate_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the PSEA network\'s mandate, objectives, and scope of work.']
  meeting_frequency varchar [note: 'Regular frequency at which the PSEA network convenes meetings.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Total number of member organizations participating in the PSEA network.']
  network_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the PSEA network for system identification and reporting.']
  network_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the PSEA network or coalition.']
  network_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the PSEA network.']
  network_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the PSEA network based on its scope and organizational structure.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review of the PSEA network\'s operations and effectiveness.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or contextual information about the PSEA network.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or administrative area where the network operates.']
  reporting_mechanism_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PSEA network has an established reporting mechanism for SEA allegations.']
  reporting_mechanism_type varchar [note: 'Type of reporting mechanism used by the PSEA network for receiving complaints.']
  survivor_assistance_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PSEA network provides or coordinates survivor assistance services.']
  terms_of_reference_url varchar [note: 'Web link to the official terms of reference document for the PSEA network.']
  training_program_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PSEA network offers training programs on safeguarding and PSEA.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website address for the PSEA network.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for psea_network. Referenced by network_id.'
}

Table technology.it_asset {
  it_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IT asset record. Primary key.']
  network_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.network_site. Business justification: IT assets are deployed at specific network sites (HQ, field offices, data centers). N:1 relationship - many assets at one network site. Enables physical/logical location tracking, network capacity pla']
  parent_it_asset_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on it_asset (parent_it_asset_id)']
  primary_it_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member or employee to whom the asset is currently assigned for use.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.vendor']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Detailed categorization of the asset within its type, specifying the specific kind of hardware or software. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: laptop|desktop|server|tablet|smartphone|router|switch|firewall|printer|scanner|application_software|operating_system|database|middleware — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  asset_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the physical or functional condition of the asset, used for maintenance planning and disposal decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|excellent|good|fair|poor|damaged|obsolete — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique identifier affixed to the physical asset for inventory tracking and auditing purposes.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the IT asset distinguishing between hardware, software, network equipment, mobile devices, peripherals, and licenses.']
  assigned_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the asset is deployed.']
  assigned_location_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the specific office, field location, or facility where the asset is assigned.']
  assigned_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of organizational location where the asset is currently deployed or stored.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset was assigned to the current staff member or location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the procurement cost.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to depreciate the asset value over its useful life.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset was disposed of, sold, donated, or decommissioned.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the asset was disposed of at end of life, important for environmental and data security compliance.']
  hostname varchar [note: 'Network hostname or computer name assigned to the asset for identification on the organization\'s network.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Network IP address assigned to the asset for connectivity and network management purposes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset record was last updated or modified.']
  license_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the software license expires and requires renewal to maintain compliance.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the asset in its operational lifecycle, indicating whether it is actively in use, stored, under repair, or decommissioned.']
  mac_address varchar [note: 'Hardware MAC address uniquely identifying the network interface of the asset.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured or developed the IT asset.']
  model varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model number or name identifying the specific product variant.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or special handling instructions related to the asset.']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'Name and version of the operating system installed on the asset, critical for software compatibility and security patching.']
  procurement_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Original purchase price or acquisition cost of the asset in the organization\'s base currency.']
  procurement_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset was purchased or acquired by the organization.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Commercial or marketing name of the IT asset product.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the purchase order used to acquire the asset, linking to procurement records.']
  salvage_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the hardware or software license.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the primary software or firmware installed on the asset.']
  support_contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for any active maintenance or support contract associated with the asset.']
  support_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the support or maintenance contract expires.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected useful life of the asset in years, used for depreciation and replacement planning.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer or vendor warranty coverage ends, after which maintenance costs may increase.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer or vendor warranty coverage begins.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Type of warranty coverage applicable to the asset, distinguishing between manufacturer, extended, or third-party warranties.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all IT hardware and software assets owned or leased by the NGO, including laptops, servers, networking equipment, mobile devices, and licensed software. Captures asset tag, serial number, model, manufacturer, procurement date, warranty expiry, assigned location (country office or HQ), assigned staff member, asset condition, and lifecycle status (active, in-repair, decommissioned). SSOT for IT asset inventory across all field and HQ locations.'
}

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SSO|LDAP|OAuth|SAML|Multi-Factor Authentication|Username Password|API Key|Certificate|Other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Real-Time|Hourly|Daily|Weekly|Monthly|On-Demand|Not Applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Perpetual|Subscription|Open Source|Freemium|Enterprise|Named User|Concurrent User|Other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified this system platform record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this system platform record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special considerations related to the platform.']

  platform_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the digital platform or enterprise information system (e.g., KoboToolbox, CommCare, DHIS2, Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud, SAP S/4HANA for Nonprofits, Workday HCM, Raisers Edge NXT, Unit4 ERP, Power BI, Tableau).']
  platform_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the platform (Active, Inactive, Pilot, Sunset, Decommissioned, Under Implementation, Maintenance). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Active|Inactive|Pilot|Sunset|Decommissioned|Under Implementation|Maintenance — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ERP|CRM|HRIS|GIS|Data Collection|Business Intelligence|Grant Management|Donor Management|Case Management|Health Information System|Financial Management|Custom Application|Collaboration Platform|Other — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Beneficiary|Program|Grant|Donor|Finance|Workforce|Volunteer|Supply Chain|Field Operations|Monitoring and Evaluation|Partnership|Communications|Technology|Compliance|Other — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  support_tier varchar [note: 'Level of support or service tier provided for the platform (Standard, Premium, Enterprise, Community, Internal, Vendor-Managed).']

Table technology.it_service {
  it_service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IT service. Primary key for the IT service catalog.']
  parent_it_service_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on it_service (parent_it_service_id)']
  primary_system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: IT services are delivered via specific platforms (e.g., Email service via Exchange platform, Helpdesk service via ServiceNow platform). This FK tracks the PRIMARY platform. N:1 relationship - many ser']
  access_request_process varchar [note: 'Process required for users to request access to this service: self_service (automatic provisioning), manager_approval (requires line manager approval), it_approval (requires IT team approval), or security_clearance (requires security review).']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Primary authentication mechanism for accessing the service: single_sign_on (SSO via organizational identity provider), username_password (basic credentials), multi_factor (MFA required), or certificate_based (digital certificates).']
  availability_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target uptime percentage for the service as defined in the SLA (e.g., 99.9% for critical services, 95% for standard services). Measured over a calendar month.']
  backup_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which data for this service is backed up: real_time (continuous replication), hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, or not_applicable (service does not store data requiring backup).']
  compliance_frameworks varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory or compliance frameworks this service must adhere to (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, ISO 27001, CHS, Sphere Standards, OMB Uniform Guidance). Used for audit and compliance reporting.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the contract or agreement governing the service provision, if applicable. Used for vendor management and compliance tracking.']
  cost_allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate service costs to programs or departments: direct_charge (charged to specific cost centers), shared_pool (distributed across all users), overhead (absorbed in indirect costs), or grant_funded (covered by specific donor grants).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service record was first created in the IT service catalog system.']
  data_classification_level varchar [note: 'Highest classification level of data handled by this service: public (no restrictions), internal (internal use only), confidential (business-sensitive), or restricted (PII, PHI, or donor-sensitive data).']
  disaster_recovery_rpo_hours varchar [note: 'Recovery Point Objective (RPO) in hours: maximum acceptable age of data that may be lost in a disaster. Defines backup frequency requirements.']
  disaster_recovery_rto_hours varchar [note: 'Recovery Time Objective (RTO) in hours: maximum acceptable time to restore the service after a disaster or major outage. Critical for business continuity planning.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of service availability: global (all locations), regional (specific regions), country_specific (single country operations), or headquarters_only (HQ office only).']
  knowledge_base_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the knowledge base, user guide, or documentation portal for this service. Enables self-service support and user training.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service record was last updated in the IT service catalog system. Used for change tracking and audit trails.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the service was last reviewed for performance, cost-effectiveness, and alignment with business needs. Part of continuous service improvement.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the IT staff member who last modified this service record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  monthly_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average monthly cost in USD to deliver this service, including licensing, infrastructure, support, and vendor fees. Used for IT budget planning and cost allocation.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next service review. Ensures regular evaluation of service performance and relevance.']
  resolution_time_target_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum time in hours to resolve an incident or fulfill a service request for this service, as defined in the SLA.']
  response_time_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum time in minutes for the IT team to acknowledge and begin working on an incident or service request for this service, as defined in the SLA.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when the service will be or was decommissioned and removed from the service catalog. Null for active services.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the IT service type: connectivity (internet, VPN), infrastructure (servers, storage), application (Salesforce, SAP, DHIS2), support (helpdesk, training), security (firewall, antivirus), or data_management (backup, archiving).']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the service for internal reference and system integration (e.g., EMAIL-001, VPN-002, STORAGE-003).']
  service_delivery_model varchar [note: 'How the service is delivered: in_house (managed by internal IT team), outsourced (managed by external vendor), hybrid (combination of internal and external), or cloud_saas (Software as a Service from cloud provider).']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of what the IT service provides, its purpose, and how it supports humanitarian operations and staff productivity.']
  service_hours varchar [note: 'Hours during which the service is available and supported: 24x7 (24 hours, 7 days a week), business_hours (Monday-Friday, 9am-5pm local time), extended_hours (weekdays plus evenings/weekends), or on_demand (available upon request).']
  service_launch_date date [note: 'Date when the service was first made available to users in production. Used for service lifecycle tracking and anniversary reporting.']
  service_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual responsible for day-to-day operational management of the service, including incident response and user support.']
  service_name varchar [note: 'The official name of the IT service as presented to users (e.g., Email Service, VPN Access, Cloud Storage, Helpdesk Support).']
  service_owner_email varchar [note: 'Primary email contact for the service owner for escalations and service-related inquiries.']
  service_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual accountable for the service delivery, performance, and continuous improvement. Typically a senior IT manager or director.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the IT service: active (available for use), inactive (temporarily unavailable), retired (decommissioned), under_development (being built), pilot (limited rollout), or maintenance (scheduled downtime).']
  service_type varchar [note: 'ITIL service classification: business_service (directly supports business operations), technical_service (underlying infrastructure), or supporting_service (enables other services).']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Service level tier defining response and resolution commitments: platinum (mission-critical, 24/7 support), gold (high priority, business hours), silver (standard support), bronze (basic support), or best_effort (no guaranteed SLA).']
  support_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for users to request support or report issues with this service (e.g., helpdesk@ngo.org, itsupport@ngo.org).']
  support_phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for users to contact IT support for this service. May include international dialing codes for global services.']
  supported_user_population varchar [note: 'Primary user group or population that this service is designed to support: all_staff, field_operations (humanitarian workers in the field), headquarters (HQ staff), program_staff, finance_staff, senior_management, or external_partners (donors, implementing partners). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all_staff|field_operations|headquarters|program_staff|finance_staff|senior_management|external_partners — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal training is required before users can access this service. True if training is mandatory, False if service is self-explanatory or training is optional.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external vendor or service provider if the service is outsourced or provided via SaaS (e.g., Microsoft, Salesforce, Amazon Web Services). Null for in-house services.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of IT services offered by the technology team to internal staff and field operations, including connectivity services, helpdesk support, cloud storage, email, VPN access, data backup, and platform administration. Captures service name, service category, service owner, SLA tier, supported user population, availability target, and current service status. Aligned with ITIL service catalog practices.'
}

Table technology.service_request {
  service_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IT service request ticket. Primary key.']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the office or field location where the requester is based (HQ, regional office, country office, field site).']
  it_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific hardware asset or device affected (e.g., laptop serial number, desktop tag, mobile device IMEI, printer asset code). Null if request is not hardware-related.']
  it_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.it_service. Business justification: Service requests are raised for specific IT services from the service catalog. The affected_system STRING field should be normalized to FK relationship with it_service. N:1 relationship - many service']
  knowledge_article_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the knowledge base article used or created as a result of resolving this service request. Null if no article linked.']
  primary_service_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who submitted the service request.']
  related_incident_it_incident_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a related major incident or problem ticket if this service request is linked to a broader system issue. Null if standalone.']
  related_service_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on service_request (related_service_request_id)']
  resulting_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.change_request. Business justification: Service requests can escalate to formal change requests when they require CAB approval or significant system modifications. N:1 relationship (service request escalates to change). Temporal note: FK po']
  assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was assigned to a technician or support group. Null if not yet assigned.']
  assignment_group varchar [note: 'Name of the IT support team or functional group responsible for handling the request (e.g., Desktop Support, Network Operations, Application Support, Field IT, Security Operations).']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was formally closed, typically after requester confirmation or auto-closure. Null if not yet closed.']
  contact_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the service request was submitted: web portal (self-service), email, phone call, live chat, walk-in (in-person), or mobile app.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center or budget code to which IT support costs for this service request are allocated, typically aligned with the requester\'s department or program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the service request record was first created in the IT helpdesk system.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service request was escalated to higher-level support or management (True = escalated, False = not escalated).']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for escalating the service request (e.g., SLA breach, technical complexity, VIP requester, repeated issue). Null if not escalated.']
  first_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the assigned technician first acknowledged or responded to the service request. Null if no response yet.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the service request record was last updated in the IT helpdesk system.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the service request based on impact and urgency: critical (mission-critical system down), high (major functionality impaired), medium (moderate impact), or low (minor inconvenience).']
  reopened_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the service request was reopened after initial resolution due to recurring issues or incomplete resolution.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Category of the IT service request: hardware fault (device malfunction), software access (application permissions), connectivity issue (network/internet), platform support (KoboToolbox, CommCare, DHIS2, Salesforce, SAP assistance), data recovery (backup restoration), or account provisioning (new user setup).']
  requester_feedback varchar [note: 'Free-text feedback or comments provided by the requester about the service experience. Null if no feedback provided.']
  requester_satisfaction_rating varchar [note: 'Satisfaction score provided by the requester after resolution, typically on a scale of 1 to 5 (1 = very dissatisfied, 5 = very satisfied). Null if feedback not provided.']
  resolution_category varchar [note: 'Classification of how the request was resolved (e.g., hardware replacement, software configuration, password reset, user training, escalation to vendor, workaround provided). Null if not yet resolved.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes documenting the actions taken by the technician to resolve the request, including troubleshooting steps, configuration changes, and final solution. Null if not yet resolved.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was marked as resolved by the technician. Null if not yet resolved.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified underlying cause of the issue (e.g., hardware failure, software bug, user error, network outage, configuration drift). Null if not determined or not applicable.']
  service_request_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the issue or service need provided by the requester, including symptoms, error messages, and context.']
  service_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service request: open (newly submitted), assigned (allocated to technician), in_progress (actively being worked), pending_user (awaiting requester input), resolved (solution provided), closed (confirmed complete), or cancelled (withdrawn). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|assigned|in_progress|pending_user|resolved|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service request resolution exceeded the SLA target time (True = breached, False = within SLA).']
  sla_target_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target resolution time in hours as defined by the IT service level agreement for this request type and priority combination.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief summary or title of the service request as entered by the requester (e.g., \'Laptop not connecting to VPN\', \'Request access to Salesforce reports\').']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was originally submitted by the requester.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable external ticket number assigned by the IT helpdesk system (e.g., INC-000012345, SR-20230415-001).']
  time_spent_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total technician effort hours logged against this service request for resolution activities. Null if not yet tracked or resolved.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every IT helpdesk and service desk request raised by staff across HQ and field offices. Captures request type (hardware fault, software access, connectivity issue, platform support, data recovery), requester identity, affected system or asset, priority level, assigned technician, SLA breach flag, resolution notes, and closure timestamp. Sourced from the IT helpdesk ticketing system (e.g., Freshservice, Jira Service Management).'
}

Table technology.change_request {
  change_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IT change request record. Primary key.']
  cab_meeting_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the CAB meeting session where this change request was reviewed. Links to meeting records for audit trail and decision context.']
  it_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.it_project. Business justification: Change requests can be part of larger IT projects (e.g., project-driven changes vs. ad-hoc operational changes). N:1 relationship - many change requests can belong to one project. Enables project-leve']
  primary_change_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member or system user who submitted the change request. Links to workforce or user management system.']
  primary_system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Change requests target specific platforms for modifications. While affected_systems is a STRING list (multiple systems), this FK tracks the PRIMARY system being changed. N:1 relationship - many change']
  rollback_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on change_request (rollback_change_request_id)']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when change implementation was completed. Used for duration analysis and schedule variance reporting.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when change implementation began. Used for tracking adherence to schedule and post-implementation analysis.']
  affected_services varchar [note: 'Business services and operational capabilities that may experience disruption or modification during change implementation. Used for stakeholder notification and service continuity planning.']
  affected_systems varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of IT systems, platforms, and applications that will be modified or impacted by the change. Examples include Salesforce, SAP, DHIS2, KoboToolbox, CommCare.']
  approval_notes varchar [note: 'Comments, conditions, or recommendations provided by approvers during the review process. Captures decision rationale and any special instructions.']
  assigned_to_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or team assigned to execute the change implementation. Provides accountability and contact information.']
  cab_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the Change Advisory Board formally approved or rejected the change request. Null if CAB review is not required or not yet completed.']
  cab_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change requires formal review and approval by the Change Advisory Board. True for normal and emergency changes; false for standard pre-approved changes.']
  cab_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval decision status from the Change Advisory Board. Conditional approval indicates approval with specific requirements or constraints.']
  change_category varchar [note: 'Functional area or domain affected by the change request. Used for routing to appropriate technical teams and impact analysis.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique change request number following organizational numbering convention (e.g., CHG0001234). Used for tracking and communication across IT teams and stakeholders.']
  change_request_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the change to be implemented, including technical specifications, configuration modifications, and scope of work. Provides comprehensive context for reviewers and implementers.']
  change_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the change request in the approval and implementation workflow. Tracks progression from initial submission through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|scheduled|in_progress|implemented|closed|cancelled — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change request based on risk, urgency, and approval requirements. Standard changes are pre-approved low-risk changes; normal changes require CAB review; emergency changes address critical incidents; expedited changes are fast-tracked normal changes.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change request was formally closed after successful implementation and post-implementation review. Marks the end of the change lifecycle.']
  closure_notes varchar [note: 'Final comments and summary recorded when closing the change request. Documents final disposition and any outstanding items or follow-up actions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change request record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  downtime_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change implementation requires system or service downtime. True if downtime is necessary; false if change can be implemented without service interruption.']
  estimated_downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Anticipated duration of system or service unavailability during change implementation, measured in minutes. Used for scheduling and stakeholder communication.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Detailed analysis of potential effects on systems, services, users, and business operations. Identifies affected components, dependencies, and downstream consequences.']
  implementation_outcome varchar [note: 'Final result of the change implementation. Successful indicates change achieved objectives; failed indicates implementation did not complete; partially successful indicates some objectives met; rolled back indicates change was reverted.']
  implementation_plan varchar [note: 'Step-by-step technical procedure for executing the change, including pre-implementation tasks, execution steps, validation checkpoints, and post-implementation activities.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business rationale and expected benefits of implementing the change. Explains why the change is necessary and how it supports organizational objectives or resolves issues.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change request record was last updated. Tracks most recent modification for audit and synchronization purposes.']
  post_implementation_notes varchar [note: 'Summary of post-implementation review findings, including actual vs. expected outcomes, issues encountered, lessons learned, and recommendations for future changes.']
  post_implementation_review_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal post-implementation review has been conducted to assess change success, lessons learned, and process improvements. Required for normal and emergency changes.']
  post_implementation_review_date date [note: 'Date when the post-implementation review was completed. Used for tracking compliance with change management process requirements.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Urgency and importance level assigned to the change request. Determines review timeline and implementation scheduling relative to other changes.']
  requester_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the change requester for status updates and communication throughout the change lifecycle.']
  requester_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who initiated the change request. Provides human-readable identification for reporting and communication.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the change based on potential impact to systems, services, and operations. Influences approval authority and implementation controls.']
  rollback_plan varchar [note: 'Documented procedure for reverting the change and restoring systems to their previous state if implementation fails or causes unacceptable issues. Critical for risk mitigation.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when change implementation should be completed. Defines the end of the approved implementation window.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when change implementation will begin. Defines the start of the implementation window approved by Change Advisory Board (CAB).']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change request was formally submitted for review. Marks the beginning of the change request lifecycle and approval workflow.']
  test_plan varchar [note: 'Testing strategy and validation procedures to verify successful implementation and confirm that the change achieves intended outcomes without introducing defects.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title summarizing the nature and purpose of the change request. Should be concise yet informative for quick identification.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of formal IT change requests submitted for review and approval before implementation, covering system upgrades, configuration changes, platform migrations, network modifications, and security patches. Captures change type (standard, normal, emergency), risk assessment, change advisory board (CAB) approval status, rollback plan, implementation window, and post-implementation review outcome. Supports ITIL Change Management process.'
}

Table technology.it_incident {
  it_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IT incident record. Primary key.']
  caused_by_it_incident_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on it_incident (caused_by_it_incident_id)']
  it_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.it_service. Business justification: IT incidents disrupt specific IT services. The affected_service STRING field should be normalized to FK. N:1 relationship - many incidents affect one service. Core ITSM relationship for service availa']
  related_change_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related change request if this incident was caused by or resolved through a change to IT infrastructure or configuration.']
  related_problem_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related problem record if this incident is linked to a known underlying problem requiring root cause analysis and permanent fix.']
  system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: IT incidents occur on specific platforms and disrupt their operations. The affected_system STRING field should be normalized to FK. N:1 relationship - many incidents can affect one platform. Core inci']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the assigned team acknowledged receipt and began investigation.']
  affected_country_office varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the country office or field location experiencing the incident (e.g., KEN for Kenya, SYR for Syria, USA for headquarters). Null if incident is global.']
  affected_program varchar [note: 'Name or code of the humanitarian program or project impacted by the incident (e.g., WASH Emergency Response, Health Services Delivery, Education Access Program).']
  assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was assigned to a support team or individual.']
  assigned_to varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the IT support team member or group currently responsible for resolving the incident.']
  breach_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether regulatory breach notification is required under GDPR, CCPA, or other data protection laws.']
  business_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of the business impact of the incident: critical (mission-critical operations halted), high (major program disruption), medium (moderate inconvenience), low (minimal impact).']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was formally closed after user confirmation that the issue is resolved.']
  communication_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a communication or notification was sent to affected users or stakeholders about the incident.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this incident record was first created in the IT service management system.']
  data_breach boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident resulted in unauthorized access to or disclosure of sensitive data (beneficiary PII, donor information, financial records).']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was first detected or when the service disruption began (may precede reported_timestamp if detected by monitoring tools).']
  downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration in minutes that the affected system or service was unavailable.']
  escalated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident was escalated to higher-level support or management due to severity or complexity.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Level to which the incident was escalated: L1 (first-line support), L2 (specialist support), L3 (engineering), management (executive attention), vendor (third-party escalation).']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was escalated.']
  financial_impact_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial cost of the incident in USD, including lost productivity, recovery costs, and potential donor impact.']
  impacted_user_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of staff, volunteers, or beneficiaries affected by the incident.']
  incident_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the incident type: hardware failure, software defect, network outage, security breach, data loss, or platform unavailability.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique incident ticket number assigned by the IT service management system (e.g., INC0012345).']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the incident: new (just reported), assigned (routed to team), in_progress (actively being worked), pending (awaiting external input), resolved (fix applied), closed (confirmed resolved), reopened (recurred after closure). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|assigned|in_progress|pending|resolved|closed|reopened — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incident_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed classification within the incident category (e.g., ransomware, server crash, database corruption, API timeout, VPN failure).']
  mean_time_to_resolution_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in minutes from incident detection to resolution. Key performance indicator for IT service quality.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this incident record was last updated.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who first reported the incident (staff member, volunteer, or system monitor).']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was first reported to the IT service desk.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the actions taken to resolve the incident and restore service.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was resolved and service was restored.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified underlying cause of the incident after investigation (e.g., hardware failure, software bug, configuration error, cyberattack, human error, third-party service outage).']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause for trend analysis: infrastructure failure, application defect, configuration error, security breach, human error, or third-party dependency.']
  security_incident boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident is classified as a security incident (data breach, ransomware, unauthorized access, phishing attack).']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the incident impact: P1 (critical - complete service outage), P2 (high - major functionality impaired), P3 (medium - partial degradation), P4 (low - minor issue).']
  user_satisfaction_rating varchar [note: 'Post-resolution satisfaction rating provided by the user (typically 1-5 scale, where 5 is highly satisfied).']
  vendor_ticket_number varchar [note: 'External ticket or case number assigned by a third-party vendor or service provider if the incident was escalated externally.']
  workaround_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temporary workaround was applied to restore service before a permanent fix was implemented.']
  workaround_description varchar [note: 'Description of the temporary workaround applied, if any.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of unplanned IT service disruptions and outages affecting NGO operations, including system downtime, network failures, data breaches, ransomware events, and platform unavailability. Captures incident category, severity level (P1–P4), affected systems, impacted country offices or programs, root cause analysis, mean time to resolution (MTTR), and escalation history. Distinct from service_request (which covers routine requests) — this captures unplanned disruptions.'
}

Table technology.network_site {
  network_site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the network site. Primary key for the network site entity.']
  upstream_network_site_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on network_site (upstream_network_site_id)']
  address varchar [note: 'Full physical address of the network site location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  backup_bandwidth_mbps decimal(12,2) [note: 'Bandwidth capacity in megabits per second (Mbps) available on the backup connectivity link.']
  backup_connectivity_type varchar [note: 'Secondary or backup connectivity technology available for failover in case primary connection fails (e.g., 4G LTE backup for fiber).']
  bandwidth_capacity_mbps decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum bandwidth capacity in megabits per second (Mbps) available at this network site.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the network site is located.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of compliance certifications or standards this network site adheres to (e.g., ISO 27001, SOC 2, CHS).']
  connectivity_type varchar [note: 'Primary connectivity technology used at this network site for internet and WAN access. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fiber|vsat|4g_lte|5g|microwave|satellite|dsl|cable|vpn — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the network site is physically located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this network site record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the network site was or will be decommissioned and taken out of service.']
  disaster_recovery_tier varchar [note: 'Disaster recovery priority tier assigned to this site (e.g., Tier 1 critical, Tier 2 important, Tier 3 standard) for business continuity planning.']
  equipment_inventory varchar [note: 'Summary list of key network equipment deployed at this site (routers, switches, access points, modems).']
  firewall_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a firewall is deployed and active at this network site for security protection.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the network site was initially installed and commissioned for operational use.']
  ip_address_range varchar [note: 'IP address range or subnet allocated to this network site (e.g., CIDR notation 10.20.30.0/24).']
  isp_contract_number varchar [note: 'Contract or account number with the ISP provider for billing and service management.']
  isp_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the Internet Service Provider or telecommunications carrier providing connectivity to this site.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent scheduled maintenance or inspection performed on this network site.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the network site for GIS mapping and infrastructure planning.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the network site for GIS mapping and infrastructure planning.']
  monthly_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average monthly operational cost in US Dollars for maintaining this network site, including ISP fees, equipment leases, and support.']
  network_administrator_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the network administrator responsible for this site. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  network_administrator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staff member or team responsible for managing and maintaining this network site.']
  network_administrator_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the network administrator for emergency contact and support escalation. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next planned maintenance or inspection of this network site.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or special instructions related to this network site.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the network site in its lifecycle.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the network site location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic or administrative region within the country (e.g., province, state, district) where the site is located.']
  security_classification varchar [note: 'Data security classification level assigned to this network site based on the sensitivity of data and systems it hosts.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the network site, typically used in network diagrams and infrastructure documentation.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the network site (e.g., \'Nairobi Country Office LAN\', \'Cox\'s Bazar Field Site VSAT\', \'Geneva HQ Data Center\').']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the network site based on organizational function and operational role.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this network site record was last modified or updated.']
  uptime_sla_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual uptime Service Level Agreement percentage guaranteed by the ISP (e.g., 99.9%).']
  vlan_number varchar [note: 'Virtual LAN identifier(s) configured at this network site for network segmentation and security.']
  vpn_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether VPN connectivity is configured and available at this network site for secure remote access.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical or logical network site in the NGO\'s connectivity infrastructure, including country office LANs, field site satellite/VSAT connections, HQ data center networks, and cloud VPN endpoints. Captures site name, location (country, region, GPS coordinates), connectivity type (fiber, VSAT, 4G/LTE, microwave), bandwidth capacity, ISP provider, uptime SLA, and network administrator responsible. SSOT for connectivity infrastructure topology.'
}

Table technology.connectivity_log {
  connectivity_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the connectivity log record.']
  country_office_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field office associated with this connectivity measurement.']
  it_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.it_incident. Business justification: Connectivity outages and network issues can trigger IT incidents. The incident_ticket_number STRING field should be normalized to FK. N:1 relationship - many connectivity log entries can be associated']
  network_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network site where connectivity was measured.']
  previous_connectivity_log_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on connectivity_log (previous_connectivity_log_id)']
  affected_users_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of users or staff affected by connectivity issues at this site.']
  bandwidth_utilization_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of available bandwidth currently in use at the time of measurement.']
  business_impact_description varchar [note: 'Description of the operational or programmatic impact caused by connectivity issues.']
  cause_classification varchar [note: 'Root cause category for connectivity issues or outages. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: isp_fault|power_outage|equipment_failure|weather|configuration_error|capacity_exceeded|security_incident|maintenance|natural_disaster|vandalism|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the root cause of connectivity issues or outage.']
  connection_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the network connection at the time of measurement.']
  connection_type varchar [note: 'Type of network connection technology used at this site.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this connectivity log record was first created in the system.']
  device_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the network device or router at the measurement site.']
  device_mac_address varchar [note: 'MAC address of the network device or router at the measurement site.']
  download_speed_mbps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured download speed in megabits per second.']
  isp_provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Internet Service Provider delivering connectivity to this site.']
  jitter_ms decimal(10,2) [note: 'Variation in latency measured in milliseconds, indicating network stability.']
  latency_ms decimal(10,2) [note: 'Network latency measured in milliseconds, representing the round-trip time for data packets.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the connectivity measurement was performed.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the connectivity measurement was captured.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this connectivity log record was last modified.']
  monitoring_tool varchar [note: 'Name of the network monitoring tool or system that captured this measurement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or observations related to this connectivity measurement.']
  outage_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of connectivity outage in minutes, if applicable to this measurement.']
  outage_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the connectivity outage was resolved.']
  outage_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the connectivity outage began.']
  packet_loss_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of data packets lost during transmission, indicating network quality.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this connectivity site for incident response.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the connectivity issue was fully resolved.']
  signal_strength_dbm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Signal strength measured in dBm for wireless connections.']
  sla_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this connectivity measurement meets the SLA requirements.']
  sla_target_uptime_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The contractual uptime percentage target defined in the SLA with the ISP.']
  upload_speed_mbps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured upload speed in megabits per second.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of network connectivity events and uptime/downtime measurements for each network site. Captures measurement timestamp, site reference, connection status (up/down/degraded), latency (ms), bandwidth utilization (%), packet loss percentage, outage duration, and cause classification (ISP fault, power outage, equipment failure, weather). Enables SLA compliance tracking and field connectivity reporting for humanitarian operations.'
}

Table technology.user_account {
  user_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the user account record. Primary key for digital identity management across all NGO platforms.']
  linked_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on user_account (linked_user_account_id)']
  partner_org_id varchar [note: 'Reference to partner organization for external authorized users from Community-Based Organizations (CBOs), Civil Society Organizations (CSOs), or other International Non-Governmental Organizations (INGOs).']
  primary_system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: User accounts have a primary system they\'re provisioned in (e.g., Active Directory, Salesforce, SAP). The primary_system STRING field should be normalized to FK. N:1 relationship - many user accounts ']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member record in the workforce domain. Links digital identity to HR identity for employees.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Overall access privilege level assigned to the user account. Determines baseline permissions across organizational systems.']
  account_locked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is locked due to excessive failed login attempts or security policy violation. True if locked; requires administrator intervention to unlock.']
  account_locked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account was locked. Null if account has never been locked.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the user account. Active accounts have full access; suspended accounts are temporarily disabled; locked accounts require admin intervention; deprovisioned accounts are permanently disabled.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the user account based on the relationship to the organization. Determines access policies and provisioning workflows.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the user account was activated and became available for login. May differ from provisioning date if account required approval workflow.']
  active_directory_guid varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier assigned by Active Directory. Used for cross-system identity federation and single sign-on (SSO).']
  beneficiary_data_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user has access to beneficiary registration, case management, and Person of Concern (PoC) data. True for program staff and case managers.']
  commcare_username varchar [note: 'Username for CommCare mobile case management platform. Used for community health worker tracking and beneficiary follow-up.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this user account record was first created in the system of record. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  data_protection_acknowledgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user has acknowledged and accepted organizational data protection policies including GDPR, beneficiary data handling, and confidentiality requirements.']
  deprovisioning_date date [note: 'Date when the account was permanently deprovisioned and access was revoked across all systems. Typically occurs upon staff departure or contract end.']
  deprovisioning_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for account deprovisioning. Captures the business justification for permanent access revocation.']
  dhis2_username varchar [note: 'Username for DHIS2 health program monitoring platform. Used for indicator tracking, aggregate reporting, and Monitoring Evaluation and Learning (MEL) activities.']
  donor_data_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user has access to donor management and constituent relationship management (CRM) systems. True for fundraising and development staff.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address associated with the user account. Used for system notifications, password resets, and organizational communication.']
  failed_login_attempts varchar [note: 'Count of consecutive failed login attempts since last successful authentication. Used for account lockout policies and security monitoring.']
  field_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user has access to field operations systems and mobile data collection platforms. True for field staff and community health workers.']
  financial_system_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user has access to financial systems including General Ledger (GL), Accounts Payable (AP), Accounts Receivable (AR), and grant financial management. True for finance staff.']
  kobotoolbox_username varchar [note: 'Username for KoboToolbox field data collection platform. Used for beneficiary surveys, needs assessments, and humanitarian data collection.']
  last_login_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful authentication to any organizational system. Used for dormant account detection and security monitoring.']
  last_password_change_date date [note: 'Date when the user last changed their password. Used to enforce password rotation policies and detect stale credentials.']
  mfa_enrolled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user has enrolled in multi-factor authentication. True if MFA is configured; false if only password authentication is enabled.']
  mfa_method varchar [note: 'The primary multi-factor authentication method configured for this account. None indicates MFA is not enabled.']
  mobile_device_registered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user has registered a mobile device for organizational access. True for users accessing CommCare, KoboToolbox, or other mobile platforms.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this user account record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking and compliance reporting.']
  password_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current password expires and must be changed. Enforces organizational password rotation policy.']
  privileged_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a privileged account with elevated permissions (administrator, super admin, system service). True for accounts requiring enhanced security monitoring.']
  provisioning_date date [note: 'Date when the user account was initially created and provisioned across organizational systems.']
  remote_access_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user is authorized for remote access via VPN or external networks. True for users working from field locations or home offices.']
  salesforce_user_code varchar [note: 'Unique user identifier in Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud. Links digital identity to donor management, constituent tracking, and campaign management activities.']
  sap_user_code varchar [note: 'User identifier in SAP S/4HANA for Nonprofits. Provides access to General Ledger (GL), Accounts Payable (AP), Accounts Receivable (AR), fund accounting, and procurement modules.']
  security_training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the user completed mandatory information security and data protection training. Required for compliance with organizational security policies.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'Date when the account was suspended. Null if account has never been suspended.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the account was suspended. Captures business justification for temporary access revocation.']
  username varchar [note: 'Unique username for system authentication. Primary login identifier across Active Directory, Salesforce, SAP, KoboToolbox, CommCare, DHIS2, and email systems.']
  workday_user_code varchar [note: 'User identifier in Workday Human Capital Management (HCM) system. Provides access to human resources, payroll, talent management, and learning modules.']

  Note: 'Master record for every digital identity and system access account provisioned for NGO staff, volunteers, and authorized partner users across all platforms (Active Directory, Salesforce, SAP, KoboToolbox, CommCare, DHIS2, email). Captures username, account type, associated staff or volunteer identity, provisioning date, last login, account status (active, suspended, deprovisioned), and multi-factor authentication (MFA) enrollment status. SSOT for digital identity management — distinct from workforce.staff_member (which owns HR identity).'
}

Table technology.access_role {
  access_role_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the access role record. Primary key.']
  parent_access_role_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on access_role (parent_access_role_id)']
  system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Access roles are defined within specific platforms (e.g., Salesforce Admin role, SAP Finance User role). The platform_system STRING field should be normalized to FK. N:1 relationship - many roles defi']
  access_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational scope of data access granted by this role (global, regional, country-level, program-specific, project-specific, department-specific).']
  active_user_count varchar [note: 'Current number of active users assigned to this role. Used for access governance reporting and license management.']
  approval_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or committee authorized to approve assignment of this role to users (e.g., Chief Information Officer, Executive Director, Security Committee).']
  approval_authority_title varchar [note: 'Job title or position of the approval authority (e.g., Chief Information Officer, Director of Information Security, Head of Human Resources).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the approval authority who formally approved this role definition for use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this role definition was formally approved by the designated approval authority.']
  audit_logging_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all actions performed by users with this role must be logged for audit and compliance purposes (True) or standard logging applies (False).']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Business rationale and justification for the existence and permission scope of this role.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory or compliance frameworks this role supports (e.g., OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200, GDPR, CHS, IATI, ASC 958).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this role record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification_level varchar [note: 'The highest data classification level this role is authorized to access (public, internal, confidential, restricted) per organizational data governance policy.']
  data_domain_access varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of data domains this role can access (e.g., Beneficiary, Donor, Finance, Program, Grant, Volunteer, Supply Chain, MEL).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this role definition expires or was retired. Null if the role is currently active with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this role definition became active and available for assignment to users.']
  last_recertification_date date [note: 'Date when this role definition was last reviewed and recertified by the role owner or approval authority.']
  least_privilege_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this role adheres to the principle of least privilege (True) or grants elevated/broad access (False).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the individual who last modified this role definition.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this role record was last updated in the system.']
  module_access varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of system modules or functional areas this role can access (e.g., Donor Management, Grant Accounting, Case Management, Field Data Collection).']
  multi_factor_authentication_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether users assigned this role must use multi-factor authentication (MFA) for access (True) or single-factor is permitted (False).']
  next_recertification_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next access recertification review of this role definition.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this role definition, assignment criteria, or usage guidelines.']
  permission_scope varchar [note: 'The level of access permission granted by this role (read-only, write, admin, execute, delete, full control).']
  privileged_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this role grants privileged or administrative access requiring enhanced monitoring and audit (True) or standard user access (False).']
  recertification_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required access recertification reviews for users assigned this role (e.g., 90, 180, 365). Null if recertification is not required.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification of this role based on the sensitivity of data and scope of permissions (low, medium, high, critical).']
  role_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or short code for the role (e.g., PROG_MGR, FIN_OFF, FLD_COORD) used across systems.']
