  role_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the role\'s purpose, responsibilities, and typical use cases within the organization.']
  role_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the role-based access control (RBAC) role (e.g., Program Manager, Finance Officer, Field Coordinator, Donor Relations Specialist).']
  role_owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the role owner for contact and accountability purposes.']
  role_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or department responsible for defining and maintaining this role (e.g., IT Director, HRIS Manager, Security Officer).']
  role_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the access role (active, inactive, suspended, deprecated, pending approval).']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the role by organizational function (functional, technical, administrative, executive, field operations, donor relations).']
  segregation_of_duties_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this role is subject to segregation of duties controls (True) to prevent conflicts of interest or fraud risk (False if not applicable).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the individual who created this role definition in the system.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of role-based access control (RBAC) roles and permission profiles defined across all NGO platforms and systems. Captures role name, associated system platform, permission scope (read/write/admin), data classification level accessible (public/internal/confidential/restricted), role owner, and approval authority. Enables least-privilege access governance and audit-ready access control documentation.'
}

Table technology.access_provisioning {
  access_provisioning_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the access provisioning transaction record.']
  access_role_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.access_role. Business justification: Access provisioning assigns specific RBAC roles to users. The access_role_assigned STRING field should be normalized to FK. N:1 relationship - many provisioning events assign one role. Core RBAC relat']
  approver_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the IT security officer, system owner, or authorized approver who reviewed and approved or rejected the access request.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: Access to donor-restricted data/systems must track grant authorization. Business need: data security compliance (donor audit requirements), segregation of duties enforcement, access review for grant-s']
  primary_access_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Human Resources Information System (HRIS) employee or volunteer identifier for the target user, formatted as EMP-XXXXXX (employee), VOL-XXXXXX (volunteer), or CON-XXXXXX (consultant).']
  primary_access_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member or system user who initiated the access provisioning request.']
  quaternary_access_provisioned_by_admin_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the IT system administrator or service desk technician who executed the access provisioning in the target system.']
  superseded_access_provisioning_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on access_provisioning (superseded_access_provisioning_id)']
  system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Access provisioning grants access to specific platforms (Salesforce, SAP, DHIS2, etc.). The target_system_platform STRING field should be normalized to FK. N:1 relationship - many provisioning events ']
  target_user_user_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member or volunteer for whom access is being provisioned, modified, or revoked.']
  tertiary_access_compliance_signoff_by_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance officer or data protection officer who provided formal sign-off for this access provisioning.']
  access_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the access is valid, calculated from effective start date to effective end date. Null for indefinite access.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Privilege level granted: read_only (view only), read_write (view and edit), admin (full module administration), super_admin (system-wide administration), or restricted (limited scope access).']
  access_review_due_date date [note: 'Date when this access provisioning is scheduled for periodic review to validate continued business need and compliance with least privilege principle.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the access provisioning request was approved or rejected by the designated approver.']
  beneficiary_data_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this access provisioning grants permission to view or edit beneficiary personally identifiable information (PII) and program participation records (True/False).']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the business need and rationale for the access provisioning request, including job role requirements, project assignment, or operational necessity.']
  compliance_signoff_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this access provisioning requires formal compliance officer or data protection officer sign-off before activation (True/False).']
  compliance_signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the compliance officer provided formal sign-off approval for this access provisioning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this access provisioning record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification_access_level varchar [note: 'Highest data classification level the user is authorized to access through this provisioning: public (unrestricted), internal (staff only), confidential (business sensitive), or restricted (PII/PHI/donor data).']
  deprovisioning_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for access revocation or suspension, including termination, role change, security incident, policy violation, or contract end. Populated only for revoke or suspend request types.']
  donor_audit_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this access provisioning is subject to donor audit requirements and must maintain enhanced documentation for compliance reporting (True/False).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the provisioned access expires or is scheduled for automatic revocation. Null indicates indefinite access subject to periodic review.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the provisioned access becomes active and the user is authorized to use the system or platform.']
  financial_data_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this access provisioning grants permission to view or edit financial transactions, donor contributions, or grant accounting data (True/False).']
  jml_lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Stage in the employee lifecycle that triggered this access provisioning: joiner (new hire), mover (role change/transfer), leaver (termination/resignation), contractor_onboard (external consultant start), or contractor_offboard (external consultant end).']
  last_access_review_date date [note: 'Date when this access provisioning was last reviewed and validated by the system owner or security officer.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this access provisioning record was most recently updated.']
  multi_factor_authentication_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provisioned access requires multi-factor authentication for login and session management (True/False).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information related to the access provisioning request and fulfillment.']
  provisioning_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the IT administrator or automated provisioning system completed the access setup in the target system.']
  remote_access_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user is authorized to access the system remotely from outside the organizational network (True/False).']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for the access provisioning request, formatted as APR-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the access provisioning request in the approval and fulfillment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|pending_approval|approved|rejected|in_progress|completed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the access provisioning request was formally submitted for approval.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Type of access provisioning action requested: grant (new access), modify (change existing access), revoke (remove access), suspend (temporary disable), reinstate (restore suspended access), or transfer (move access to different user).']
  security_incident_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the security incident that triggered emergency access revocation or suspension, formatted as INC-XXXXXXXX. Populated only when access change is incident-driven.']
  target_system_environment varchar [note: 'Environment tier of the target system where access is being provisioned: production (live operational), staging (pre-production testing), development (build environment), training (user education), or sandbox (isolated testing).']
  target_user_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the target user receiving the access provisioning action, used for notification and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every user access provisioning, modification, and deprovisioning event across all NGO systems and platforms. Captures request type (grant, modify, revoke), requesting manager, approver, target system platform, access role assigned, justification, effective date, and compliance sign-off. Supports joiner-mover-leaver (JML) lifecycle governance and donor audit requirements for data access controls.'
}

Table technology.security_control {
  security_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the security control record. Primary key.']
  enforced_psea_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safeguarding.psea_policy. Business justification: Security controls (encryption, access restrictions, audit logging, data retention) implement specific safeguarding policy requirements. Compliance teams need to map which technical controls enforce wh']
  parent_security_control_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on security_control (parent_security_control_id)']
  primary_system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Security controls are implemented on specific platforms. While applicable_systems is a STRING list (controls often apply to multiple systems), this FK tracks the PRIMARY platform. N:1 relationship - m']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this security control record is currently active and in use (True) or has been archived or decommissioned (False).']
  annual_maintenance_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated annual cost to maintain, operate, and support this security control, including subscription fees, support contracts, and staff time.']
  applicable_data_classification varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of data classification levels protected by this control (e.g., restricted, confidential, internal, public). Indicates which data sensitivity tiers require this control.']
  applicable_systems varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IT systems, platforms, or applications to which this security control applies (e.g., Salesforce, SAP, DHIS2, KoboToolbox, CommCare, network infrastructure).']
  audit_trail_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this control requires detailed audit logging and trail retention for compliance or forensic purposes (True/False).']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree to which the security control is automated: manual (human-executed), semi-automated (human-initiated with automated execution), or fully automated (no human intervention required).']
  compensating_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this control serves as a compensating control for another control that cannot be fully implemented (True/False).']
  compliance_mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this control is mandatory for regulatory or donor compliance requirements (True) or is a best-practice recommendation (False).']
  control_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the security control objective, scope, and implementation approach.']
  control_domain varchar [note: 'Primary security domain or category to which this control belongs (e.g., access management, encryption, vulnerability management, incident response). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: access_management|asset_management|cryptography|physical_security|operations_security|communications_security|system_acquisition|supplier_relationships|incident_management|business_continuity|compliance|vulnerability_management|identity_management|network_security|endpoint_security|data_protection — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  control_identifier varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the security control, typically following framework naming conventions (e.g., ISO-27001-A.9.1.1, NIST-CSF-PR.AC-1, CIS-5.1).']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the security control (e.g., Access Control Policy, Multi-Factor Authentication, Encryption at Rest).']
  control_objective varchar [note: 'Specific security objective or goal that this control is designed to achieve (e.g., prevent unauthorized access, detect malware, ensure data integrity).']
  control_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or role responsible for the ongoing management and effectiveness of this security control.']
  control_owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or unit to which the control owner belongs (e.g., IT Security, Infrastructure, Compliance).']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the control by its function: preventive (stops incidents), detective (identifies incidents), corrective (remediates incidents), deterrent (discourages threats), compensating (alternative control), or directive (policy-based).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this security control record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost fields (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  effectiveness_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment outcome indicating whether the control is achieving its intended security objective: effective (fully meeting objectives), partially effective (meeting some objectives), ineffective (not meeting objectives), or not tested (no assessment conducted).']
  evidence_location varchar [note: 'File path, URL, or document reference where evidence of control implementation and effectiveness is stored for audit purposes.']
  exception_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the granted exception expires and the control must be re-evaluated or implemented.']
  exception_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal exception or waiver has been granted for non-implementation or partial implementation of this control (True/False).']
  exception_justification varchar [note: 'Business or technical justification for granting an exception to this security control requirement.']
  framework_mapping varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of security frameworks and specific control identifiers to which this control maps (e.g., ISO-27001:A.9.1.1, NIST-CSF:PR.AC-1, CIS-Controls:5.1). Supports multi-framework compliance mapping.']
  implementation_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to implement this security control, including software licenses, hardware, consulting, and labor.']
  implementation_date date [note: 'Date when the security control was fully implemented and became operational.']
  implementation_guidance varchar [note: 'Detailed guidance, procedures, or references for implementing and maintaining this security control.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the security control implementation.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent assessment, audit, or effectiveness test of this security control.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this security control record was last updated or modified.']
  maturity_level varchar [note: 'Maturity assessment of the control implementation based on capability maturity model: initial (ad-hoc), developing (repeatable), defined (documented process), managed (measured and controlled), optimizing (continuous improvement).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified this security control record.']
  next_assessment_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled assessment or audit of this security control.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context regarding the security control implementation, assessment, or operational considerations.']
  related_policy_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational policy, standard, or procedure document that governs this security control.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which remediation actions must be completed to bring the control to effective status.']
  remediation_plan varchar [note: 'Description of the action plan to address control deficiencies, gaps, or ineffectiveness identified during assessments.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with failure or absence of this control, indicating the potential impact to the organization.']
  testing_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which the security control effectiveness is tested or validated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: continuous|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annually|annually|ad_hoc — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vendor_solution varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party vendor or technology solution used to implement this control (e.g., CrowdStrike, Qualys, Okta, Splunk).']

  Note: 'Master catalog of information security controls implemented across the NGO\'s technology environment, mapped to frameworks such as ISO 27001, NIST CSF, and CIS Controls. Captures control ID, control domain (access management, encryption, vulnerability management, incident response), implementation status, control owner, last assessment date, and effectiveness rating. Supports cybersecurity governance and donor audit readiness.'
}

Table technology.security_assessment {
  security_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the security assessment record. Primary key.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: Security assessments may be donor-required (e.g., USAID ADS 545 compliance) or grant-funded. Business need: donor compliance reporting, grant budget tracking for assessment costs, audit evidence for d']
  followup_security_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on security_assessment (followup_security_assessment_id)']
  system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IT system or platform being assessed (e.g., Salesforce, SAP, DHIS2, KoboToolbox).']
  assessment_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for conducting the security assessment, including consultant fees, tool licenses, and internal resource costs.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the security assessment was conducted or completed.']
  assessment_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the security assessment activities concluded.']
  assessment_frequency varchar [note: 'Planned frequency for conducting security assessments on this system (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, ad-hoc, or continuous monitoring).']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or reference number for the security assessment, used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  assessment_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scope of the security assessment, including systems, networks, applications, or infrastructure components covered.']
  assessment_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the security assessment activities commenced.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the security assessment.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of security assessment conducted: penetration test (simulated attack), vulnerability scan (automated tool scan), ISMS audit (Information Security Management System review), third-party review (external consultant assessment), compliance audit (regulatory check), or risk assessment (threat analysis).']
  assessor_certification varchar [note: 'Professional certifications held by the assessor (e.g., CISSP, CEH, CISA, OSCP), demonstrating competence and credibility.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lead assessor or security professional who conducted the assessment.']
  beneficiary_data_at_risk boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the assessed system processes or stores beneficiary personally identifiable information (PII) or sensitive program data, requiring heightened protection per humanitarian standards.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the assessed system meets applicable compliance requirements (e.g., ISO 27001, GDPR, CHS, donor security requirements).']
  conducting_entity_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization, department, or individual who conducted the security assessment (e.g., internal IT Security team, external firm name).']
  conducting_entity_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment was conducted by internal IT/security staff, external consultants/auditors, or a joint team.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this security assessment record was first created in the system.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical severity findings identified during the assessment. Critical findings represent immediate threats requiring urgent remediation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the assessment cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  data_classification_assessed varchar [note: 'Highest data classification level of information processed or stored on the assessed system, determining assessment rigor requirements.']
  donor_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assessment results must be reported to donors as part of grant compliance or security attestation requirements.']
  executive_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of assessment findings, risk posture, and key recommendations for executive leadership.']
  high_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of high severity findings identified during the assessment. High findings represent significant vulnerabilities requiring prompt attention.']
  key_recommendations varchar [note: 'Primary recommendations for improving security posture based on assessment findings.']
  low_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of low severity findings identified during the assessment. Low findings represent minor issues or informational observations.']
  medium_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of medium severity findings identified during the assessment. Medium findings represent moderate risks requiring planned remediation.']
  methodology_used varchar [note: 'Security assessment methodology or framework used (e.g., OWASP Testing Guide, NIST SP 800-115, PTES, OSSTMM).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier of the person who last modified this security assessment record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this security assessment record was last modified.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next security assessment of this system or infrastructure, based on organizational policy or regulatory requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or context related to the security assessment.']
  overall_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the assessed system or infrastructure based on the aggregate findings and their severity.']
  remediation_deadline date [note: 'Target date by which identified vulnerabilities and findings must be remediated or mitigated.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of remediation efforts for findings identified in this assessment.']
  report_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the detailed assessment report document stored in the document management system.']
  report_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal assessment report was issued to stakeholders.']
  tools_used varchar [note: 'List of security tools and software used during the assessment (e.g., Nessus, Burp Suite, Metasploit, Qualys).']
  total_findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of findings identified across all severity levels during the assessment.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier of the person who created this security assessment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of formal information security assessments, vulnerability scans, penetration tests, and cybersecurity audits conducted on NGO systems and infrastructure. Captures assessment type (penetration test, vulnerability scan, ISMS audit, third-party review), scope, conducting entity (internal/external), assessment date, findings count by severity (critical/high/medium/low), overall risk rating, and remediation deadline. Distinct from it_incident (which captures actual breaches).'
}

Table technology.vulnerability {
  vulnerability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cybersecurity vulnerability record. Primary key for the vulnerability data product.']
  it_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.it_asset. Business justification: Vulnerabilities can be specific to individual IT assets (e.g., unpatched laptop, vulnerable server). The affected_asset_identifier STRING field should be normalized to FK. N:1 relationship - many vuln']
  related_incident_it_incident_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of any security incident that resulted from or is related to the exploitation of this vulnerability.']
  related_vulnerability_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vulnerability (related_vulnerability_id)']
  system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Vulnerabilities are discovered in specific platforms (e.g., Salesforce CVE, SAP security advisory). The affected_system_name STRING field should be normalized to FK. N:1 relationship - many vulnerabil']
  actual_remediation_date date [note: 'Actual date when the vulnerability was successfully remediated or closed, after verification of fix effectiveness.']
  affected_component varchar [note: 'Specific component, module, library, or service within the system that contains the vulnerability (e.g., Apache Struts, OpenSSL, Windows RDP).']
  affected_data_classification varchar [note: 'Highest data classification level of information that could be exposed or compromised if this vulnerability is exploited.']
  affected_version varchar [note: 'Version number or range of the affected software, application, or component that contains the vulnerability.']
  business_impact varchar [note: 'Description of the potential business impact if the vulnerability is exploited, including operational disruption, data breach, financial loss, or reputational damage.']
  compliance_impact varchar [note: 'Description of regulatory or compliance implications if the vulnerability is not remediated, including potential violations of donor requirements, GDPR, PCI-DSS, or other frameworks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vulnerability record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  cve_identifier varchar [note: 'Standardized CVE identifier assigned by MITRE for publicly disclosed cybersecurity vulnerabilities. Format: CVE-YYYY-NNNNN.']
  cvss_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'Numerical severity score of the vulnerability based on CVSS framework, ranging from 0.0 (informational) to 10.0 (critical).']
  cvss_vector varchar [note: 'CVSS vector string representing the detailed scoring metrics (Attack Vector, Attack Complexity, Privileges Required, User Interaction, Scope, Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability).']
  discovery_date date [note: 'Date when the vulnerability was first discovered or identified within the organization\'s environment.']
  discovery_method varchar [note: 'Method or source through which the vulnerability was identified (e.g., vulnerability scan, penetration test, vendor security advisory, internal audit).']
  exploitability_status varchar [note: 'Current state of exploit availability and active exploitation: whether exploit code exists, is publicly available, or is being actively used in attacks.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified the vulnerability record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vulnerability record was last updated or modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context about the vulnerability, remediation efforts, or organizational decisions regarding risk acceptance.']
  patch_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a vendor-provided patch or security update is available to remediate the vulnerability.']
  patch_identifier varchar [note: 'Vendor-assigned identifier or reference number for the security patch or update that addresses the vulnerability (e.g., MS21-001, KB4012598).']
  reference_urls varchar [note: 'Additional reference links to external resources, security bulletins, or technical documentation related to the vulnerability (comma-separated list).']
  remediation_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team responsible for remediating the vulnerability (e.g., IT Security Team, System Administrator, Application Owner).']
  remediation_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed plan or approach for addressing the vulnerability, including patching, configuration changes, compensating controls, or risk acceptance rationale.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual, team, vendor, or security researcher who reported or discovered the vulnerability.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Organizational risk rating assigned to the vulnerability based on CVSS score, exploitability, asset criticality, and business impact.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative severity classification of the vulnerability based on CVSS score ranges: Critical (9.0-10.0), High (7.0-8.9), Medium (4.0-6.9), Low (0.1-3.9), Informational (0.0).']
  target_remediation_date date [note: 'Planned or target date by which the vulnerability should be remediated, based on severity and organizational risk tolerance.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title or name of the vulnerability for quick identification and reference.']
  vendor_advisory_url varchar [note: 'Web link to the vendor\'s official security advisory or bulletin providing details about the vulnerability and remediation guidance.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the remediation was verified and confirmed effective through re-scanning or testing.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of post-remediation verification testing to confirm the vulnerability has been successfully resolved and no longer exploitable.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team who performed the verification testing to confirm successful remediation.']
  vulnerability_description varchar [note: 'Detailed technical description of the vulnerability, including the nature of the weakness, potential exploit vectors, and affected components.']
  vulnerability_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vulnerability: Open (newly identified), In Progress (remediation underway), Remediated (fix applied), Accepted (risk accepted), Mitigated (compensating controls applied), Closed (verified resolved).']
  vulnerability_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vulnerability by attack type or weakness category (e.g., SQL Injection, XSS, Buffer Overflow, Authentication Bypass). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SQL Injection|Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)|Buffer Overflow|Authentication Bypass|Privilege Escalation|Remote Code Execution|Denial of Service|Information Disclosure|Insecure Deserialization|Security Misconfiguration — promote to reference product]']
  workaround_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temporary workaround or compensating control is available to mitigate the vulnerability until a permanent fix is applied.']
  workaround_description varchar [note: 'Description of the temporary workaround or compensating control that can reduce risk until the vulnerability is fully remediated.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created the vulnerability record in the system.']

  Note: 'Master record of identified cybersecurity vulnerabilities and weaknesses in NGO systems, applications, and infrastructure. Captures CVE identifier, affected system or asset, vulnerability severity (CVSS score), discovery method (scan, pen test, vendor advisory), discovery date, remediation status, assigned remediation owner, target remediation date, and actual closure date. Enables vulnerability lifecycle management and risk prioritization.'
}

Table technology.software_license {
  software_license_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the software license record. Primary key.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: Software licenses purchased with grant funds must track funding source. Business need: allowable cost verification (donor-specific software restrictions), grant budget compliance, audit trail for gran']
  system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Software licenses are often tied to specific platforms they enable (e.g., Salesforce licenses, SAP licenses, Microsoft 365 for specific platform). N:1 relationship - many licenses for one platform. En']
  upgraded_from_software_license_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on software_license (upgraded_from_software_license_id)']
  annual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total annual cost of the license or subscription in the specified currency. For multi-year agreements, this represents the annualized cost. Used for budget tracking and cost allocation.']
  auto_renewal_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the license or subscription is configured for automatic renewal. True if auto-renewal is active, false if manual renewal is required.']
  compliance_frameworks varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory or industry compliance frameworks that this software must adhere to (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, SOC 2, Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS), International Aid Transparency Initiative (IATI), OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the organization\'s usage is compliant with the license terms: compliant (usage within purchased entitlements), non-compliant (over-deployed or terms violation), under review (compliance check in progress), or audit required (vendor audit requested).']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the master service agreement, Memorandum of Understanding (MoU), or Letter of Agreement (LoA) governing this license.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The organizational cost center or department code to which this license cost is allocated (e.g., IT, Programs, Finance). Used for financial reporting and budget tracking per OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.']
  cost_per_seat decimal(15,2) [note: 'The cost per individual license seat or user, calculated as annual cost divided by total seats purchased. Used for cost optimization and per-user budget allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this license record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the annual cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  data_classification_level varchar [note: 'The highest data classification level that this software is authorized to process or store (restricted, confidential, internal, public), per the organization\'s data governance policy.']
  deployment_type varchar [note: 'The deployment model for the licensed software: cloud (vendor-hosted cloud infrastructure), on-premise (NGO-hosted on local servers), hybrid (combination of cloud and on-premise), or SaaS (Software as a Service, fully managed by vendor).']
  disaster_recovery_tier varchar [note: 'The disaster recovery priority tier for this software: tier 1 (critical, immediate recovery), tier 2 (important, recovery within hours), tier 3 (standard, recovery within days), tier 4 (low priority), or not applicable.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date the license becomes active and usage rights begin. For subscriptions, this is the start of the current billing period.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date the license expires or the subscription term ends. Null for perpetual licenses. Critical for renewal planning and compliance tracking.']
  is_mission_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this software is mission-critical to humanitarian operations or core business functions. True if downtime would severely impact program delivery or beneficiary services.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent internal or vendor-initiated license compliance audit.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known license key, agreement reference, or subscription identifier provided by the vendor (e.g., Microsoft 365 tenant ID, Salesforce org ID, SAP license key).']
  license_owner_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the license owner for renewal notifications and vendor communications.']
  license_owner_name varchar [note: 'The name of the staff member or role responsible for managing this license, including renewals, compliance, and vendor relationship management.']
  license_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the license: active (in use and compliant), expired (past renewal date), suspended (temporarily disabled), pending renewal (approaching expiration), cancelled (terminated), or trial (evaluation period).']
  license_type varchar [note: 'The licensing model governing usage rights: perpetual (one-time purchase, indefinite use), subscription (recurring payment, time-bound), concurrent (shared pool of active sessions), named user (assigned to specific individuals), device (tied to hardware), site (location-based), enterprise (organization-wide), or open source (freely licensed). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: perpetual|subscription|concurrent|named_user|device|site|enterprise|open_source — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the person who last modified this license record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this license record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next license compliance audit or review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information, special terms, renewal reminders, or vendor-specific notes related to this license.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'The billing cycle or payment schedule for the license: monthly, quarterly, annually, one-time (perpetual), or multi-year (prepaid multi-year subscription).']
  primary_business_domain varchar [note: 'The primary business function or domain supported by this software (e.g., Donor Management, Program Management, Financial Management, Field Data Collection, Monitoring Evaluation and Learning).']
  product_name varchar [note: 'The name of the software product or Software as a Service (SaaS) subscription (e.g., Microsoft 365 E5, Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud, SAP S/4HANA, KoboToolbox, CommCare, DHIS2, Tableau Creator).']
  product_version varchar [note: 'The version or release number of the licensed software product (e.g., SAP S/4HANA 2021, Salesforce Spring \'23, DHIS2 2.38).']
  purchase_date date [note: 'The date the license was originally purchased or the subscription was first activated.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'The purchase order number associated with the license procurement, linking to the organization\'s Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Unit4 ERP).']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for license renewal or subscription extension. Used for proactive renewal management and budget planning.']
  seats_available varchar [note: 'The number of unused licenses available for assignment (calculated as total_seats_purchased minus seats_consumed).']
  seats_consumed varchar [note: 'The number of licenses currently assigned or actively in use by staff, volunteers, or partners. Used to track utilization and identify over-provisioning or under-provisioning.']
  support_tier varchar [note: 'The level of vendor support included with the license: basic (email support), standard (business hours support), premium (24/7 support with SLA), enterprise (dedicated account manager), or community (open-source community support).']
  technical_contact_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the technical contact for vendor support escalations and technical communications.']
  technical_contact_name varchar [note: 'The name of the technical staff member responsible for software implementation, configuration, and technical support.']
  total_seats_purchased varchar [note: 'The total number of user licenses, seats, or entitlements purchased under this license agreement. For concurrent licenses, this represents the maximum simultaneous users allowed.']
  vendor_account_number varchar [note: 'The organization\'s customer account number or tenant identifier with the vendor (e.g., Salesforce Org ID, Microsoft tenant ID, SAP customer number).']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the software vendor or publisher (e.g., Microsoft, Salesforce, SAP, Dimagi, DHIS2 Core Team).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the person who created this license record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all software licenses and SaaS subscriptions managed by the NGO, including Microsoft 365, Salesforce, SAP, KoboToolbox, CommCare, DHIS2, and security tools. Captures license type (perpetual, subscription, concurrent, named user), vendor, product name, total seats purchased, seats consumed, license key or agreement reference, renewal date, annual cost, and cost center allocation. Enables license compliance and renewal management.'
}

Table technology.it_project {
  it_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IT project record. Primary key.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: IT projects are often grant-funded (e.g., USAID Digital Development, Gates Foundation technology grants). Business need: cost allocation to grant budgets, donor reporting on technology investments, al']
  it_manager_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.staff_member']
  it_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.staff_member']
  parent_it_project_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on it_project (parent_it_project_id)']
  primary_it_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member serving as the IT project manager, responsible for planning, execution, and delivery.']
  primary_system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: IT projects often focus on implementing, upgrading, or integrating a primary platform. While affected_systems is a STRING list (multiple), this FK tracks the PRIMARY platform. N:1 relationship - many ']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.staff_member']
  tertiary_it_business_sponsor_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the senior business stakeholder (typically a director or VP) who sponsors and champions the project.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure incurred to date on the project, tracked against the approved budget.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when the project was completed and deliverables were accepted by the business sponsor. Null for projects still in progress.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when project execution commenced, which may differ from the planned start date due to delays or early initiation.']
  affected_systems varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of system platforms or applications impacted by this project (e.g., \'Salesforce, SAP S/4HANA, Power BI\'). Links to technology.system_platform records.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total approved budget envelope for the IT project, including all cost categories (labor, software licenses, hardware, consulting, training).']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Business case or rationale for the project, including problem statement, expected benefits, and alignment with organizational strategy.']
  compliance_frameworks varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of compliance frameworks or standards that this project must adhere to (e.g., \'GDPR, IATI, CHS, OMB 2 CFR 200, ISO 27001\').']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the contract or Statement of Work (SoW) governing vendor engagement for this project.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IT project record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for budget and cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  data_classification_level varchar [note: 'Highest data classification level of information handled by systems or processes within the scope of this project.']
  delivery_methodology varchar [note: 'Project management methodology used for delivery: agile (sprint-based, iterative), waterfall (sequential phases), hybrid (combination of agile and waterfall), or iterative (phased releases).']
  forecast_end_date date [note: 'Current forecast or revised completion date based on actual progress and known risks, updated regularly during project execution.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of funding for the IT project (e.g., \'Unrestricted Operating Budget\', \'USAID Digital Transformation Grant\', \'DFID ICT4D Program\', \'Internal IT Capital Fund\').']
  go_live_date date [note: 'Date when the system, platform, or solution delivered by the project was deployed to production and made available to end users.']
  health_status varchar [note: 'RAG (Red-Amber-Green) health indicator for the project: green (on track, no issues), yellow (at risk, requires attention), red (critical issues, intervention needed). Assessed based on schedule, budget, scope, and quality metrics.']
  integration_count varchar [note: 'Number of system integrations (APIs, ETL pipelines, data feeds) developed or modified as part of this project.']
  it_project_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the project objectives, scope, deliverables, and expected business outcomes.']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Summary of key lessons learned during project execution, documented during project closure for knowledge transfer and continuous improvement.']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Summary of key milestone completion status, typically a brief text indicating which major milestones have been achieved (e.g., \'Requirements Complete, Design 80%, Build 40%\').']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this IT project record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IT project record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or context relevant to the project that do not fit into other structured fields.']
  percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall project completion percentage (0.00 to 100.00), calculated based on earned value, milestone completion, or weighted task progress.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Originally planned completion date for the project, as documented in the project charter or initial plan.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Originally planned start date for project execution, as documented in the project charter or initial plan.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the project for resource allocation and scheduling decisions.']
  project_category varchar [note: 'Strategic classification of the project: strategic (aligned with multi-year digital transformation roadmap), operational (business-as-usual system maintenance or enhancement), compliance (regulatory or donor-mandated requirement), or innovation (pilot, proof-of-concept, emerging technology exploration).']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code for the IT project, typically following organizational naming conventions (e.g., DHIS-2024-001, SAP-MIG-2023).']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the IT project (e.g., \'Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud Implementation\', \'DHIS2 Upgrade to Version 2.39\', \'Data Governance Framework Rollout\').']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the IT project: planning (requirements gathering, design), active (in execution), on_hold (temporarily paused), completed (delivered but not yet closed), cancelled (terminated before completion), or closed (formally closed with lessons learned documented).']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the IT project by its primary objective: system implementation (new platform deployment), platform migration (moving from one system to another), infrastructure upgrade (hardware/network improvements), data governance (policies, standards, MDM), security enhancement (cybersecurity, compliance), or integration development (API, ETL, data pipelines).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment for the project based on identified risks, dependencies, complexity, and external factors.']
  sponsoring_domain varchar [note: 'The primary business domain or functional area that sponsors and benefits from this IT project (e.g., \'Program\', \'Finance\', \'Monitoring and Evaluation\', \'Donor Relations\'). Maps to the organization\'s data domain taxonomy.']
  success_criteria varchar [note: 'Measurable criteria that define project success, including Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), acceptance criteria, and business outcome targets.']
  user_count varchar [note: 'Estimated or actual number of end users who will be impacted by or will use the solution delivered by this project.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary external vendor, consultant, or implementation partner engaged for this project (if applicable).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this IT project record.']

  Note: 'Master record for technology projects and digital transformation initiatives managed by the IT department, including system implementations, platform migrations, infrastructure upgrades, and data governance programs. Captures project name, project type, sponsoring business domain, project manager, budget envelope, start date, planned end date, actual end date, milestone status, and delivery methodology (Agile/Waterfall). Distinct from program.intervention (which owns humanitarian program projects).'
}

Table technology.platform_integration {
  platform_integration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the platform integration record. Primary key.']
  owner_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.staff_member']
  platform_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.staff_member']
  primary_platform_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member who is the business owner responsible for this integration.']
  replaced_platform_integration_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on platform_integration (replaced_platform_integration_id)']
  source_system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source system platform from which data originates in this integration.']
  target_system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target system platform to which data is loaded in this integration.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Authentication mechanism used to secure the integration connection (OAuth2, API key, basic auth, certificate, SAML, none).']
  compliance_frameworks varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of compliance frameworks this integration must adhere to (e.g., \'GDPR, CHS, IATI, 2 CFR 200\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this integration record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification_level varchar [note: 'Highest data classification level of the payload being transferred (restricted, confidential, internal, public).']
  data_domain varchar [note: 'Business data domain that this integration serves (e.g., \'Beneficiary\', \'Donor\', \'Program\', \'Finance\', \'Monitoring and Evaluation\').']
  data_entity varchar [note: 'Primary data entity or object being exchanged (e.g., \'Beneficiary Registration\', \'Donor Gift\', \'Program Indicator\', \'Financial Transaction\').']
  data_transformation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data transformation or mapping is required between source and target schemas (true) or not (false).']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the integration was or will be decommissioned and retired from service.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the technical documentation, integration specification, or runbook for this integration.']
  encryption_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data in transit is encrypted (true) or not (false).']
  encryption_protocol varchar [note: 'Encryption protocol used for data in transit (e.g., \'TLS 1.3\', \'AES-256\').']
  error_handling_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy for handling errors during integration execution (retry, skip, halt, log and continue).']
  go_live_date date [note: 'Date when the integration was first activated in production.']
  integration_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the integration for technical reference and logging.']
  integration_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the integration (e.g., \'KoboToolbox to DHIS2 Beneficiary Sync\', \'Salesforce to SAP Donor Gift Feed\').']
  integration_pattern varchar [note: 'Technical integration pattern employed (API, ETL, webhook, file transfer, message queue, database replication).']
  integration_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the integration pattern used (data sync, event stream, batch transfer, API integration, file exchange, webhook).']
  last_failed_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent failed execution of this integration.']
  last_successful_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful execution of this integration.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this integration record was last modified.']
  monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated monitoring and alerting is enabled for this integration (true) or not (false).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context about the integration for operational reference.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the integration (active, inactive, suspended, testing, decommissioned).']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of retry attempts configured for failed integration runs.']
  sla_uptime_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target uptime percentage defined in the service level agreement for this integration (e.g., 99.50 for 99.5%).']
  source_endpoint varchar [note: 'Technical endpoint, API URL, or file path from which data is extracted in the source system.']
  sync_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which data is synchronized between source and target systems (real-time, hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, on-demand).']
  sync_schedule varchar [note: 'Detailed schedule specification (e.g., cron expression, time of day) for batch integrations.']
  target_endpoint varchar [note: 'Technical endpoint, API URL, or file path to which data is loaded in the target system.']
  timeout_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum time in seconds allowed for a single integration execution before timeout.']
  total_records_transferred varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of records successfully transferred through this integration since go-live.']
  transformation_logic varchar [note: 'Description or reference to the transformation logic, mapping rules, or ETL script applied during data exchange.']

  Note: 'Master record for all system-to-system integrations and data exchange pipelines connecting NGO platforms, such as KoboToolbox-to-DHIS2, Salesforce-to-SAP, CommCare-to-beneficiary registry, and IATI publishing pipelines. Captures integration name, source system, target system, integration pattern (API, ETL, webhook, file transfer), data frequency, data classification of payload, integration owner, and operational status. SSOT for the integration architecture landscape.'
}

Table technology.backup_schedule {
  backup_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the backup schedule record. Primary key.']
  parent_backup_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on backup_schedule (parent_backup_schedule_id)']
  system_platform_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IT system or platform covered by this backup schedule.']
  alert_on_failure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated alerts are sent when a backup fails or encounters errors.']
  alert_recipients varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of email addresses or distribution lists that receive backup failure alerts.']
  backup_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which backups are executed for this schedule.']
  backup_type varchar [note: 'Type of backup performed: full (complete copy), incremental (changes since last backup), differential (changes since last full), snapshot (point-in-time), or continuous (real-time replication).']
  backup_verification_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated verification and integrity checks are performed on backup files after completion.']
  backup_window_end_time timestamp [note: 'Time of day when the backup window ends, in HH:MM format (24-hour).']
  backup_window_start_time timestamp [note: 'Time of day when the backup window begins, in HH:MM format (24-hour).']
  compliance_frameworks varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of compliance frameworks or regulations applicable to this backup schedule (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2, ISO27001).']
  compression_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether backup data is compressed to reduce storage space and transfer time.']
  compression_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average compression ratio achieved for backups (e.g., 2.5 means data is compressed to 40% of original size).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this backup schedule record was first created in the system.']
  data_asset_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific data asset, database, or repository covered by this backup schedule.']
  data_classification_level varchar [note: 'Highest data classification level of the data asset covered by this backup schedule, determining security and access controls.']
  disaster_recovery_tier varchar [note: 'Disaster recovery tier classification indicating the criticality and priority of this backup schedule for business continuity planning.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this backup schedule was deactivated or superseded. Null if currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this backup schedule became active and backups began executing.']
  encryption_algorithm varchar [note: 'Encryption algorithm used to secure backup data (e.g., AES-256, RSA-2048).']
  encryption_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether backup data is encrypted at rest and in transit.']
  last_backup_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration in minutes of the most recent backup execution.']
  last_backup_size_gb decimal(18,3) [note: 'Size of the most recent backup in gigabytes.']
  last_backup_status varchar [note: 'Outcome status of the most recent backup execution attempt.']
  last_backup_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful backup execution for this schedule.']
  last_verification_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent backup verification or integrity check.']
  last_verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent backup verification or integrity check.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this backup schedule record was last modified.']
  next_scheduled_backup_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the next backup is scheduled to execute.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this backup schedule.']
  responsible_team varchar [note: 'Name of the IT team or department responsible for managing and monitoring this backup schedule.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days that backup copies are retained before being eligible for deletion or archival.']
  rpo_minutes varchar [note: 'Recovery Point Objective in minutes: the maximum acceptable amount of data loss measured in time. Defines how much data the organization can afford to lose.']
  rto_minutes varchar [note: 'Recovery Time Objective in minutes: the maximum acceptable time to restore the system or data after a disruption. Defines how quickly the organization must recover.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the backup schedule, used for external reference and reporting.']
  schedule_cron_expression varchar [note: 'Cron expression defining the precise timing and recurrence pattern for automated backup execution.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the backup schedule describing the system or data asset covered.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the backup schedule.']
  storage_location_type varchar [note: 'Primary storage location type for backup data: on-site (local datacenter), cloud (cloud provider), offsite (third-party facility), or hybrid (multiple locations).']
  storage_path varchar [note: 'File system path, bucket name, or URI where backup files are stored.']
  storage_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the storage provider or vendor hosting the backup data (e.g., AWS S3, Azure Blob, local NAS).']
  technical_owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the technical owner for notifications and escalations.']
  technical_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the technical owner or system administrator responsible for this backup schedule.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the data backup and disaster recovery schedules for all critical NGO systems and data repositories. Captures system or data asset covered, backup frequency (daily/weekly/monthly), backup type (full/incremental/differential), retention period, storage location (on-site/cloud/offsite), recovery point objective (RPO), recovery time objective (RTO), and last successful backup timestamp. Supports business continuity planning.'
}

Table technology.it_procurement {
  it_procurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IT procurement request or purchase order record.']
  approver_staff_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the staff member who approved the IT procurement request, typically a budget holder or IT manager.']
  award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grant.award. Business justification: IT procurement funded by grants must track award source. Business need: grant budget compliance, allowable cost verification (donor procurement rules), audit trail, cost allocation, donor reporting on']
  parent_it_procurement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on it_procurement (parent_it_procurement_id)']
  primary_it_staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the staff member who initiated the IT procurement request.']
  resulting_it_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.it_asset. Business justification: IT procurement of hardware results in IT asset records. Tracks procurement-to-asset lifecycle. N:1 relationship (procurement creates asset). Temporal note: FK populated AFTER delivery and asset regist']
  resulting_software_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to technology.software_license. Business justification: IT procurement of software/SaaS results in software license records. Tracks procurement-to-license lifecycle. N:1 relationship (procurement creates license). Temporal note: FK populated after license ']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred after procurement completion, based on final invoice amounts.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the IT goods or services were received and confirmed by the requesting party.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the IT procurement request was formally approved by the authorized approver.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the staff member who approved the IT procurement request.']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'Budget line or cost center code against which this IT procurement is charged, aligned with the organization\'s Chart of Accounts (CoA).']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the business need and rationale for this IT procurement, including expected benefits and alignment with organizational strategy.']
  compliance_check_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this IT procurement requires additional compliance review (e.g., sanctions screening, data protection impact assessment, export control review).']
  compliance_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of compliance review: not_required (no review needed), pending (under review), approved (cleared), rejected (compliance issue identified).']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the master contract or framework agreement under which this IT procurement is made, if applicable.']
  country_office_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code identifying the country office or field location requesting the IT procurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this IT procurement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the procurement transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  data_classification_level varchar [note: 'Highest data classification level that the procured IT system or service will handle, determining security and compliance requirements.']
  delivery_confirmed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the staff member who confirmed receipt and acceptance of the IT goods or services.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost of the IT procurement request, including all line items, taxes, and fees.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Anticipated date by which the IT goods or services are expected to be delivered or made available.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Vendor invoice number associated with this IT procurement, used for payment processing and financial reconciliation.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the IT goods or services being procured, including specifications, quantities, and business justification.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this IT procurement record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or special instructions related to this IT procurement request.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date on which the purchase order was issued to the vendor.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which payment was made to the vendor for this IT procurement.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of payment to the vendor: pending (awaiting payment), paid (fully settled), partially_paid (partial payment made), overdue (payment past due date), cancelled (payment voided).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority of the procurement request: critical (emergency/operational impact), high (urgent business need), medium (planned requirement), low (nice-to-have).']
  procurement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the IT procurement request: draft (being prepared), submitted (awaiting review), pending_approval (in approval workflow), approved (authorized for purchase), rejected (denied), ordered (purchase order issued to vendor), received (goods/services delivered), cancelled (request withdrawn). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|pending_approval|approved|rejected|ordered|received|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Category of IT procurement: hardware (laptops, servers, network equipment), software license (perpetual or subscription), cloud service (SaaS, IaaS, PaaS), managed service (outsourced IT operations), consulting (professional services), maintenance (support contracts), or telecom (connectivity services). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hardware|software_license|cloud_service|managed_service|consulting|maintenance|telecom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Program or project code that this IT procurement supports, enabling program-level cost tracking and reporting.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Unique purchase order number issued to the vendor after approval, serving as the contractual commitment to purchase.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when an IT procurement request is rejected, documenting the rationale for denial.']
  requester_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the staff member who initiated the IT procurement request, used for communication and notifications.']
  requester_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the staff member who initiated the IT procurement request.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for the IT procurement requisition, typically generated by the ERP or procurement system.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the IT procurement request was formally submitted for approval, marking the start of the approval workflow.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor in the organization\'s vendor master data system.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the supplier or service provider from whom the IT goods or services are being procured.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of IT-specific procurement requests and purchase orders for hardware, software, and technology services. Captures requisition type (hardware, software license, cloud service, managed service), requested item, vendor, estimated cost, budget code, approval chain, procurement status, and delivery confirmation. Complements supply.purchase_order (which covers humanitarian commodity procurement) — this is scoped exclusively to IT goods and services.'
}

Table technology.cab_meeting {
  cab_meeting_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cab_meeting']
  followup_cab_meeting_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on cab_meeting (followup_cab_meeting_id)']
  staff_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who chairs or facilitates the CAB meeting, responsible for agenda management and decision facilitation.']
  action_items_count varchar [note: 'Number of follow-up action items or tasks assigned during the CAB meeting that require completion before the next meeting.']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the CAB meeting concluded, capturing the true duration of the meeting.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the CAB meeting commenced, which may differ from scheduled time due to delays or early starts.']
  agenda_document_url varchar [note: 'Link or file path to the meeting agenda document outlining topics, change requests, and discussion items for the CAB meeting.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the CAB meeting was cancelled or postponed, if applicable (e.g., lack of quorum, emergency situation, no changes to review).']
  changes_approved varchar [note: 'Count of change requests that received approval from the CAB during the meeting.']
  changes_deferred varchar [note: 'Count of change requests that were postponed or tabled for future CAB review, requiring additional information or analysis.']
  changes_rejected varchar [note: 'Count of change requests that were rejected or denied by the CAB during the meeting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the CAB meeting record was first created in the system.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter during which the CAB meeting occurred, enabling quarterly performance analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year during which the CAB meeting occurred, used for reporting and trend analysis (e.g., FY2024).']
  is_emergency_meeting boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this CAB meeting was convened on an emergency basis to address critical or urgent change requests outside the regular schedule.']
  meeting_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the CAB meeting is held (e.g., conference room name, Zoom link, Microsoft Teams channel).']
  meeting_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing key discussion points, decisions, concerns raised, and general observations from the CAB meeting.']
  meeting_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the CAB meeting, typically following organizational numbering convention (e.g., CAB-2024-001).']
  meeting_platform varchar [note: 'The technology platform or medium used to conduct the CAB meeting (in-person, video conferencing tool, or hybrid).']
  meeting_series_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this CAB meeting to a recurring series or sequence of related meetings (e.g., weekly CAB, monthly emergency CAB).']
  meeting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the CAB meeting indicating whether it is planned, active, concluded, or cancelled.']
  meeting_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the CAB meeting based on urgency and purpose (standard for routine changes, emergency for critical incidents, expedited for time-sensitive approvals).']
  minutes_document_url varchar [note: 'Link or file path to the official meeting minutes documenting discussions, decisions, and action items from the CAB meeting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the CAB meeting record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  next_meeting_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the subsequent CAB meeting, typically determined during the current meeting.']
  quorum_met boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the minimum required number of voting members was present to conduct official business.']
  quorum_required varchar [note: 'Minimum number of voting members required to be present for the CAB meeting to make binding decisions.']
  recording_url varchar [note: 'Link to the audio or video recording of the CAB meeting for reference and compliance purposes.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the CAB meeting is planned to occur.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the CAB meeting is scheduled to conclude, including timezone information.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the CAB meeting is scheduled to begin, including timezone information.']
  total_attendees varchar [note: 'Total count of individuals who attended the CAB meeting, including voting members, observers, and presenters.']
  total_changes_reviewed varchar [note: 'Count of change requests that were presented and reviewed during the CAB meeting.']
  voting_members_present varchar [note: 'Count of authorized voting members who attended the CAB meeting and are eligible to approve or reject change requests.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for cab_meeting. Referenced by cab_meeting_id.'
}

Table technology.it_problem {
  it_problem_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for it_problem']
  related_it_problem_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on it_problem (related_it_problem_id)']
  actual_resolution_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual number of hours spent investigating and resolving the problem.']
  affected_configuration_item varchar [note: 'Configuration item or asset identified as the source or affected component of the problem.']
  affected_service varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the IT service affected by this problem.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of management approval for problem resolution actions or change requests.']
  assigned_group varchar [note: 'IT support group or team responsible for managing this problem.']
  assigned_to varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or team currently assigned to investigate and resolve the problem.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Business rationale and justification for prioritizing and resolving this problem.']
  change_request_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the change request created to implement the problem resolution.']
  closed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who closed the problem record.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the problem record was formally closed after verification and documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this problem record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the IT problem, including symptoms, impact, and context.']
  detected_date date [note: 'Date when the underlying problem was first detected in the IT environment.']
  environment varchar [note: 'IT environment where the problem was identified or is occurring.']
  estimated_resolution_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated number of hours required to fully resolve the problem.']
  geographic_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location or region where the problem is affecting operations.']
  impact varchar [note: 'Scope of organizational impact caused by the problem.']
  incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of incidents that have been linked to or caused by this problem.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when formal investigation of the problem began.']
  known_error boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this problem has been classified as a known error with documented root cause and workaround.']
  known_error_date date [note: 'Date when the problem was officially classified as a known error.']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Key lessons learned and knowledge captured from investigating and resolving this problem.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this problem record was last modified or updated.']
  preventive_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of preventive measures implemented to avoid recurrence of this problem.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the problem based on impact and urgency.']
  problem_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the IT problem type.']
  problem_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique reference number for the IT problem, typically following organizational numbering convention (e.g., PRB0001234).']
